Kwa mfano, malengo mengi ya afya na maendeleo yanaweza kufikiwa kwa wakati mmoja kwa kukabiliana na uchafuzi wa hewa, ambao unawajibika kwa vifo vya mapema milioni 7 kila mwaka. Uzalishaji chafuzi unajumuisha vichafuzi vikali vya hali ya hewa vya muda mfupi (SLCPs)—hasa kaboni nyeusi, ozoni na methane—ambazo ni hatari sana kwa afya lakini zina maisha mafupi tu ya angahewa.
translations.hypothesis: Kwa mfano, utendaji wa huduma za afya unaozingatia mazingira unaweza kuleta faida za kiafya kwa wagonjwa na wafanyakazi, huku pia ukiongoza kwa uhifadhi wa nishati, kupunguza hatari za mabadiliko ya tabianchi, na kuleta akiba ya gharama za muda mrefu. Shirika la Afya Duniani (WHO) limejitolea kuendeleza maendeleo endelevu katika kazi zake zote, ili kusaidia kulinda watu wa kizazi kijacho dhidi ya hatari za kiafya zinazoongezeka za sasa. Athari: Mbinu nyingi za maendeleo endelevu zinaweza kuleta faida kubwa kwa afya, mazingira, na hali ya hewa, na faida hizi zinaweza kuonekana karibu mara moja. Kwa mfano, malengo mengi ya afya na maendeleo yanaweza kufikiwa kwa wakati mmoja kwa kushughulikia uchafuzi wa hewa, ambao unachukuliwa kuwa husababisha takriban vifo milioni 7 mapema kila mwaka. Kemikali zinazochafua hewa ni pamoja na gesi hatari za muda mfupi (SLCPs)—haswa kaboni nyeusi, ozone, na methane—ambazo ni hatari sana kwa afya, lakini zina muda mfupi tu katika angahewa.

translations.source: Transport policies and investments that favour clean public transport, along with walking and cycling, can have a dramatic impact on air quality. This can reduce multiple health risks at the same time, including stroke, heart attack, lung disease and some cancers. They can also reduce the estimated 1.25 million deaths annually from traffic injury, while also improving access to health services, particularly in densely populated areas. Sustainable development housing policies can further reduce emissions through considerations such as building siting and land use, choices of construction materials, design features and ventilation and energy. WHO response: WHO’s work is guided by the Sustainable Development Goals, particularly Goal 3: “Ensure healthy lives and promote well-being for all at all ages.”
translations.reference: Sera za usafiri na uwekezaji unaopendelea usafiri safi wa umma, pamoja na kutembea na kuendesha baiskeli, zinaweza kuwa na athari kubwa katika ubora wa hewa. Hii inaweza kupunguza hatari nyingi za kiafya kwa wakati mmoja, pamoja na kiharusi, mshtuko wa moyo, ugonjwa wa mapafu na saratani kadhaa. Wanaweza pia kupunguza makadirio ya vifo milioni 1.25 kila mwaka kutokana na majeraha ya barabarani, huku pia wakiboresha upatikanaji wa huduma za afya, hasa katika maeneo yenye watu wengi. Sera za maendeleo endelevu za makazi zinaweza kupunguza zaidi uzalishaji wa hewa chafu kupitia mambo ya kuzingatia kama vile eneo la majengo na matumizi ya ardhi, uchaguzi wa vifaa vya ujenzi, vipengele vya muundo na uingizaji hewa na nishati. Jibu la WHO: Kazi ya WHO inaongozwa na Malengo ya Maendeleo Endelevu, hasa Lengo la 3: "Hakikisha maisha yenye afya na kukuza ustawi kwa wote katika umri wote."
translations.hypothesis: Sera na uwekezaji katika usafiri unaozingatia usafiri wa umma unaofaa mazingira, pamoja na kutembea na kutumia baiskeli, zinaweza kuleta mabadiliko makubwa katika ubora wa hewa. Hii inaweza kupunguza hatari nyingi za kiafya kwa wakati mmoja, ikiwa ni pamoja na mapigo ya moyo, msongo wa moyo, magonjwa ya mapafu na aina fulani za saratani. Pia, inaweza kupunguza idadi ya vifo inayokadiriwa kuwa milioni 1.25 kila mwaka kutokana na ajali za barabarani, huku pia ikiboresha upatikanaji wa huduma za afya, hasa katika maeneo yenye idadi kubwa ya watu. Sera za makazi endelevu zinaweza kupunguza zaidi utoaji wa gesi hatari kupitia mambo kama vile mahali ambapo majengo yajengwa, matumizi ya ardhi, aina za nyenzo za ujenzi, muundo wa majengo, uingizaji hewa na matumizi ya nishati. Jibu la WHO: Kazi ya WHO inafuata Malengo ya Maendeleo Endelevu, hasa Lengo la 3: "Kuhakikisha maisha yenye afya na kukuza ustawi kwa wote katika kila umri."

translations.source: The Goals inform projects and policies across the Organization, including guidelines on housing, urban development, transportation systems and health care delivery. For example, the WHO Housing and health guidelines, first published in 2018, highlight the increasing impact of housing conditions on human health in light of urban growth, climate and demographic changes. The guidelines provide Member States, partners and the public with evidence-based recommendations relevant to inadequate living space (crowding), low and high indoor temperatures, injury hazards in the home, and accessibility of housing for people with functional impairments. The Urban Health Initiative (UHI) also focuses on ways for cities to enable good health by catalysing effective action on urban air pollution and short-lived climate pollutants (SLCPs)—saving lives by linking health, environment and sustainable development. WHO also collaborates with the Climate and Clean Air Coalition (CCAC) on the Breathe Life campaign, which aims to cut air pollution and reduce the 7 million premature deaths annually that it causes.
translations.reference: Malengo yanaarifu miradi na sera kote katika Shirika, ikijumuisha miongozo ya makazi, maendeleo ya mijini, mifumo ya usafirishaji na utoaji wa huduma za afya. Kwa mfano, miongozo ya Makazi na afya ya WHO, iliyochapishwa kwa mara ya kwanza mwaka wa 2018, inaangazia ongezeko la athari za hali ya makazi kwa afya ya binadamu kwa kuzingatia ukuaji wa miji, hali ya hewa na mabadiliko ya idadi ya watu. Mwongozo huo unazipa Nchi Wanachama, washirika na umma mapendekezo yenye msingi wa ushahidi yanayohusiana na uhaba wa nafasi ya kuishi (msongamano), halijoto ya chini na ya juu ndani ya nyumba, hatari za majeraha nyumbani, na upatikanaji wa nyumba kwa watu walio na matatizo ya utendaji. Mpango wa Afya Mijini (UHI) pia unaangazia njia za miji kuwezesha afya njema kwa kuchochea hatua madhubuti dhidi ya uchafuzi wa hewa mijini na vichafuzi vya hali ya hewa ya muda mfupi (SLCPs)—kuokoa maisha kwa kuunganisha afya, mazingira na maendeleo endelevu. WHO pia inashirikiana na Muungano wa Hali ya Hewa na Safi (CCAC) kwenye kampeni ya Breathe Life, ambayo inalenga kupunguza uchafuzi wa hewa na kupunguza vifo vya mapema milioni 7 kila mwaka ambavyo husababisha.
translations.hypothesis: Malengo hayo huongoza miradi na sera katika shirika lote, ikiwa ni pamoja na miongozo kuhusu makazi, maendeleo ya mijini, mifumo ya usafiri, na utoaji wa huduma za afya. Kwa mfano, miongozo ya WHO kuhusu makazi na afya, iliyochapishwa kwa mara ya kwanza mwaka wa 2018, inaangazia athari inayoongezeka ya hali ya makazi kwa afya ya binadamu, kutokana na ukuaji wa mijini, mabadiliko ya tabianchi, na mabadiliko ya idadi ya watu. Miongozo hiyo hutoa mapendekezo yanayostahili kwa nchi wanachama, washirika, na umma, kuhusu masuala kama vile upungufu wa nafasi ya kuishi (mkusanyiko mkubwa), halijoto ndogo na kubwa ndani ya nyumba, hatari za majeraha nyumbani, na ufikivu wa makazi kwa watu wenye ulemavu. Mpango wa Afya ya Mijini (UHI) pia unaangazia njia ambazo miji inaweza kuchangia afya bora kwa kuchukua hatua bora kuhusu uchafuzi wa hewa katika miji na gesi za hali ya hewa zinazochomwa haraka (SLCPs) – na hivyo kuokoa maisha kwa kuunganisha afya, mazingira, na maendeleo endelevu. WHO pia inashirikiana na Muungano wa Hewa Safi na Tabianchi (CCAC) katika kampeni ya "Breathe Life," ambayo inalenga kupunguza uchafuzi wa hewa na kupunguza vifo milioni 7 ambavyo hutokea kila mwaka kutokana na uchafuzi huo.

translations.source: Syphilis Overview: Syphilis is a bacterial sexually transmitted infection (STI) caused by Treponema pallidum which results in substantial morbidity and mortality, and it is curable. Syphilis is transmitted through sexual contact with the infectious lesions, via blood transfusion or from a pregnant woman to her fetus. Mother-to-child transmission of syphilis is usually devastating to the fetus in cases that were not detected or treated sufficiently early in pregnancy. Maternal syphilis, when untreated, treated late or not treated with penicillin, results in adverse birth outcomes (ABOs) estimated in 50−80% of cases, depending on the stage of syphilis.
translations.reference: Kaswende Muhtasari: Kaswende ni ugonjwa wa zinaa unaosababishwa na bakteria (STI) unaosababishwa na Treponema pallidum ambayo husababisha magonjwa na vifo vingi, na inatibika. Kaswende huambukizwa kwa njia ya kujamiiana na vidonda vya kuambukiza, kwa kuongezewa damu au kutoka kwa mwanamke mjamzito hadi kwa kijusi chake. Uambukizaji wa kaswende kutoka kwa mama kwenda kwa mtoto huwa mbaya kwa kijusi katika hali ambazo hazikugunduliwa au kutibiwa vya kutosha mapema katika ujauzito. Kaswende ya kina mama, isipotibiwa, kutibiwa kuchelewa au kutotibiwa na penicillin, husababisha matokeo mabaya ya uzazi (ABOs) yanayokadiriwa katika 50-80% ya kesi, kutegemeana na hatua ya kaswende.
translations.hypothesis: Mwangaza Kuhusu Ugonjwa wa Syphilis: Syphilis ni ugonjwa wa bakteria unaotarajiwa kupitia mahusiano ya ngono (STI) unaosababishwa na bakteria ya *Treponema pallidum*. Ugonjwa huu husababisha matatizo makubwa na vifo, lakini unaweza kupona. Syphilis huenezwa kupitia mawasiliano ya ngono na maeneo yaliyoathirika, kupitia uhamisho wa damu, au kutoka kwa mwanamke mjamzito kwenda kwa mtoto wake. Uambukizi wa syphilis kutoka kwa mama kwenda kwa mtoto mara nyingi huwa na madhara makubwa kwa mtoto, hasa katika kesi ambazo ugonjwa ha Kugundulika au kutibiwa mapema vya kutosha wakati wa ujauzito. Ugonjwa wa syphilis kwa mama, wakati hautibiwi, hutibiwa kwa kuchelewa, au hutibiwa kwa dawa nyingine isiyo ya penicillin, husababisha matokeo mabaya ya kuzaliwa (ABO) katika takriban asilimia 50-80 ya kesi, kulingana na hatua ya ugonjwa.

translations.source: These ABOs are often severe and include stillbirth (most commonly), neonatal death, prematurity, low birth weight, and congenitally infected infants. Syphilis can be prevented through safer sex practices including the correct and consistent use of condoms. Many people with syphilis do not have any symptoms or have only minor symptoms that go unnoticed. Identifying infection through laboratory tests and treatment of positive cases will prevent further transmission and adverse pregnancy outcomes including congenital syphilis. Screening can be done using rapid tests that can provide results in less than 20 minutes, allowing for immediate treatment.
translations.reference: ABO hizi mara nyingi huwa kali na hujumuisha kuzaliwa mfu (mara nyingi), kifo cha watoto wachanga, kuzaliwa kabla ya wakati, uzito wa chini, na watoto wachanga waliozaliwa kuzaliwa. Kaswende inaweza kuzuiwa kwa njia salama za ngono ikijumuisha matumizi sahihi na ya mara kwa mara ya kondomu. Watu wengi wenye kaswende hawana dalili zozote au wana dalili ndogo tu ambazo hazitambuliki. Kutambua maambukizi kupitia vipimo vya maabara na matibabu ya kesi chanya kutazuia maambukizi zaidi na matokeo mabaya ya ujauzito ikiwa ni pamoja na kaswende ya kuzaliwa. Uchunguzi unaweza kufanywa kwa kutumia vipimo vya haraka ambavyo vinaweza kutoa matokeo kwa chini ya dakika 20, kuruhusu matibabu ya haraka.
translations.hypothesis: Magonjwa haya, yanayohusiana na kikundi cha ABO, mara nyingi huwa hatari na yanaweza kusababisha vifo vya watoto kabla ya kuzaliwa (ambavyo ni jambo la kawaida), vifo vya watoto wachanga, kuzaliwa mapema, uzito hafifu wa mtoto aliyezaliwa, na watoto walioambukizwa tangu kuzaliwa. Ugonjwa wa syphilis unaweza kuzuiliwa kupitia tabia salama za kingono, ikiwa ni pamoja na matumizi sahihi na ya kawaida ya kondomu. Watu wengi walio na syphilis hawana dalili zozote au huwa na dalili ndogo ambazo hazizingatiwi. Kuamua kama mtu amekuwa na maambukizi kupitia vipimo vya maabara na kutibu kesi ambazo zimebainika kuwa na maambukizi, itasaidia kuzuia kuenea zaidi kwa ugonjwa huo na matokeo mabaya ya ujauzito, ikiwa ni pamoja na syphilis iliyoambukiza mtoto tangu kuzaliwa. Uchunguzi unaweza kufanywa kwa kutumia vipimo vya haraka ambavyo vinaweza kutoa matokeo katika muda mfupi zaidi ya dakika 20, na hivyo kuruhusu matibabu ya haraka.

translations.source: When untreated, syphilis lasts many years and goes through different stages. Treatment for syphilis is done with injectable penicillin benzathine, with 1 to 3 doses depending on the stage of the disease. Only penicillin can prevent congenital syphilis. Treatment with penicillin has been hampered by periodic and prolonged shortages of benzathine penicillin in numerous countries, including high-, low- and middle-income countries. Symptoms: Many people with syphilis do not present symptoms or do not perceive them.
translations.reference: Isipotibiwa, kaswende hudumu miaka mingi na hupitia hatua tofauti. Matibabu ya kaswende hufanywa na penicillin ya sindano ya benzathine, na dozi 1 hadi 3 kulingana na hatua ya ugonjwa. Penicillin pekee inaweza kuzuia kaswende ya kuzaliwa. Matibabu na penicillin yametatizwa na upungufu wa mara kwa mara na wa muda mrefu wa penicillin ya benzathine katika nchi nyingi, zikiwemo nchi zenye kipato cha juu, cha chini na cha kati. Dalili: Watu wengi wenye kaswende hawaonyeshi dalili au hawazioni.
translations.hypothesis: Ugonjwa wa syphilis, ikiwa hautibiwi, unaweza kudumu kwa miaka mingi na husonga kupitia hatua tofauti. Matibabu ya syphilis hufanywa kwa kutumia sindano za penicillin benzathine, ambapo hupewa dozi 1 hadi 3 kulingana na hatua ya ugonjwa. Penicillin pekee ndiyo inaweza kuzuia ugonjwa wa syphilis ambao huambukizwa kutoka kwa mama kwa mtoto. Matibabu kwa kutumia penicillin yamekuwa yakikabiliwa na upungufu wa mara kwa mara na wa muda mrefu wa penicillin benzathine katika nchi nyingi, ikiwa ni pamoja na nchi zilizo na mapato ya juu, ya chini, na ya kati. Dalili: Watu wengi walio na syphilis hawadhihirishi dalili zozote, au hawizisikii.

translations.source: Untreated, syphilis lasts many years and is characterized into different stages. Sexual transmission typically occurs during early stages, i.e., up to 2 years after infection. During the initial phase (primary syphilis), which lasts on average 21 days, a solitary, painless, usually hard and round sore (chancre) appears at the site of inoculation (contact), often in the vagina, penis or anus (but it may also be extra-genital) and may go unnoticed. The chancre heals with or without treatment within 3 to 10 weeks. If untreated, the disease progresses to the secondary stage.
translations.reference: Kaswende ambayo haijatibiwa, hudumu miaka mingi na ina sifa katika hatua tofauti. Maambukizi ya ngono kwa kawaida hutokea katika hatua za mwanzo, yaani, hadi miaka 2 baada ya kuambukizwa. Wakati wa awamu ya awali (kaswende ya msingi), ambayo hudumu kwa wastani wa siku 21, kidonda cha upweke, kisicho na uchungu, kawaida kigumu na cha pande zote (chancre) huonekana kwenye sehemu ya chanjo (mawasiliano), mara nyingi kwenye uke, uume au mkundu (lakini pia inaweza kuwa nje ya sehemu ya siri) na inaweza kwenda bila kutambuliwa. Chancre huponya kwa au bila matibabu ndani ya wiki 3 hadi 10. Ikiwa haijatibiwa, ugonjwa unaendelea hadi hatua ya sekondari.
translations.hypothesis: Ugonjwa wa syphilis, ukipigwa maradhi, unaweza kudumu kwa miaka mingi na una hatua tofauti. Mara nyingi, maambukizi husababishwa na uhusiano wa ngono, hasa katika hatua za mwanzo, yaani, hadi miaka miwili baada ya kuambukizwa. Katika hatua ya awali (syphilis ya hatua ya kwanza), ambayo hudumu kwa wastani siku 21, makovu moja, ambayo hayavutii, kwa kawaida yana ngumu na yana umbo la mviringo (chancre) huonekana katika eneo ambapo maambukizi yamefanyika (ambapo kulikuwa na mgusano), mara nyingi kwenye sehemu ya uzazi ya mwanamke, sehemu ya kiume au kwenye njia ya haja, lakini pia inaweza kuonekana katika sehemu nyingine za mwili na huenda haionekani. Krovu hilo hupona ndani ya wiki 3 hadi 10, na au bila matibabu. Ikiwa haitibiwi, ugonjwa huo unaendelea hadi hatua ya pili.

translations.source: Secondary syphilis is characterized by a rash that can vary widely and mimic other infectious or non-infectious conditions, but characteristically affects the palms and soles. The rash is non-itchy and can be minimal enough to be overlooked. In warm and moist areas of the body, such as the anus and labia, large white or grey raised lesions may develop at the location of the primary chancre (condyloma lata). The symptoms and signs of secondary syphilis spontaneously resolve, even without treatment. Without treatment, the latent stage starts.
translations.reference: Pili kaswende ina sifa ya upele ambao unaweza kutofautiana sana na kuiga hali zingine za kuambukiza au zisizo za kuambukiza, lakini kitabia huathiri viganja na nyayo. Upele hauwashi na unaweza kuwa mdogo vya kutosha kupuuzwa. Katika sehemu zenye joto na unyevunyevu za mwili, kama vile njia ya haja kubwa na midomo, vidonda vikubwa vyeupe au kijivu vilivyoinuliwa vinaweza kutokea katika eneo la chancre ya msingi (condyloma lata). Dalili na ishara za kaswende ya pili hutatuliwa yenyewe, hata bila matibabu. Bila matibabu, hatua ya latent huanza.
translations.hypothesis: Ugonjwa wa syphilis wa hatua ya pili unaonyeshwa na upele ambao unaweza kutofautiana sana na kuiga magonjwa mengine, yaambukizi au yasiyo yaambukizi, lakini kwa kawaida huathiri mikono na vidole vya miguu. Upele huo hauvunji na unaweza kuwa mdogo sana kiasi kwamba unaweza kupuuzwa. Katika maeneo yenye joto na yenye unyevu ya mwili, kama vile eneo la anus na sehemu za uzazi, vidonda vikubwa vya nyeupe au kijivu ambavyo vimeinuliwa vinaweza kuonekana katika eneo ambapo vidonda vya awali vya syphilis (condyloma lata) vilikuwa. Dalili na ishara za ugonjwa wa syphilis wa hatua ya pili huondoka peke yake, hata bila matibabu. Bila matibabu, hatua ya kimya (latent) huanza.

translations.source: Latent syphilis presents no clinical symptoms or signs and the treatment is different from the early phase. After years or decades without treatment, neurological and cardiovascular diseases among others may occur. Treatment: Syphilis diagnosis is based on the person’s history, physical examination, laboratory testing and sometimes radiology, as symptoms are not common or noticeable. Identifying asymptomatic infection through laboratory tests and treatment of positive cases will prevent further transmission and adverse pregnancy outcomes, including congenital syphilis. Laboratory tests for syphilis include direct detection of T. pallidum or indirect methods such as serology.
translations.reference: Kaswende iliyojificha haitoi dalili zozote za kimatibabu au ishara na matibabu ni tofauti na awamu ya awali. Baada ya miaka au miongo bila matibabu, magonjwa ya neva na ya moyo na mishipa kati ya wengine yanaweza kutokea. Matibabu: Utambuzi wa kaswende unatokana na historia ya mtu, uchunguzi wa kimwili, upimaji wa kimaabara na wakati mwingine radiolojia, kwani dalili si za kawaida au hazionekani. Kutambua maambukizi ya dalili kupitia vipimo vya maabara na matibabu ya kesi chanya kutazuia maambukizi zaidi na matokeo mabaya ya ujauzito, ikiwa ni pamoja na kaswende ya kuzaliwa. Vipimo vya kimaabara vya kaswende ni pamoja na kugundua moja kwa moja T. pallidum au mbinu zisizo za moja kwa moja kama vile seroloji.
translations.hypothesis: Ugonjwa wa syphilis ambao haonyeshi dalili au ishara zozote (syphilis ya aina ya siri) hutendewa tofauti na awamu za mapema. Baada ya miaka au miongo bila matibabu, magonjwa ya neva na ya moyo, pamoja na magonjwa mengine, yanaweza kutokea. Matibabu: Utambuzi wa syphilis hutegemea historia ya mgonjwa, uchunguzi wa kimwili, vipimo vya maabara, na wakati mwingine uchunguzi wa picha, kwani dalili hazipo kwa kawaida au hazionekani. Kubaini maambukizi bila dalili kupitia vipimo vya maabara na kutibu wagonjwa walioambukizwa kutazuia kuenea zaidi kwa ugonjwa na matokeo mabaya ya ujauzito, ikiwa ni pamoja na syphilis inayopatikana kwa mtoto tumbuni. Vipimo vya maabara vya syphilis vinajumuisha utambuzi wa moja kwa moja wa bakteria *T. pallidum* au mbinu zisizo za moja kwa moja, kama vile uchunguzi wa kemikali kwenye damu.

translations.source: Rapid screening and diagnostic tests are also available and can provide results in less than 20 minutes, facilitating immediate treatment initiation. WHO recommends syphilis to be treated with injectable penicillin (benzathine penicillin G) with 1 or 3 doses, depending on the stage of the disease. WHO has detailed treatment guidelines for syphilis, and one specifically for the screening and treatment of syphilis in pregnancy. Congenital syphilis There are currently no diagnostic tests for congenital syphilis.
translations.reference: Uchunguzi wa haraka na vipimo vya uchunguzi pia vinapatikana na vinaweza kutoa matokeo kwa chini ya dakika 20, kuwezesha kuanza kwa matibabu mara moja. WHO inapendekeza kaswende kutibiwa kwa penicillin ya sindano (benzathine penicillin G) kwa dozi 1 au 3, kulingana na hatua ya ugonjwa. WHO ina miongozo ya kina ya matibabu ya kaswende, na moja mahsusi kwa uchunguzi na matibabu ya kaswende katika ujauzito. Kaswende ya kuzaliwa Kwa sasa hakuna vipimo vya uchunguzi wa kaswende ya kuzaliwa.
translations.hypothesis: Mitihani ya uchunguzi wa haraka na mitihani ya utambuzi pia inapatikana, na inaweza kutoa matokeo katika chini ya dakika 20, na hivyo kurahisisha kuanza matibabu mara moja. Shirika la Afya Duniani (WHO) inapendekeza kwamba ugonjwa wa syphilis utibiwe kwa kutumia penicillin inayotolewa kupitia sindano (benzathine penicillin G), kwa dozi 1 au 3, kulingana na hatua ya ugonjwa. WHO ina maelekezo ya kina ya matibabu kwa ugonjwa wa syphilis, na maelekezo maalum kwa uchunguzi na matibabu ya syphilis kwa wanawake wajawazito. Syphilis iliyopatikana mapacha: Hivi sasa, hakuna mitihani ya utambuzi kwa syphilis iliyopatikana mapacha.

translations.source: All live or stillborn infants of women with syphilis should be examined for evidence of congenital syphilis. Live-born infants should be examined and tested at birth to define treatment requirement. When indicated, also at monthly intervals for 3 months until the infant’s serology are, and remain, negative, as maternal antibodies can be passively transmitted to the baby during pregnancy.
translations.reference: Watoto wote walio hai au waliozaliwa wakiwa wamekufa katika wanawake walio na kaswende wanapaswa kuchunguzwa kwa ushahidi wa kaswende ya kuzaliwa. Watoto waliozaliwa hai wanapaswa kuchunguzwa na kupimwa wakati wa kuzaliwa ili kufafanua mahitaji ya matibabu. Inapoonyeshwa, pia kwa vipindi vya kila mwezi kwa miezi 3 hadi serolojia ya mtoto mchanga iwe, na kubaki, hasi, kwani kingamwili za mama zinaweza kupitishwa kwa mtoto wakati wa ujauzito.
translations.hypothesis: Watoto wote waliozaliwa hai au waliozaliwa wakiwa wamekufa, ambao wamemzalia wanawake walio na ugonjwa wa syphilis, wanapaswa kufanyiwa uchunguzi ili kubaini kama wana dalili za ugonjwa wa syphilis uliopatikana tangu kuzaliwa. Watoto waliozaliwa hai wanapaswa kufanyiwa uchunguzi na vipimo mara moja baada ya kuzaliwa ili kubaini matibabu yanayohitajika. Pia, ikiwa ni lazima, uchunguzi na vipimo vinapaswa kufanyika kila mwezi kwa miezi mitatu, hadi matokeo ya vipimo ya mtoto yanaonyesha kuwa hakuna ugonjwa, na yanabaki kuwa hasi. Hii ni kwa sababu kinga za mama zinaweza kuhamishiwa kwa mtoto wakati wa ujauzito.

translations.source: Taeniasis and cysticercosis Overview: Infection with the tapeworm Taenia solium can result in two distinct conditions: taeniasis and cysticercosis. Taeniasis is the intestinal infection with the tapeworm and it occurs when a person eats raw or undercooked, infected pork. Taeniasis has no major impact on human health. However, tapeworm eggs pass in the faeces of the tapeworm carrier and in the absence of a proper sanitation may contaminate the environment.
translations.reference: Taeniasis na cysticercosis Muhtasari: Kuambukizwa na minyoo Taenia solium kunaweza kusababisha hali mbili tofauti: taeniasis na cysticercosis. Taeniasis ni maambukizi ya matumbo na minyoo na hutokea wakati mtu anakula nyama ya nguruwe mbichi au ambayo haijaiva vizuri. Taeniasis haina athari kubwa kwa afya ya binadamu. Hata hivyo, mayai ya minyoo hupita kwenye kinyesi cha mtoaji wa minyoo na kukosekana kwa usafi wa mazingira kunaweza kuchafua mazingira.
translations.hypothesis: Taeniasis na cysticercosis: Maambukizi ya minyoo aina ya Taenia solium yanaweza kusababisha hali mbili tofauti: taeniasis na cysticercosis. Taeniasis ni maambukizi ya matumbo yanayosababishwa na minyoo hiyo, na hutokea wakati mtu anakula nyama ya nguruwe ambayo haijapikwa vizuri au ambayo imekuwa ya ajabu. Taeniasis haina athari kubwa kwa afya ya binadamu. Hata hivyo, mayai ya minyoo hayo huhamishwa kupitia kinyesi cha mtu aliyeambukizwa, na ikiwa usafi wa mazingira hauhudumiwi vizuri, mazingira yanaweza kuambukizwa.

translations.source: While tapeworm eggs are infective for pigs, T. solium eggs may also infect humans if they are ingested by a person (via the fecal-oral route, or by ingesting contaminated food or water). Ingestion of tapeworm eggs causes infection with the larval parasite in the tissues such as muscles, skin, eyes and the central nervous system (human cysticercosis), with possible devastating effects on health. When cysts develop in the brain, the condition is referred to as neurocysticercosis. Symptoms: Taeniasis is usually characterized by mild and non-specific symptoms associated to an intestinal infection (abdominal pain, nausea, diarrhoea, etc.). These symptoms may continue until the tapeworm dies following treatment, otherwise it may live for years.
translations.reference: Ingawa mayai ya minyoo yanaambukiza nguruwe, mayai ya T. solium pia yanaweza kumwambukiza binadamu ikiwa yamemezwa na mtu (kupitia njia ya kinyesi-kwa mdomo, au kwa kumeza chakula au maji yaliyochafuliwa). Umezaji wa mayai ya minyoo husababisha kuambukizwa na vimelea vya buu kwenye tishu kama vile misuli, ngozi, macho na mfumo mkuu wa neva (human cysticercosis), pamoja na athari mbaya kwa afya. Wakati uvimbe hutokea kwenye ubongo, hali hiyo inaitwa neurocysticercosis. Dalili: Taeniasis kawaida huwa na dalili za upole na zisizo maalum zinazohusiana na maambukizi ya matumbo (maumivu ya tumbo, kichefuchefu, kuhara, nk). Dalili hizi zinaweza kuendelea hadi mdudu afe baada ya matibabu, vinginevyo anaweza kuishi kwa miaka.
translations.hypothesis: Ingawa mayai ya minyoo wa utumbo yanaweza kuambukiza nguruwe, mayai ya *T. solium* pia yanaweza kuambukiza binadamu ikiwa mtu atayatumia (kupitia njia ya kuambukiza kutoka kwa kinyesi hadi kinywa, au kwa kula chakula au maji yaliyochafuliwa). Kutumia mayai ya minyoo wa utumbo husababisha maambukizi ya kiumbe mdogo ndani ya tishu kama vile misuli, ngozi, macho na mfumo mkuu wa neva (cystercosis kwa binadamu), ambayo inaweza kuwa na athari mbaya sana kwa afya. Wakati mishipa (cysts) inakua ndani ya ubongo, hali hiyo inajulikana kama neurocysticercosis. Dalili: Taeniasis kwa kawaida huonyeshwa na dalili hafifu na zisizo za kawaida ambazo huhusiana na maambukizi ya matumbo (maumivu ya tumbo, kichefuchefu, kuhara, n.k.). Dalili hizi zinaweza kuendelea hadi minyoo wa utumbo akufa baada ya matibabu, vinginevyo anaweza kuishi kwa miaka mingi.

translations.source: For cysticercosis, the incubation period is variable, and infected people may remain asymptomatic for years. In some endemic regions, infected people may develop visible or palpable nodules (a small solid bump or node which is solid that can be detected by touch) beneath the skin. Neurocysticercosis is associated with a variety of signs and symptoms depending on the number, size, stage and location of the pathological changes as well as the host’s immune response and the parasite’s genotype, but it can also be clinically asymptomatic. Symptoms may include chronic headaches, blindness, seizures (epilepsy if they are recurrent), hydrocephalus, meningitis, dementia and symptoms caused by lesions occupying spaces of the central nervous system. Treatment: The treatment of taeniasis by Taenia solium is important to prevent neurocysticercosis and as a tool to assist in controlling or stopping the parasite transmission cycle.
translations.reference: Kwa cysticercosis, kipindi cha incubation ni tofauti, na watu walioambukizwa wanaweza kubaki bila dalili kwa miaka. Katika baadhi ya maeneo yenye ugonjwa huo, watu walioambukizwa wanaweza kuendeleza vinundu vinavyoonekana au vinavyoweza kubalika (kitunguu kidogo kigumu au nodi ambayo ni dhabiti ambayo inaweza kutambuliwa kwa kuguswa) chini ya ngozi. Neurocysticercosis inahusishwa na aina mbalimbali za ishara na dalili kulingana na idadi, ukubwa, hatua na eneo la mabadiliko ya pathological pamoja na majibu ya kinga ya mwenyeji na genotype ya vimelea, lakini pia inaweza kuwa kliniki bila dalili. Dalili zinaweza kujumuisha maumivu ya kichwa ya muda mrefu, upofu, kifafa (kifafa ikiwa kinajirudia), hydrocephalus, meningitis, shida ya akili na dalili zinazosababishwa na vidonda vinavyochukua nafasi za mfumo mkuu wa neva. Matibabu: Matibabu ya taeniasis na Taenia solium ni muhimu ili kuzuia neurocysticercosis na kama chombo cha kusaidia katika kudhibiti au kusimamisha mzunguko wa maambukizi ya vimelea.
translations.hypothesis: Kwa ugonjwa wa cysticercosis, kipindi cha kutokea kwa dalili kinaweza kutofautiana, na watu walioambukizwa wanaweza kuwa hawajonyonyeshwa na dalili zozote kwa miaka mingi. Katika baadhi ya maeneo ambapo ugonjwa huu ni wa kawaida, watu walioambukizwa wanaweza kuona au kuhisi uvimbe mdogo (eneo dogo thabiti ambalo linaweza kutambuliwa kwa kugusa) chini ya ngozi. Ugonjwa wa neurocysticercosis unaweza kusababishwa na dalili na matokeo mbalimbali, kulingana na idadi, ukubwa, hatua na eneo la mabadiliko ya kiafya, pamoja na jinsi mwili unavyo athiri ugonjwa na aina ya mnyama husika, lakini pia unaweza kuwa bila dalili zozote. Dalili zinaweza kujumuisha maumivu ya kichwa ya muda mrefu, upofu, kifafa (epilepsi ikiwa ni mara kwa mara), ukuaji wa maji katika ubongo, meningitis, upotevu wa kumbukumbu na dalili zinazosababishwa na uharibifu katika sehemu za mfumo mkuu wa neva. Matibabu: Matibabu ya ugonjwa wa taeniasis unaosababishwa na bakteria ya Taenia solium ni muhimu kwa kuzuia ugonjwa wa neurocysticercosis na kama njia ya kusaidia kudhibiti au kusimamisha mzunguko waambukizi wa bakteria huyo.

translations.source: The treatment can be done on an individual bases, or as Mass Drug Administration depending on the local circumstances and the control approaches being implemented. Taeniasis can be treated using anthelmintics (Praziquantel, Niclosamide, Albendazole). In neurocysticercosis, since the destruction of cysts may lead to an inflammatory response, specialised treatment of active disease is required and may include long courses with high doses of anthelmintic, as well as supporting therapy with corticosteroids and/or anti-epileptic drugs, and possibly surgery. The dosage and the duration of treatment can vary greatly and depend mainly on the number, size, location and developmental stage of the cysts, their surrounding inflammatory edema, acuteness and severity of clinical symptoms or signs.
translations.reference: Matibabu yanaweza kufanywa kwa misingi ya mtu binafsi, au kama Utawala wa Misa ya Dawa kulingana na hali ya eneo na mbinu za udhibiti zinazotekelezwa. Taeniasis inaweza kutibiwa kwa kutumia anthelmintics (Praziquantel, Niclosamide, Albendazole). Katika neurocysticercosis, kwa kuwa uharibifu wa cysts unaweza kusababisha majibu ya uchochezi, matibabu maalum ya ugonjwa hai inahitajika na inaweza kujumuisha kozi ndefu na kipimo cha juu cha anthelmintic, pamoja na tiba ya kusaidia na corticosteroids na/au dawa za kuzuia kifafa, na ikiwezekana upasuaji. Muda wa matibabu na kipimo inaweza kutofautiana sana na inategemea hasa idadi, ukubwa, eneo na hatua ya maendeleo ya cysts, edema yao ya uchochezi inayozunguka, ukali na ukali wa dalili za kliniki au ishara.
translations.hypothesis: Matibabu yanaweza kufanywa kwa mtu mmoja mmoja, au kama utoaji wa dawa kwa idadi kubwa ya watu, kulingana na hali za eneo husika na mbinu za udhibiti ambazo zinafanywa. Taeniasis (uambukizi wa teke) inaweza kutibiwa kwa kutumia dawa za kuua minyoo (Praziquantel, Niclosamide, Albendazole). Katika kesi ya neurocysticercosis (uambukizi wa ubongo na teke), kwani uharibifu wa cysts (viini) unaweza kusababisha athari ya uchochezi, matibabu maalum ya ugonjwa unaoendelea ni muhimu, na yanaweza kujumuisha matibabu ya muda mrefu na dozi kubwa za dawa za kuua minyoo, pamoja na matibabu ya kusaidia kwa kutumia corticosteroids (dawa za kupunguza uchochezi) na/au dawa za kuzuia kifafa, na pengine upasuaji. Kipimo na muda wa matibabu unaweza kutofautiana sana na hutegemea hasa idadi, ukubwa, eneo na hatua ya ukuaji wa cysts, uvimbe wa uchochezi unaozunguka, ukali na utovu wa dalili za kliniki.

translations.source: Tetanus Overview: Tetanus is a serious illness contracted through exposure to the spores of the bacterium, Clostridium tetani, which live in soil, saliva, dust and manure. The bacteria can enter the body through a deep cuts, wounds or burns affecting the nervous system. The infection leads to painful muscle contractions, particularly of the jaw and neck muscle, and is commonly known as “lockjaw”. People of all ages can get tetanus but the disease is particularly common and serious in newborn babies and their mothers when the mother is unprotected from tetanus by the vaccine, tetanus toxoid.
translations.reference: Pepopunda Muhtasari: Pepopunda ni ugonjwa hatari unaoambukizwa kwa kuambukizwa na vijidudu vya bakteria, Clostridium tetani, wanaoishi kwenye udongo, mate, vumbi na samadi. Bakteria wanaweza kuingia ndani ya mwili kupitia majeraha ya kina, majeraha au kuchomwa na kuathiri mfumo wa neva. Maambukizi husababisha mikazo ya misuli yenye uchungu, haswa ya misuli ya taya na shingo, na inajulikana kama "lockjaw". Watu wa rika zote wanaweza kupata pepopunda lakini ugonjwa huu ni wa kawaida na mbaya zaidi kwa watoto wachanga na mama zao wakati mama hana kinga dhidi ya pepopunda kwa chanjo, pepopunda toxoid.
translations.hypothesis: Tetanus: Tetanus ni ugonjwa mbaya unaopatikana kutokana na kuwasiliana na spora za bakteria inayoitwa Clostridium tetani, ambayo huishi katika udongo, mate, vumbi na taka za wanyama. Bakteria hizi zinaweza kuingia mwilini kupitia majeraha makubwa, vidonda au kuchomwa ambavyo huathiri mfumo wa neva. Maambukizi haya husababisha misuli kuwa na maumivu makubwa, hasa misuli ya taya na shingo, na ugonjwa huu unajulikana kwa jina la "taya ngumu." Watu wa umri wowote wanaweza kupata tetanus, lakini ugonjwa huu ni wa kawaida sana na hatari zaidi kwa watoto wachanga na mama zao, hasa wakati mama huyo hajalindwa dhidi ya tetanus kwa kutumia chanjo, ambayo ni toxoid ya tetanus.

translations.source: Tetanus occurring during pregnancy or within 6 weeks of the end of pregnancy is called maternal tetanus, while tetanus occurring within the first 28 days of life is called neonatal tetanus. The disease remains an important public health problem in many parts of the world, but especially in low-income countries or districts, where immunization coverage is low and unclean birth practices are common. WHO estimates that in 2018 (the latest year for which estimates are available), 25 000 newborns died from neonatal tetanus, 88% reduction from the situation in 2000. Symptoms: Nearly all cases of tetanus occur in people who have never been vaccinated or in adults who have not kept up to date on their booster shots. Signs and symptoms of tetanus appear anytime from 3 to 21 days after tetanus bacteria enter the body through a wound.
translations.reference: Pepopunda inayotokea wakati wa ujauzito au ndani ya wiki 6 za mwisho wa ujauzito huitwa pepopunda ya mama, wakati pepopunda ikitokea ndani ya siku 28 za kwanza za maisha inaitwa pepopunda changa. Ugonjwa huu unasalia kuwa tatizo muhimu la afya ya umma katika sehemu nyingi za dunia, lakini hasa katika nchi za kipato cha chini au wilaya, ambapo chanjo ni ya chini na njia chafu za uzazi ni za kawaida. WHO inakadiria kuwa katika 2018 (mwaka wa hivi karibuni ambao makadirio yanapatikana), watoto wachanga 25,000 walikufa kutokana na pepopunda ya watoto wachanga, 88% walipungua kutoka hali hiyo mnamo 2000. Dalili: Takriban visa vyote vya pepopunda hutokea kwa watu ambao hawajawahi kupata chanjo au kwa watu wazima ambao hawajasasisha picha zao za nyongeza. Ishara na dalili za pepopunda huonekana wakati wowote kutoka siku 3 hadi 21 baada ya bakteria ya pepopunda kuingia mwilini kupitia kidonda.
translations.hypothesis: Tetanasi inayotokea wakati wa ujauzito au ndani ya wiki 6 baada ya kumalizika kwa ujauzito huitwa tetanus ya mama, wakati tetanus inayotokea ndani ya siku 28 za kwanza za maisha ya mtoto huitwa tetanus ya mtoto. Ugonjwa huu bado ni tatizo kubwa la afya ya umma katika sehemu nyingi za dunia, hasa katika nchi au wilaya zilizo na kipato cha chini, ambapo idadi ya watu waliochanganywa chanjo ni ndogo na mazoea mabaya ya uzazi ni ya kawaida. Shirika la Afya Duniani (WHO) linakadiria kwamba mwaka wa 2018 (mwaka wa hivi karibuni ambapo takwimu zipo), watoto wapya 25,000 walikufa kutokana na tetanus ya mtoto, ambayo ni kupungua kwa asilimia 88 ikilinganishwa na hali iliyokuwa mwaka wa 2000. Dalili: Karibu kesi zote za tetanus hutokea kwa watu ambao hawajawahi kupata chanjo au kwa watu wazima ambao hawajafanya chanjo za kuongeza kinga. Dalili za tetanus zinaweza kuonekana kuanzia siku 3 hadi 21 baada ya bakteria ya tetanus kuingia mwilini kupitia jeraha.

translations.source: Most cases occur within 14 days. Symptoms can include: jaw cramping or the inability to open the mouth muscle spasms often in the back, abdomen and extremities sudden painful muscle spasms often triggered by sudden noises
translations.reference: Kesi nyingi hutokea ndani ya siku 14. Dalili zinaweza kujumuisha: kuuma kwa taya au kutoweza kufungua mdomo misuli ya spasms mara nyingi nyuma, ya tumbo na mwisho mshtuko wa ghafla wa misuli yenye uchungu mara nyingi huchochewa na kelele za ghafla
translations.hypothesis: Matukio mengi hutokea ndani ya siku 14. Dalili zinaweza kujumuisha: maumivu au ugumu katika taya, au uwezekano wa kutoweza kufungua mdomo, misuko ya misuli ambayo mara nyingi hutokea kwenye mgongo, tumbo na viungo vya mwili, na misuko ya ghafla ya misuli ambayo husababisha maumivu, na ambayo mara nyingi husababishwa na sauti za ghafla.

translations.source: trouble swallowing seizures headache fever and sweating changes in blood pressure or fast heart rate.
translations.reference: shida kumeza mishtuko wa moyo maumivu ya kichwa homa na jasho mabadiliko katika shinikizo la damu au kasi ya moyo.
translations.hypothesis: Matatizo ya kunywa, kifafa, maumivu ya kichwa, homa, na jasho, pamoja na mabadiliko katika shinikizo la damu au hali ya moyo kuongezeka.

translations.source: In neonatal tetanus, symptoms include muscle spasms, which are often preceded by the newborn’s inability to suck or breastfeed, and excessive crying. Tetanus is diagnosed on the basis of clinical features and does not require laboratory confirmation. Tetanus requires treatment in a medical facility, often in a referral hospital. People who recover from tetanus do not have natural immunity and can be infected again and therefore need to be immunized. Prevention: Tetanus can be prevented through immunization with tetanus-toxoid-containing vaccines (TTCV), which are included in routine immunization programmes globally and administered during antenatal care contacts.
translations.reference: Katika tetanasi ya watoto wachanga, dalili ni pamoja na mshtuko wa misuli, ambayo mara nyingi hutanguliwa na kutoweza kwa mtoto mchanga kunyonya au kunyonyesha, na kulia sana. Tetanasi hugunduliwa kwa misingi ya vipengele vya kliniki na hauhitaji uthibitisho wa maabara. Pepopunda inahitaji matibabu katika kituo cha matibabu, mara nyingi katika hospitali ya rufaa. Watu wanaopona pepopunda hawana kinga ya asili na wanaweza kuambukizwa tena na hivyo wanahitaji kuchanjwa. Kinga: Pepopunda inaweza kuzuiwa kwa chanjo zenye pepopunda-toxoid (TTCV), ambazo zimejumuishwa katika programu za kawaida za chanjo ulimwenguni kote na kusimamiwa wakati wa mawasiliano ya utunzaji wa ujauzito.
translations.hypothesis: Katika kesi ya tetanasi ya watoto wachanga, dalili zinajumuisha misuguko ya misuli, ambayo mara nyingi huandamana na uwezo wa mtoto kuziba au kunyonyesha, pamoja na kulia kupita kiasi. Tetanasi hutambuliwa kwa msingi wa dalili za kimatibabu na haihitaji uthibitisho wa maabara. Tetanasi inahitaji matibabu katika kituo cha afya, mara nyingi katika hospitali maalum. Watu ambao hupona kutoka tetanasi hawana kinga asili na wanaweza kuambukizwa tena, kwa hivyo wanahitaji kupigwa chanjo. Kuzuia: Tetanasi inaweza kuzuiwa kupitia chanjo kwa kutumia chanjo zenye sumu ya tetanasi (TTCV), ambazo zimejumuishwa katika programu za kawaida za chanjo duniani kote na zinatolewa wakati wa huduma ya uzazi.

translations.source: To be protected throughout life, WHO recommends that an individual receives 6 doses (3 primary plus 3 booster doses) of TTCV. The 3-dose primary series should begin as early as 6 weeks of age, with subsequent doses given with a minimum interval of 4 weeks between doses. The 3 booster doses should preferably be given during the second year of life (12–23 months), at 4–7 years of age, and at 9–15 years of age. Ideally, there should be at least 4 years between booster doses. There are many kinds of vaccines used to protect against tetanus: diphtheria and tetanus (DT) vaccinesdiphtheria, tetanus, and pertussis (whooping cough) (DTaP) vaccinestetanus and diphtheria (Td) vaccinestetanus, diphtheria, and pertussis (Tdap) vaccines.
translations.reference: Ili kulindwa katika maisha yote, WHO inapendekeza kwamba mtu binafsi apokee dozi 6 (3 za msingi pamoja na dozi 3 za nyongeza) za TTCV. Mfululizo wa msingi wa dozi 3 unapaswa kuanza mapema kama wiki 6 za umri, na dozi zinazofuata zipewe na muda wa chini wa wiki 4 kati ya dozi. Dozi 3 za nyongeza zinafaa kutolewa katika mwaka wa pili wa maisha (miezi 12-23), katika umri wa miaka 4-7, na katika umri wa miaka 9-15. Kimsingi, kunapaswa kuwa na angalau miaka 4 kati ya dozi za nyongeza. Kuna aina nyingi za chanjo zinazotumiwa kulinda dhidi ya pepopunda: chanjo ya diphtheria na pepopunda (DT) chanjo ya diphtheria, pepopunda na kifaduro (DTaP) chanjo ya chanjo ya dondakoo na diphtheria (Td) chanjo ya diphtheria, diphtheria na pertussis (Tdap).
translations.hypothesis: Ili mtu apate kinga ya maisha yote, Shirika la Afya Duniani (WHO) inapendekeza kwamba mtu apokee dozi 6 (dozi 3 za msingi pamoja na dozi 3 za ziada) za chanjo dhidi ya ugonjwa wa tetanus. Mfululizo wa dozi 3 za msingi unapaswa kuanza mapema iwezekanavyo, kuanzia umri wa wiki 6, na dozi zifuatazo zinapaswa kutolewa kwa muda wa angalau wiki 4 kati ya kila dozi. Dozi 3 za ziada zinapaswa kutolewa, ikiwezekana, wakati wa mwaka wa pili wa maisha (kati ya miezi 12 na 23), katika umri wa miaka 4-7, na katika umri wa miaka 9-15. Ni vyema kuwa kuna muda wa angalau miaka 4 kati ya dozi za ziada. Kuna aina nyingi za chanjo zinazotumika kulinda dhidi ya ugonjwa wa tetanus, kama vile: chanjo za diphtheria na tetanus (DT), chanjo za diphtheria, tetanus, na pertussis (DTaP), chanjo za tetanus na diphtheria (Td), na chanjo za tetanus, diphtheria, na pertussis (Tdap).

translations.source: Neonatal tetanus can be prevented by immunizing women of reproductive age with TTCV, either during pregnancy or outside of pregnancy. Additionally, robust medical practices can also prevent tetanus disease including clean delivery and cord care during childbirth, and proper wound care for surgical and dental procedures.
translations.reference: Pepopunda ya watoto wachanga inaweza kuzuiwa kwa kuwapa chanjo ya TTCV wanawake walio katika umri wa kuzaa, wakati wa ujauzito au nje ya ujauzito. Zaidi ya hayo, mbinu thabiti za matibabu zinaweza pia kuzuia ugonjwa wa pepopunda ikiwa ni pamoja na kujifungua safi na utunzaji wa kamba wakati wa kuzaa, na utunzaji sahihi wa jeraha kwa upasuaji na meno.
translations.hypothesis: Ugonjwa wa tetanasi unaowapata watoto wachanga unaweza kuzuiwa kwa kuongezewa chanjo ya TTCV kwa wanawake walio katika umri wa kuzaa, iwe wakati wa ujauzito au wakati mwingine. Pia, utunzaji bora wa afya unaweza kuzuia ugonjwa wa tetanasi, ikiwa ni pamoja na utoaji salama na utunzaji wa kamba ya tumbo wakati wa kujifungua, na utunzaji sahihi wa majeraha kwa ajili ya taratibu za upasuaji na meno.

translations.source: Tick-borne encephalitis Overview: Tick-borne encephalitis (TBE) is an important cause of viral infections of the central nervous system in eastern, central, northern and increasingly western European countries, and in northern China, Mongolia, and the Russian Federation. Tick-borne encephalitis virus is a member of the family Flaviviridae. Approximately 10 000–12 000 clinical cases of tick-borne encephalitis are reported each year, but this figure is believed to be significantly lower than the actual total number of clinical cases. The vast majority of infections with the virus result from infected ticks, which often remain firmly attached to the skin for days.
translations.reference: Ugonjwa wa encephalitis unaoenezwa na Jibu Muhtasari: Ugonjwa wa encephalitis unaoenezwa na Jibu (TBE) ni sababu muhimu ya maambukizo ya virusi ya mfumo mkuu wa neva katika nchi za mashariki, kati, kaskazini na inazidi kuongezeka magharibi mwa Ulaya, na kaskazini mwa Uchina, Mongolia, na Shirikisho la Urusi. Virusi vya encephalitis vinavyoenezwa na Jibu ni mwanachama wa familia ya Flaviviridae. Takriban visa 10 000-12 000 vya ugonjwa wa encephalitis unaoenezwa na kupe huripotiwa kila mwaka, lakini takwimu hii inaaminika kuwa chini sana kuliko idadi halisi ya kesi za kimatibabu. Idadi kubwa ya maambukizo na virusi hutokana na kupe walioambukizwa, ambao mara nyingi hubakia kushikamana na ngozi kwa siku.
translations.hypothesis: Ugonjwa wa encephalitis unaosababishwa na makini: Ugonjwa wa encephalitis unaosababishwa na makini (TBE) ni mojawapo ya sababu muhimu za maambukizi ya virusi kwenye mfumo wa neva katika nchi za Ulaya ya mashariki, kati, kaskazini, na siku hizi pia katika nchi za Ulaya ya magharibi, pamoja na kaskazini mwa China, Mongolia, na Shirikisho la Urusi. Virusi vya encephalitis vinavyosababishwa na makini ni sehemu ya familia ya Flaviviridae. Takriban visa 10,000 hadi 12,000 vya ugonjwa wa encephalitis unaosababishwa na makini hutangazwa kila mwaka, lakini idadi hii inakadiriwa kuwa ndogo sana kuliko idadi halisi ya visa. Sehemu kubwa ya maambukizi ya virusi hivi husababishwa na makini yaliyo na virusi, ambayo mara nyingi husalia yakamezwa kwenye ngozi kwa siku kadhaa.

translations.source: On rare occasions, infection can result from consumption of unpasteurized milk from infect goats, sheep or cows. People come in contact with the ticks during outdoor activities in forested areas up to an altitude of about 2000 meters. There is no direct person-to-person transmission. Symptoms: Most infections remain asymptomatic. In case of clinical illness, the incubation period for tick-borne encephalitis lasts 2–28 days (most commonly 7–14 days) and is followed by 1–8 days of general cold symptoms, such as fatigue, headache and general malaise, usually combined with fever of ≥38 °C.
translations.reference: Katika matukio machache, maambukizi yanaweza kutokana na unywaji wa maziwa ambayo hayajasafishwa kutoka kwa mbuzi, kondoo au ng'ombe. Watu hugusana na kupe wakati wa shughuli za nje katika maeneo ya misitu hadi mwinuko wa mita 2000. Hakuna maambukizi ya moja kwa moja kutoka kwa mtu hadi kwa mtu. Dalili: Maambukizi mengi hubaki bila dalili. Katika kesi ya ugonjwa wa kliniki, kipindi cha incubation cha encephalitis inayoenezwa na kupe huchukua siku 2-28 (mara nyingi siku 7-14) na hufuatiwa na siku 1-8 za dalili za jumla za baridi, kama vile uchovu, maumivu ya kichwa na malaise ya jumla kawaida hujumuishwa na homa ya ≥38 °C.
translations.hypothesis: Mara chache, maambukizi yanaweza kutokea kutokana na kunywa maziwa ambayo hayajapigwa joto kutoka kwa mbuzi, kondoo au ng'ombe walioambukizwa. Watu huwasiliana na vidudu hivi wakati wa shughuli za nje katika maeneo ya misitu, hadi urefu wa takriban mita 2000. Hakuna usambazaji wa maambukizi kutoka mtu hadi mtu. Dalili: Maambukizi mengi hayatokeza dalili zozote. Katika kesi ya ugonjwa, kipindi cha kutokea kwa dalili (incubation period) kwa ugonjwa wa encephalitis unaosababishwa na vidudu hivi ni siku 2 hadi 28 (kawaida siku 7 hadi 14), na kisha kuna kipindi cha siku 1 hadi 8 cha dalili za kawaida za homa, kama vile uchovu, maumivu ya kichwa na hisia ya udhaifu, ambazo kwa kawaida huambatana na homa ya digrii 38 au zaidi.

translations.source: After an asymptomatic interval of 1–20 days up to 15% of patients experience a second phase of the disease characterized by fever frequently exceeding 40 °C and signs of central nervous system involvement, such as meningitis (e.g. fever, headache, and a stiff neck), encephalitis (e.g., drowsiness, confusion, and sensory disturbances), myelitis or radiculitis. Encephalitis developing during this second phase may result in paralysis, permanent sequelae or death. About 1% of cases with neurological pathologies may die; higher fatality rates have been reported from the Russian federation, which may be related to a different virus subtype. Severity of illness increases with age of the patient, but fatalities have been reported from all age groups. There is no specific treatment for tick-borne encephalitis.
translations.reference: Baada ya muda usio na dalili wa siku 1-20 hadi 15% ya wagonjwa hupata awamu ya pili ya ugonjwa huo na homa inayozidi 40 ° C mara kwa mara na ishara za kuhusika kwa mfumo mkuu wa neva, kama vile meninjitisi (k.m. homa, maumivu ya kichwa na kukakamaa), encephalitis (kwa mfano, kusinzia, kuchanganyikiwa, na usumbufu wa hisia), myelitis au radiculitis. Ugonjwa wa encephalitis unaoendelea katika awamu hii ya pili unaweza kusababisha kupooza, matokeo ya kudumu au kifo. Karibu 1% ya kesi zilizo na patholojia za neva zinaweza kufa; viwango vya juu vya vifo vimeripotiwa kutoka kwa shirikisho la Urusi, ambalo linaweza kuhusishwa na aina tofauti ya virusi. Ukali wa ugonjwa huongezeka na umri wa mgonjwa, lakini vifo vimeripotiwa kutoka kwa vikundi vyote vya umri. Hakuna matibabu maalum kwa ugojwa tick-borne encephalitis.
translations.hypothesis: Baada ya kipindi ambacho hakina dalili za kawaida, ambacho kinaweza kudumu kwa siku 1 hadi 20, hadi asilimia 15 ya wagonjwa huonyesha awamu ya pili ya ugonjwa, ambayo inaweza kuambatana na homa inayozidi mara nyingi digrii 40 °C, pamoja na dalili za uhusiano na mfumo wa neva, kama vile meningitis (kwa mfano, homa, maumivu ya kichwa, na uti wa shingo unaokali), encephalitis (kwa mfano, usingizi, kuchanganyikiwa, na matatizo ya hisia), myelitis, au radiculitis. Encephalitis inayotokea katika awamu hii ya pili inaweza kusababisha ulemavu, matokeo ya kudumu, au kifo. Karibu asilimia 1 ya wagonjwa walio na matatizo ya neva wanaweza kufa; vifo vingi zaidi yameripotiwa kutoka Shirikisho la Urusi, ambayo yanaweza kuhusiana na aina tofauti ya virusi. Ugonjwa huo huwa mbaya zaidi kadri umri wa mgonjwa unavyoongezeka, lakini vifo vimeripotiwa katika makundi yote ya umri. Hakuna tiba maalum kwa encephalitis inayotokana na mende.

translations.source: Prevention: People can protect themselves from ticks by wearing appropriate clothing, including long trousers and closed footwear, when hiking or camping in countries or areas at risk. The whole body should be inspected daily and attached ticks removed as soon as possible. The consumption of unpasteurized dairy products should also be avoided in those areas. Immunization offers the most effective protection. Currently, there are 4 widely used vaccines of assured quality:
translations.reference: Kinga: Watu wanaweza kujilinda dhidi ya kupe kwa kuvaa mavazi yanayofaa, ikiwa ni pamoja na suruali ndefu na viatu vilivyofungwa, wanapopanda au kupiga kambi katika nchi au maeneo yaliyo hatarini. Mwili mzima unapaswa kuchunguzwa kila siku na kupe zilizounganishwa kuondolewa haraka iwezekanavyo. Matumizi ya bidhaa za maziwa ambazo hazijasafishwa pia zinapaswa kuepukwa katika maeneo hayo. Kinga hutoa ulinzi bora zaidi. Hivi sasa, kuna chanjo 4 zinazotumiwa sana za ubora uliohakikishwa:
translations.hypothesis: Kuzuia: Watu wanaweza kujilinda dhidi ya buibui kwa kuvaa nguo zinazofaa, ikiwa ni pamoja na suruali ndefu na viatu vilivyofunikwa, wakati wa matembezi au kupiga kambi katika nchi au maeneo yenye hatari. Mwili wote unapaswa kuchunguzwa kila siku, na buibui zilizoshikamana zinapaswa kuondolewa haraka iwezekanavyo. Pia, matumizi ya bidhaa za maziwa ambazo hazijapashwa (yaani, hazijachomwa) yanapaswa kuepukwa katika maeneo hayo. Chanjo ndiyo njia bora zaidi ya kujilinda. Kwa sasa, kuna chanjo 4 zinazotumika sana na ambazo zina ubora thabiti:

translations.source: FSME-Immun and Encepur, manufactured in Austria and Germany respectively, and based on European strains of the virus; and TBE-Moscow and EnceVir, manufactured in the Russian Federation and based on Far-Eastern strains. The 4 vaccines are considered to be safe and effective. In areas where the disease is highly endemic, WHO recommends that vaccination be offered to all age groups, including children. Ticks also transmit Borreliosis (Lyme disease), which is a bacterial infection.
translations.reference: FSME-Immun na Encepur, iliyotengenezwa nchini Austria na Ujerumani ipasavyo, na kulingana na aina za virusi za Ulaya ; na TBE-Moscow na EnceVir, iliyotengenezwa katika Shirikisho la Urusi na kwa kulingana na matatizo ya Mashariki ya Mbali. Chanjo 4 zinachukuliwa kuwa salama na zenye ufanisi. Katika maeneo ambayo ugonjwa huo umeenea sana, WHO inapendekeza kwamba chanjo itolewe kwa vikundi vyote vya umri, pamoja na watoto. Kupe pia huambukiza Borreliosis (ugonjwa wa Lyme), ambao ni maambukizi ya bakteria.
translations.hypothesis:
Tafadhali angalia tafsiri hii:

FSME-Immun na Encepur, ambazo hutengenezwa Austria na Ujerumani mtawatimuti, na zinatokana na aina za virusi ambazo zipo Ulaya; na TBE-Moscow na EnceVir, ambazo hutengenezwa Shirikisho la Urusi na zinatokana na aina za virusi ambazo zipo Mashariki mwa Siberia. Chanjo hizo nne zinafanywa kuwa salama na za ufanisi. Katika maeneo ambapo ugonjwa huo unaenea sana, Shirika la Afya Duniani (WHO) inapendekeza kwamba chanjo ipatikane kwa makundi yote ya umri, ikiwa ni pamoja na watoto. Mbu pia husambaza ugonjwa wa Borreliosis (ugonjwa wa Lyme), ambao ni maambukizi ya bakteria.

translations.source: TBE vaccination is not effective against this disease, which however is treatable with antimicrobials.
translations.reference: Chanjo ya TBE haifai dhidi ya ugonjwa huu, ambao hata hivyo unatibika kwa dawa za kuua viini.
translations.hypothesis: Chanjo ya TBE haifanyi kazi dhidi ya ugonjwa huu, lakini ugonjwa huo unaweza kutibika kwa kutumia dawa za kuzuia bakteria.

translations.source: Tobacco Overview: Nicotine contained in tobacco is highly addictive and tobacco use is a major risk factor for cardiovascular and respiratory diseases, over 20 different types or subtypes of cancer, and many other debilitating health conditions. Every year, more than 8 million people die from tobacco use. Most tobacco-related deaths occur in low- and middle-income countries, which are often targets of intensive tobacco industry interference and marketing. Tobacco can also be deadly for non-smokers.
translations.reference: Tumbaku Muhtasari: Nikotini iliyo katika tumbaku inalevya sana na utumiaji wa tumbaku ni sababu kuu ya hatari kwa magonjwa ya moyo na mishipa na ya kupumua, zaidi ya aina 20 tofauti za saratani, na hali zingine nyingi za kiafya zinazodhoofisha. Kila mwaka, zaidi ya watu milioni 8 hufa kutokana na matumizi ya tumbaku. Vifo vingi vinavyohusiana na tumbaku hutokea katika nchi zenye kipato cha chini na cha kati, ambazo mara nyingi hulengwa na kuingiliwa kwa sekta ya tumbaku na masoko. Tumbaku pia inaweza kuwa mbaya kwa wasiovuta sigara.
translations.hypothesis: Mwangaza Kuhusu Toba: Nikotini iliyomo katika toba ni dawa inayotumika sana na matumizi ya toba ni hatari kubwa kwa magonjwa ya moyo na mapafu, aina zaidi ya 20 za saratani, na hali nyingine nyingi za kiafya ambazo husababisha ulemavu. Kila mwaka, zaidi ya watu milioni 8 hufa kutokana na matumizi ya toba. Vifo vingi vinavyohusiana na toba hutokea katika nchi zilizo na kipato cha chini na cha kati, ambazo mara nyingi huwa lengo la uingiliaji na uuzaji mkakati wa sekta ya toba. Toba pia inaweza kuwa hatari kwa watu ambao hawavuti.

translations.source: Second-hand smoke exposure has also been implicated in adverse health outcomes, causing 1.2 million deaths annually. Nearly half of all children breathe air polluted by tobacco smoke and 65 000 children die each year due to illnesses related to second-hand smoke. Smoking while pregnant can lead to several life-long health conditions for babies. Heated tobacco products (HTPs) contain tobacco and expose users to toxic emissions, many of which cause cancer and are harmful to health. Electronic nicotine delivery systems (ENDS) and electronic non-nicotine delivery systems (ENNDS), commonly known as e-cigarettes, do not contain tobacco and may or may not contain nicotine, but are harmful to health and undoubtedly unsafe.
translations.reference: Uvutaji wa moshi wa sigara pia umehusishwa katika matokeo mabaya ya kiafya, na kusababisha vifo milioni 1.2 kila mwaka. Takriban nusu ya watoto wote huvuta hewa iliyochafuliwa na moshi wa tumbaku na watoto 65,000 hufa kila mwaka kutokana na magonjwa yanayohusiana na moshi wa sigara. Kuvuta sigara wakati wa ujauzito kunaweza kusababisha hali kadhaa za kiafya kwa watoto. Bidhaa za tumbaku zinazopashwa joto (HTPs) zina tumbaku na huwaweka watumiaji kwenye hewa chafu zenye sumu, nyingi ambazo husababisha saratani na ni hatari kwa afya. Mifumo ya kielektroniki ya utoaji wa nikotini (ENDS) na mifumo ya kielektroniki ya uwasilishaji isiyo ya nikotini (ENNDS), inayojulikana kama sigara za kielektroniki, haina tumbaku na inaweza au isiwe na nikotini, lakini inadhuru kwa afya na bila shaka si salama.
translations.hypothesis: Uhusiano wa moshi wa sigara unaweza kusababisha matatizo ya kiafya, na husababisha vifo vya watu milioni 1.2 kila mwaka. Karibu nusu ya watoto wote hupumua hewa iliyochanganywa na moshi wa sigara, na watoto 65,000 hufa kila mwaka kutokana na magonjwa yanayohusiana na moshi wa sigara. Kuvuta sigara wakati wa ujauzito kunaweza kusababisha matatizo mengi ya kiafya ambayo yanaendelea kwa maisha yote kwa watoto. Bidhaa za sigara ambazo hutumia joto (HTPs) zina tumbaku na huwafichua watumiaji kwa kemikali hatari, ambazo nyingi husababisha saratani na ni hatari kwa afya. Vifaa vya kielektroniki ambavyo hutumia nikotini (ENDS) na vifaa vya kielektroniki ambavyo havitumii nikotini (ENNDS), ambavyo kwa kawaida hujulikana kama "e-sigara," havina tumbaku na zinaweza au hazina nikotini, lakini ni hatari kwa afya na hakika hazina usalama.

translations.source: However, it is too early to provide a clear answer on the long-term impact of HTPs and/or e-cigarette use. Impact: An estimated 1.3 billion people worldwide use tobacco products, 80% of whom are in low- and middle-income countries. Tobacco use contributes to poverty by diverting household spending from basic needs, such as food and shelter, to tobacco. This spending behaviour is difficult to curb because tobacco is so addictive. It also causes premature death and disability of productive age adults in households thus leading to reduced household income and increased healthcare costs.
translations.reference: Hata hivyo, ni mapema mno kutoa jibu wazi kuhusu athari za muda mrefu za HTPs na/au matumizi ya sigara za kielektroniki. Athari: Inakadiriwa kuwa watu bilioni 1.3 duniani kote wanatumia bidhaa za tumbaku, 80% kati yao wakiwa katika nchi za kipato cha chini na cha kati. Utumiaji wa tumbaku huchangia umaskini kwa kubadilisha matumizi ya kaya kutoka kwa mahitaji ya kimsingi, kama vile chakula na malazi, hadi tumbaku. Tabia hii ya matumizi ni ngumu kudhibiti kwa sababu tumbaku ina uraibu sana. Pia husababisha vifo vya mapema na ulemavu wa watu wazima wenye umri wa kuzalisha katika kaya hivyo kusababisha kupungua kwa mapato ya kaya na kuongezeka kwa gharama za huduma za afya.
translations.hypothesis: Hata hivyo, bado ni mapema kutoa jibu wazi kuhusu athari za muda mrefu za matumizi ya vifaa vya kutumia tumbaku (HTPs) na/au sigara za kielektroniki. Athari: Inakadiriwa kwamba watu bilioni 1.3 duniani kote hutumia bidhaa za tumbaku, ambapo asilimia 80 yao wanaishi katika nchi zilizo na kipato cha chini na cha kati. Matumizi ya tumbaku huongeza umaskini kwa kukeleza matumizi ya familia kutoka kwa mahitaji ya msingi, kama vile chakula na makazi, hadi kwenye tumbaku. Tabia hii ya matumizi ni ngumu kuizuia kwa sababu tumbaku huleta uraibu mkubwa. Pia husababisha vifo vya mapema na ulemavu kwa watu wazima wenye umri wa kufanya kazi, na hivyo kusababisha kupungua kwa mapato ya familia na kuongezeka kwa gharama za huduma za afya.

translations.source: In addition to the detrimental impact of tobacco on health, the total economic cost of smoking (from health expenditures and productivity losses together) are estimated to be around US$ 1.4 trillion per year, equivalent in magnitude to 1.8% of the world's annual gross domestic product (GDP). Almost 40% of this cost occurred in developing countries, highlighting the substantial burden these countries suffer. Tobacco taxes are applied in part to combat this effect and are seen as the most cost-effective way of curbing tobacco use, particularly among youth and low-income populations. A tax increase that increases tobacco prices by 10% decreases tobacco consumption by about 4% in high-income countries and about 5% in low- and middle-income countries. WHO response: The scale of this human and economic tragedy is shocking, but it’s also preventable.
translations.reference: Pamoja na athari mbaya za tumbaku kwa afya, jumla ya gharama za kiuchumi za uvutaji wa sigara (kutokana na matumizi ya afya na upotevu wa tija kwa pamoja) inakadiriwa kuwa karibu dola za Marekani trilioni 1.4 kwa mwaka, sawa na asilimia 1.8 ya pato la taifa kwa mwaka (GDP). Takriban 40% ya gharama hii ilitokea katika nchi zinazoendelea, ikionyesha mzigo mkubwa ambao nchi hizi zinakabiliwa. Ushuru wa tumbaku hutumiwa kwa kiasi ili kukabiliana na athari hii na inaonekana kama njia ya gharama nafuu zaidi ya kuzuia matumizi ya tumbaku, hasa miongoni mwa vijana na watu wa kipato cha chini. Ongezeko la ushuru ambalo linaongeza bei ya tumbaku kwa 10% hupunguza matumizi ya tumbaku kwa takriban 4% katika nchi zenye mapato ya juu na karibu 5% katika nchi zenye kipato cha chini na cha kati. Jibu la WHO: Kiwango cha janga hili la kibinadamu na kiuchumi ni la kushangaza, lakini pia linaweza kuzuilika.
translations.hypothesis: Mbali na athari mbaya za tumbaku kwa afya, gharama ya uchumi ya jumla ya kuvuta sigara (ikiwa ni pamoja na matumizi ya fedha za afya na hasara za uzalishaji) inakadiriwa kuwa dola za Marekani trilioni 1.4 kila mwaka, ambayo ni sawa na asilimia 1.8 ya jumla ya bidhaa ya taifa (GDP) ya dunia. Takriban asilimia 40 ya gharama hii ilitokea katika nchi zinazoendelea, jambo ambalo linaonyesha mzigo mkubwa ambao nchi hizi zinakumbana nao. Kodi za tumbaku hutumika, kwa sehemu, ili kupambana na athari hii, na zinaonekana kama njia bora na yenye gharama nafuu ya kupunguza matumizi ya tumbaku, hasa miongoni mwa vijana na watu wenye kipato cha chini. Kuongezeka kwa kodi ambayo huongeza bei ya tumbaku kwa asilimia 10 hupunguza matumizi ya tumbaku kwa takriban asilimia 4 katika nchi zilizo na kipato cha juu, na takriban asilimia 5 katika nchi zilizo na kipato cha chini na cha kati. Jibu la WHO: Ukubwa wa janga hili la kibinadamu na kiuchumi ni wa kushangaza, lakini pia linaweza kuepukwa.

translations.source: Big Tobacco — along with all manufacturers of tobacco products — is fighting to ensure the dangers of their products are concealed, but we are fighting back: In 2003, WHO Member States unanimously adopted the WHO Framework Convention on Tobacco Control (WHO FCTC), the only public health treaty under the auspices of WHO. In force since 2005, it has currently 181 Parties. To help countries implement the WHO FCTC, WHO introduced MPOWER, a package of technical measures and resources, each of which corresponds to at least one provision of the WHO FCTC. MPOWER builds the capacity of countries to implement 6 measures to reduce the demand for tobacco products: monitor tobacco use and prevention policies, protect people from tobacco use, offer help to quit tobacco use, warn about the dangers of tobacco, enforce bans on tobacco advertising, promotion and sponsorship and raise taxes on tobacco.
translations.reference: Tumbaku Kubwa - pamoja na watengenezaji wote wa bidhaa za tumbaku - inapigania kuhakikisha hatari za bidhaa zao zinafichwa, lakini tunapambana: Mnamo 2003, Nchi Wanachama wa WHO zilipitisha kwa kauli moja Mkataba wa Mfumo wa WHO wa Kudhibiti Tumbaku (WHO FCTC), mkataba pekee wa afya ya umma chini ya udhamini wa WHO. Imetumika tangu 2005, kwa sasa ina Vyama 181. Ili kusaidia nchi kutekeleza WHO FCTC, WHO ilianzisha MPOWER, kifurushi cha hatua za kiufundi na rasilimali, ambayo kila moja inalingana na angalau kifungu kimoja cha WHO FCTC. MPOWER inajenga uwezo wa nchi kutekeleza hatua 6 za kupunguza mahitaji ya bidhaa za tumbaku: kufuatilia matumizi ya tumbaku na sera za kuzuia, kulinda watu dhidi ya matumizi ya tumbaku, kutoa msaada wa kuacha matumizi ya tumbaku, kuonya juu ya hatari ya tumbaku, kutekeleza marufuku ya utangazaji wa tumbaku kukuza na kufadhili na kuongeza kodi kwenye tumbaku.
translations.hypothesis: Kampuni kubwa za utengenezaji sigara—pamoja na wazalishaji wote wa bidhaa za tumbaku—zinaendelea kujaribu kuhifadhi hatari za bidhaa zao, lakini sisi tunapinga: Mnamo mwaka wa 2003, nchi wanachama za Shirika la Afya Duniani (WHO) zilikubali kwa pamoja Mkataba wa Kimataifa wa WHO kuhusu Udhibiti wa Tumbaku (WHO FCTC), ambao ndio mkataba pekee wa afya ya umma unaotekelezwa na WHO. Mkataba huo, ambao ulianza kutumika mnamo mwaka wa 2005, una nchi 181 ambazo zimejiunga naye. Ili kusaidia nchi kutekeleza WHO FCTC, WHO ilianzisha MPOWER, ambayo ni mkusanyiko wa hatua na rasilimali za kiufundi, ambazo kila moja inalingana na angalau kipengele kimoja cha WHO FCTC. MPOWER inalenga kuimarisha uwezo wa nchi katika kutekeleza hatua 6 za kupunguza mahitaji ya bidhaa za tumbaku: kufuatilia matumizi ya tumbaku na sera za kuzuia, kulinda watu dhidi ya matumizi ya tumbaku, kutoa msaada kwa wale wanaotaka kuacha matumizi ya tumbaku, kutoa onyo kuhusu hatari za tumbaku, kutekeleza marufuku ya matangazo, uendelezaji na ufadhili wa tumbaku, na kuongeza kodi za tumbaku.

translations.source: Trachoma Overview: Trachoma is a disease of the eye and the leading infectious cause of blindness worldwide. It is caused by an obligate intracellular bacterium called Chlamydia trachomatis. Infection is transmitted from person to person by direct or indirect transfer of ocular and nasal discharges of infected people; indirect transfer includes carriage on the body of species of flies. Preschool-age children harbour the principal reservoir of infection.
translations.reference: Trakoma Muhtasari: Trakoma ni ugonjwa wa macho na unaongoza kwa kusababisha upofu duniani kote. Inasababishwa na bakteria ya lazima ndani ya seli inayoitwa Chlamydia trachomatis. Maambukizi hupitishwa kutoka kwa mtu hadi kwa mtu kwa uhamisho wa moja kwa moja au usio wa moja kwa moja wa kutokwa kwa macho na pua ya watu walioambukizwa; uhamisho usio wa moja kwa moja unajumuisha kubeba kwenye mwili wa aina za nzi. Watoto wa umri wa shule ya mapema huhifadhi hifadhi kuu ya maambukizi.
translations.hypothesis: Mwangaza: Mwangaza ni ugonjwa unaowahusu macho na ndio chanzo kikuu cha kupoteza mwangaza duniani kote. Ugonjwa huu husababishwa na bakteria maalum inayojulikana kama Chlamydia trachomatis, ambayo huishi ndani ya seli. Maambukizi husambazwa kutoka kwa mtu mmoja hadi mwingine kupitia kuwasiliana moja kwa moja au kupitia vitu ambavyo vina maji au mkojo kutoka kwa watu walioambukizwa; usambazaji usio wa moja kwa moja unaweza kufanyika kupitia wadudu kama vile nzi. Watoto wadogo huwa na idadi kubwa ya bakteria wanaosababisha ugonjwa huu.

translations.source: Models suggest that an individual requires more than 150 lifetime infections to develop the blinding complications of trachoma. Trachoma is endemic in some of the world’s poorest populations, who live in rural and remote areas and have highly inadequate access to water, sanitation and healthcare. Africa is the most affected continent, but it also has the most widespread control efforts. The disease is also found in Central and South America, Asia, Australia, and the Middle East. As of 2 January 2020, 13 countries had reported achieving elimination goals.
translations.reference: Mifano zinaeleza kuwa mtu anahitaji zaidi ya maambukizi 150 kwa maisha yote ili kuendeleza matatizo ya upofu ya trakoma. Trakoma imeenea katika baadhi ya watu maskini zaidi duniani, ambao wanaishi vijijini na maeneo ya mbali na hawana upatikanaji wa kutosha wa maji, usafi wa mazingira na huduma za afya. Afrika ndilo bara lililoathiriwa zaidi, lakini pia lina juhudi kubwa zaidi za kudhibiti. Ugonjwa huo pia hupatikana Amerika ya Kati na Kusini, Asia, Australia, na Mashariki ya Kati. Kufikia 2 Januari 2020, nchi 13 zilikuwa zimeripoti kufikia malengo ya kuondoa.
translations.hypothesis: Mitindo ya utafiti inaonyesha kwamba mtu anahitaji kuambukizwa zaidi ya mara 150 katika maisha yake ili kupata matatizo makubwa ya trachoma ambayo husababisha upofu. Trachoma ni magonjwa ambayo huenea sana katika baadhi ya makundi maskini zaidi duniani, ambayo hufanya maisha yao katika maeneo ya vijijini na mbali, na ambayo yana upungufu mkubwa wa upatikanaji wa maji safi, usafi, na huduma za afya. Afrika ndiyo bara ambalo limeathirika zaidi, lakini pia ndilo ambalo lina juhudi kubwa za kudhibiti ugonjwa huo. Pia, ugonjwa huu unaweza kupatikana katika Amerika ya Kati na Kusini, Asia, Australia, na Mashariki ya Kati. Mnamo Januari 2, 2020, nchi 13 ziliripoti kwamba zimefanikiwa katika kufikia malengo ya kuondoa ugonjwa huo.

translations.source: In 2019 alone, 92 622 people received surgical treatment for advanced trachoma and 95.2 million were treated with antibiotics. Despite these successes, the disease remains a serious public health concern, with an estimated US$ 8 billion annual loss in productivity due to blindness and visual impairment. Symptoms: Infection is associated with inflammatory changes in the conjunctiva known as “active trachoma”. An individual’s immune system can clear a single episode of infection, but in endemic communities, re-acquisition of the organism occurs frequently. After years of repeated episodes of active trachoma, the inside of the eyelid can become so severely scarred (trachomatous conjunctival scarring) that it turns inwards and causes the eyelashes to rub against the eyeball (trachomatous trichiasis), resulting in constant pain and light intolerance; this and other alterations of the eye can lead to scarring of the cornea.
translations.reference: Katika mwaka wa 2019 pekee, watu 92 622 walipata matibabu ya upasuaji kwa trakoma ya hali ya juu na milioni 95.2 walitibiwa kwa viua vijasumu. Licha ya mafanikio hayo, ugonjwa huu bado ni tatizo kubwa la afya ya jamii, huku kukiwa na wastani wa hasara ya dola za Marekani bilioni 8 kwa mwaka katika uzalishaji kutokana na upofu na ulemavu wa macho. Dalili: Maambukizi yanahusishwa na mabadiliko ya uchochezi katika kiwambo cha sikio inayojulikana kama "trakoma hai". Kinga ya mtu binafsi inaweza kufuta sehemu moja ya maambukizi, lakini katika jumuiya za endemic, kupatikana tena kwa viumbe hutokea mara kwa mara. Baada ya miaka ya matukio ya mara kwa mara ya trakoma hai, ndani ya kope inaweza kuwa na kovu kali sana (kovu la trachomatous conjunctival scarring) kwamba hugeuka ndani na kusababisha kope kusugua kwenye mboni ya jicho (trachomatous trichiasis), na kusababisha maumivu ya mara kwa mara na kutovumilia mwanga; mabadiliko haya na mengine ya jicho yanaweza kusababisha kovu ya konea.
translations.hypothesis: Mnamo mwaka wa 2019 pekee, watu 92,622 walipokea matibabu ya upasuaji kwa ajili ya trachoma iliyosonga mbele, na watu milioni 95.2 walitibiwa kwa kutumia dawa za kuzuia bakteria. Licha ya mafanikio haya, ugonjwa huo bado ni tatizo kubwa la afya ya umma, na inakadiriwa kusababisha hasara ya dola bilioni 8 za Marekani kila mwaka kutokana na upofu na udhaifu wa kuona. Dalili: Maambukizi husababishwa na mabadiliko ya uchochezi kwenye utando wa jicho, ambayo huitwa "trachoma inayobadilika." Mfumo wa kinga wa mtu binafsi unaweza kuondoa maambukizi kwa mara moja, lakini katika maeneo ambapo ugonjwa huo ni wa kawaida, maambukizi yanaweza kurudiwa mara kwa mara. Baada ya miaka ya matukio ya mara kwa mara ya trachoma inayobadilika, sehemu ya ndani ya mimbawa ya jicho inaweza kuwa na makovu makubwa sana (makovu ya trachoma kwenye utando wa jicho) kiasi kwamba inageuka ndani na kusababisha manyoya ya jicho kukaba kwenye jicho (trachiasis ya trachoma), na kusababisha maumivu ya mara kwa mara na kutokuvumilia mwanga; hii na mabadiliko mengine ya jicho yanaweza kusababisha makovu kwenye cornea.

translations.source: Left untreated, this condition leads to the formation of irreversible opacities, with resulting visual impairment or blindness. The age at which this occurs depends on several factors including local transmission intensity. In very highly endemic communities, it can occur in childhood, though onset of visual impairment between the ages of 30 and 40 years is more typical. Women are blinded up to four times as often as men, probably due to more frequent contact with infected children. Individuals living in the poorest and most remote communities of Africa, Asia, Australia, the Middle East, Central and South America and the Pacific Islands are at risk.
translations.reference: Ikiachwa bila kutibiwa, hali hii husababisha kuundwa kwa opacities isiyoweza kutenduliwa, na kusababisha uharibifu wa kuona au upofu. Umri ambao hii hutokea inategemea mambo kadhaa ikiwa ni pamoja na ukubwa wa maambukizi ya ndani. Katika jamii zenye magonjwa mengi, inaweza kutokea utotoni, ingawa mwanzo wa ulemavu wa kuona kati ya umri wa miaka 30 na 40 ni kawaida zaidi. Wanawake hupofushwa hadi mara nne zaidi ya wanaume, labda kutokana na kuwasiliana mara kwa mara na watoto walioambukizwa. Watu wanaoishi katika jamii maskini zaidi na za mbali zaidi za Afrika, Asia, Australia, Mashariki ya Kati, Amerika ya Kati na Kusini na Visiwa vya Pasifiki wako hatarini.
translations.hypothesis: Iwapo hali hii haitibiwi, husababisha kuundwa kwa kasoro ambazo hazirejeuki, na hivyo kusababisha udhaifu wa kuona au kulemewa kabisa. Umri ambao hali hii huanza kutokea hutegemea mambo kadhaa, ikiwa ni pamoja na ukubwa wa kuenea kwa ugonjwa katika eneo husika. Katika maeneo ambapo ugonjwa huu ni wa kawaida sana, unaweza kutokea hata kwa watoto, ingawa kwa kawaida huanza kuonekana kati ya umri wa miaka 30 na 40. Wanawake huwa na uwezekano mkubwa wa kulemewa kuliko wanaume, pengine kwa sababu ya kuwasiliana mara kwa mara na watoto walioambukizwa. Watu wanaowakaa katika maeneo maskini na mbali zaidi ya Afrika, Asia, Australia, Mashariki ya Kati, Amerika ya Kati na Kusini, na Visiwa vya Pasifiki wako hatarini.

translations.source: Treatment: Trachoma can be prevented by limiting environmental risk factors. These include inadequate hygiene, crowded households, inadequate access to water and inadequate access to and use of sanitation. Infections usually begin at an early age and grow less frequent and shorter in duration with increasing age. Infections are most often acquired through family and contact with others with active disease. Repeated infections cause the most serious symptoms of the disease.
translations.reference: Matibabu: Trakoma inaweza kuzuiwa kwa kupunguza mambo hatarishi ya kimazingira. Haya ni pamoja na ukosefu wa usafi wa mazingira, kaya zenye msongamano wa watu, ukosefu wa maji na uhaba wa upatikanaji na matumizi ya vyoo. Maambukizi kwa kawaida huanza katika umri mdogo na kukua chini ya mara kwa mara kwa muda mfupi na kuongezeka kwa umri. Maambukizi mara nyingi hupatikana kwa njia ya familia na kuwasiliana na wengine wenye ugonjwa hai. Maambukizi ya kujirudia husababisha dalili mbaya zaidi za ugonjwa huo.
translations.hypothesis: Matibabu: Ugonjwa wa trachoma unaweza kuzuiwa kwa kupunguza mambo yanayohusiana na mazingira ambayo huongeza hatari ya ugonjwa. Mambo haya ni pamoja na usafi duni, makazi yenye watu wengi, upungufu wa upatikanaji wa maji, na upungufu wa upatikanaji na matumizi ya huduma za usafi. Mara nyingi, maambukizi huanza katika umri mdogo, na hupungua ukubwa na muda kadri umri unavyoongezeka. Maambukizi hayo kwa kawaida hupatikana kupitia familia na kuwasiliana na watu wengine ambao wana ugonjwa huo. Maambukizi yanayorudiwa husababisha dalili kali zaidi za ugonjwa huo.

translations.source: Trachoma can be eliminated as a public health problem using the SAFE strategy, comprising: Surgery to treat trachomatous trichiasis, the blinding stage of the disease; Antibiotics to clear infection, particularly mass drug administration of the antibiotic azithromycin, which is donated by the manufacturer to national programmes through the International Trachoma Initiative; Facial cleanliness; and Environmental improvement, particularly better access to water and sanitation.
translations.reference: Trakoma inaweza kuondolewa kama tatizo la afya ya umma kwa kutumia mkakati SALAMA, unaojumuisha: Upasuaji wa kutibu trichiasis ya trachomatous, hatua ya upofu ya ugonjwa huo; Dawa za antibiotiki za kuondoa maambukizi, hasa usimamizi wa dawa nyingi za antiniotiki azithromycin, ambayo hutolewa na mtengenezaji kwa programu za kitaifa kupitia Mpango wa Kimataifa wa Trakoma; usafi wa uso; na Uboreshaji wa mazingira, hasa upatikanaji bora wa maji na usafi wa mazingira.
translations.hypothesis: Ugonjwa wa trachoma unaweza kuondolewa kama tatizo la afya ya umma kwa kutumia mbinu ya SAFE, ambayo inajumuisha: upasuaji wa kutibu trichiasis ya trachoma, hatua ya ugonjwa ambayo husababisha upofu; matibabu ya dawa za kuzuia maambukizi, hasa utoaji wa dawa ya azithromycin kwa idadi kubwa ya watu, ambayo hupewa na kampuni ya utengenezaji kwa programu za kitaifa kupitia Mpango wa Kimataifa wa Trachoma; usafi wa uso; na uboreshaji wa mazingira, hasa kupata maji safi na huduma bora za usafi.

translations.source: WHO adopted the SAFE strategy in 1993 and continues in its mandate to provide leadership and coordination of international efforts to eliminate trachoma as a public health problem.
translations.reference: WHO ilipitisha mkakati wa SALAMA mnamo 1993 na inaendelea katika jukumu lake la kutoa uongozi na uratibu wa juhudi za kimataifa za kuondoa trakoma kama shida ya afya ya umma.
translations.hypothesis: Shirika la Afya Duniani (WHO) liliunga mkono mkakati wa SAFE mwaka wa 1993, na bado linaendelea na jukumu lake la kuongoza na kuratibu juhudi za kimataifa za kuondoa ugonjwa wa trachoma kama tatizo la afya ya umma.

translations.source: Traditional, Complementary and Integrative Medicine Overview: Traditional medicine Traditional medicine has a long history.
