It is the sum total of the knowledge, skill, and practices based on the theories, beliefs, and experiences indigenous to different cultures, whether explicable or not, used in the maintenance of health as well as in the prevention, diagnosis, improvement or treatment of physical and mental illness. Complementary medicine
translations.reference: Dawa ya asili, Nyongeza na Shirikishi Muhtasari: Dawa ya jadi Dawa ya jadi ina historia ndefu. Ni jumla ya maarifa, ustadi, na mazoea kulingana na nadharia, imani, na uzoefu wa asili wa tamaduni tofauti, iwe ya kuelezeka au la, inayotumika katika kudumisha afya na vile vile katika kuzuia, utambuzi, uboreshaji au matibabu ya magonjwa ya kimwili na kiakili. Dawa ya ziada
translations.hypothesis: Mwangaza wa Matibabu ya Jadi, Mbadala, na Jumuishi: Matibabu ya Jadi. Matibabu ya jadi ina historia ndefu. Ni jumla ya maarifa, ujuzi, na mbinu ambazo zinatokana na nadharia, imani, na uzoefu wa tamaduni mbalimbali, iwe zile ambazo zinaweza kuelezeka au la, na ambazo hutumika katika kudumisha afya, pamoja na katika kuzuia, kutambua, kuboresha, au kutibu magonjwa ya kimwili na ya akili. Matibabu ya mbadala.

translations.source: The terms “complementary medicine” or “alternative medicine” refer to a broad set of health care practices that are not part of that country’s own tradition or conventional medicine and are not fully integrated into the dominant health-care system. They are used interchangeably with traditional medicine in some countries. Herbal medicines Herbal medicines include herbs, herbal materials, herbal preparations and finished herbal products, that contain as active ingredients parts of plants, or other plant materials, or combinations. Impact: The WHO Traditional Medicine Strategy 2014–2023 was developed and launched in response to the World Health Assembly resolution on traditional medicine (WHA62.13).
translations.reference: Maneno "dawa ya ziada" au "dawa mbadala" yanarejelea seti pana ya mazoea ya utunzaji wa afya ambayo si sehemu ya mila ya nchi hiyo au tiba ya kawaida na haijajumuishwa kikamilifu katika mfumo mkuu wa huduma ya afya. Zinatumika kwa kubadilishana na dawa za jadi katika nchi zingine. Dawa za mitishamba Dawa za mitishamba ni pamoja na mimea, mimea, maandalizi ya mitishamba na bidhaa za mitishamba zilizomalizika, ambazo zina viungo hai vya sehemu za mimea, au vitu vingine vya mimea, au mchanganyiko. Athari: Mkakati wa Tiba Asilia wa WHO 2014–2023 ulitayarishwa na kuzinduliwa kwa kuitikia azimio la Bunge la Afya Ulimwenguni kuhusu tiba asilia (WHA62.13).
translations.hypothesis: Maneno "tiba mbadala" au "tiba ya asili" yanarejelea aina pana ya mbinu za utunzaji wa afya ambazo hazijajumuishwa katika mila ya nchi husika au tiba ya kawaida, na ambazo hazijajumuishwa kikamilifu katika mfumo mkuu wa utunzaji wa afya. Katika baadhi ya nchi, maneno haya hutumika kwa pamoja na "tiba ya jadi." Tiba za mimea: Tiba za mimea hujumuisha mimea, vifaa vya mimea, maandalizi ya mimea, na bidhaa zilizokamilishwa za mimea, ambazo zina sehemu za mimea, au nyenzo nyingine za mimea, au mchanganyiko kama viungo vinavyotumika. Athari: Mkakati wa Shirika la Afya Duniani (WHO) kuhusu Tiba ya Jadi kwa mwaka wa 2014–2023 uliundwa na kuzinduliwa kama jibu kwa azimio la Mkutano Mkuu wa Afya Duniani kuhusu tiba ya jadi (WHA62.13).

translations.source: The strategy aims to support Member States in developing proactive policies and implementing action plans that will strengthen the role traditional medicine plays in keeping populations healthy. Addressing the challenges, responding to the needs identified by Member States and building on the work done under the WHO traditional medicine strategy: 2002–2005, the updated strategy for the period 2014–2023 devotes more attention than its predecessor to prioritizing health services and systems, including traditional and complementary medicine products, practices and practitioners. The strategic objectives are: To build the knowledge base for active management of T&CM through appropriate national policies; To strengthen the quality assurance, safety, proper use and effectiveness of T&CM by regulating products, practices and practitioners;
translations.reference: Mkakati huo unalenga kusaidia Nchi Wanachama katika kuunda sera tendaji na kutekeleza mipango ya utekelezaji ambayo itaimarisha jukumu la tiba asilia katika kuweka idadi ya watu kuwa na afya bora. Kushughulikia changamoto, kukabiliana na mahitaji yaliyoainishwa na Nchi Wanachama na kuendeleza kazi iliyofanywa chini ya mkakati wa tiba asilia wa WHO: 2002-2005, mkakati uliosasishwa wa kipindi cha 2014-2023 unatoa kipaumbele zaidi kuliko mtangulizi wake katika kuweka kipaumbele huduma za afya na mifumo, ikiwa ni pamoja na bidhaa za tiba asilia na nyongeza, mazoea na watendaji. Malengo ya kimkakati ni: Kujenga msingi wa maarifa kwa usimamizi hai wa T&CM kupitia sera zinazofaa za kitaifa; Kuimarisha uhakikisho wa ubora, usalama, matumizi sahihi na ufanisi wa T&CM kwa kudhibiti bidhaa, mazoea na watendaji;
translations.hypothesis: Mkakati huu unalenga kuunga mkono nchi wanachama katika kuendeleza sera za maendeleo na kutekeleza mipango ya hatua ambayo itaimarisha jukumu la dawa za jadi katika kuwezesha afya ya watu. Akizingatia changamoto, kutoa majibu kwa mahitaji yaliyotambulika na nchi wanachama, na kujenga juu ya kazi iliyofanywa katika mkakati wa dawa za jadi wa WHO wa mwaka 2002–2005, mkakati huu mpya wa kipindi cha 2014–2023 unatoa umakini zaidi kuliko ule uliopita katika kuweka kipaumbele huduma na mifumo ya afya, ikiwa ni pamoja na bidhaa, mbinu na wataalamu wa dawa za jadi na za ziada. Malengo ya kimkakati ni: Kuunda msingi wa maarifa kwa usimamizi madhubuti wa dawa za jadi na za ziada kupitia sera za kitaifa zinazofaa; Kuimarisha ubora, usalama, matumizi sahihi na ufanisi wa dawa za jadi na za ziada kwa kusimamia bidhaa, mbinu na wataalamu.

translations.source: To promote universal health coverage by integrating T&CM services into health care service delivery and self-health care. WHO response: The WHO global report on traditional and complementary medicine 2019 was developed to address the gap in reliable, credible and official data from Member States in the area of T&CM. This report reviews global progress in T&CM over the past two decades and is based on contributions from 179 WHO Member States. It provides valuable information for policy-makers, health professionals and the public for capitalizing on the potential contribution of T&CM to health and well-being.
translations.reference: Kukuza huduma ya afya kwa wote kwa kuunganisha huduma za T&CM katika utoaji wa huduma za afya na kujihudumia. Jibu la WHO: Ripoti ya kimataifa ya WHO kuhusu tiba asilia na nyongeza ya 2019 ilitengenezwa ili kushughulikia pengo la data za kuaminika, zinazoaminika na rasmi kutoka kwa Nchi Wanachama katika eneo la T&CM. Ripoti hii hukagua maendeleo ya kimataifa katika T&CM katika kipindi cha miongo miwili iliyopita na inategemea michango kutoka Nchi 179 Wanachama wa WHO. Inatoa taarifa muhimu kwa watunga sera, wataalamu wa afya na umma kwa kutumia mchango unaowezekana wa T&CM kwa afya na ustawi.
translations.hypothesis: Ili kukuza utoaji wa huduma za afya kwa kila mtu, huduma za tiba na matibabu ya jadi na mbadala zinapaswa kuunganishwa katika utoaji wa huduma za afya na katika utunzaji wa afya binafsi. Jibu la WHO: Ripoti ya kimataifa ya WHO kuhusu tiba na matibabu ya jadi na mbadala ya mwaka 2019 ilitayarishwa ili kujibu pengo la data ya kuaminika, ya kweli, na rasmi kutoka kwa nchi wanachama katika eneo la tiba na matibabu ya jadi na mbadala. Ripoti hii inatoa tathmini ya maendeleo ya kimataifa katika tiba na matibabu ya jadi na mbadala katika kipindi cha miaka miwili iliyopita, na imetokana na michango kutoka kwa nchi wanachama 179 za WHO. Inatoa taarifa muhimu kwa watunga sera, wataalamu wa afya, na umma kwa ajili ya kutumia uwezo wa tiba na matibabu ya jadi na mbadala katika kuimarisha afya na ustawi.

translations.source: Transplantation Overview: Transplantation of human cells, tissues and organs Transplantation of human cells, tissues or organs saves many lives and restores essential functions where no alternatives of comparable effectiveness exist. In 50 years, transplantation has become a successful worldwide practice. However, there are large differences between countries in access to suitable transplantation and in the level of safety, quality, efficacy of donation and transplantation of human cells, tissues and organs.
translations.reference: Kupandikiza Muhtasari: Kupandikiza seli za binadamu, tishu na viungo Kupandikiza seli za binadamu, tishu au viungo huokoa maisha ya watu wengi na kurejesha kazi muhimu ambapo hakuna njia mbadala za ufanisi kulinganishwa zilizopo. Katika miaka 50, upandikizaji umekuwa jambo la mafanikio duniani kote. Hata hivyo, kuna tofauti kubwa kati ya nchi katika upatikanaji wa upandikizaji unaofaa na katika kiwango cha usalama, ubora, ufanisi wa mchango na upandikizaji wa seli za binadamu, tishu na viungo.
translations.hypothesis: Mwangazaji wa Upandikizaji: Upandikizaji wa seli, tishu na viungo vya binadamu. Upandikizaji wa seli, tishu au viungo vya binadamu unaokoa maisha mengi na unaendeleza utendaji muhimu ambapo hakuna njia nyingine yenye ufanisi sawa. Katika miaka 50, upandikizaji umekuwa mchakato unaofanikiwa duniani kote. Hata hivyo, kuna tofauti kubwa kati ya nchi katika upatikanaji wa upandikizaji unaofaa, na pia katika kiwango cha usalama, ubora, na ufanisi wa kuchangia na upandikizaji wa seli, tishu na viungo vya binadamu.

translations.source: The ethical aspects of transplantation are at the forefront. In particular, the unmet patients’ needs and the shortage of transplants lead to the temptation of trafficking in human body components for transplantation. Human transplantation Human cells and tissues for transplantation can save lives or restore essential functions. For examplea corneal graft can restore sight in corneal blindness;
translations.reference: Vipengele vya maadili vya upandikizaji viko mbele. Hasa, mahitaji ya wagonjwa ambayo hayajafikiwa na uhaba wa vipandikizi husababisha jaribu la usafirishaji wa viungo vya mwili wa binadamu kwa ajili ya upandikizaji. Kupandikiza binadamu Seli za binadamu na tishu za kupandikiza zinaweza kuokoa maisha au kurejesha kazi muhimu. Kwa mfano pandikizi la corneal linaweza kurejesha kuona kwa upofu wa corneal;
translations.hypothesis: Masuala ya maadili yanayohusiana na utoaji wa viungo vya mwili kwa ajili ya upandikizaji yamekuwa muhimu sana. Hasa, uhitaji usiofikia wa wagonjwa na upungufu wa viungo vinavyoweza kutumika kwa upandikizaji husababisha tamaa ya biashara haramu ya viungo vya mwili vya binadamu kwa ajili ya upandikizaji. Upandikizaji wa viungo vya binadamu: Seli na tishu za binadamu ambazo hutumika kwa upandikizaji zinaweza kuokoa maisha au kurejesha utendaji muhimu. Kwa mfano, upandikizaji wa cornea unaweza kurejesha uwezo wa kuona kwa wagonjwa ambao wamepoteza uwezo wao wa kuona kutokana na tatizo la cornea.

translations.source: the transplantation of haematopoietic stem cells can cure congenital or acquired diseases including some leukaemias See further information on Haematopoeitic Stem Cells the transplantation of a human heart valve often constitutes the best replacement situation and recipient patients do not require long term anti-coagulation therapy. Xenotransplantation Xenotransplantation, animal to human, defined as living cells, tissues or organs of animal origin and human body fluids, cells, tissues or organs that have ex vivo with these living, xenogeneic materials, has the potential to constitute an alternative to material of human origin and bridge the shortfall in human material for transplantation.
translations.reference: upandikizaji wa seli za shina za hematopoietic unaweza kuponya magonjwa ya kuzaliwa au kupatikana ikiwa ni pamoja na baadhi ya leukemia. Tazama habari zaidi juu ya Seli za Shina za Hematopoeitic upandikizaji wa vali ya moyo wa binadamu mara nyingi hujumuisha hali bora ya uingizwaji na wagonjwa wanaopokea hawahitaji tiba ya muda mrefu ya kuzuia mgando. Xenotransplantation Xenotransplantation, mnyama kwa binadamu, inayofafanuliwa kama chembe hai, tishu au viungo vya asili ya wanyama na maji maji ya mwili wa binadamu, seli, tishu au viungo ambavyo vina ex vivo na nyenzo hizi hai, xenogeneic, ina uwezo wa kuunda nyenzo mbadala ya asili ya mwanadamu na kuziba upungufu katika nyenzo za binadamu kwa ajili ya upandikizaji.
translations.hypothesis: Upandaji wa seli za msingi za damu unaweza kutibu magonjwa ya kuzaliwa au yale ambayo yanapata mtu, ikiwa ni pamoja na saratani fulani za damu. Angalia maelezo zaidi kuhusu seli za msingi za damu. Upandaji wa kofia ya moyo ya binadamu mara nyingi huleta matokeo bora, na wagonjwa wanaopokea upasuaji huyu hawahitaji tiba ya muda mrefu ya kuzuia kuganda kwa damu. Upandaji wa viungo kutoka kwa wanyama (Xenotransplantation): Xenotransplantation, ambayo ni upandaji wa seli, tishu, au viungo kutoka kwa wanyama kwa binadamu, inahusu seli, tishu, au viungo vya binadamu ambavyo vimechanganywa na nyenzo hizo za wanyama nje ya mwili. Mbinu hii ina uwezo wa kuwa mbadala kwa nyenzo za binadamu na kuongeza upatikanaji wa nyenzo za binadamu zinazohitajika kwa upandaji.

translations.source: Donation: Donations from deceased persons should be developed to their maximum therapeutic potential avoiding, whenever possible, the inherent risks to live donors, as stated in WHO Guiding Principle 3. There is a recognized need for communities, and health professionals, to become better educated about donation and transplantation and that is the key to the success of deceased donation programmes. However, despite the frequent use of materials donated from deceased donors, the donations from living donors are necessary for some types of transplants or to compensate for the limited supply of material available from deceased donors in order to meet patient needs. Living donation is thus practised despite the fact that it involves risks for the donor that may not be negligible. As the procurement of human material for transplantation from deceased or living donors and the subsequent allogeneic transplantation may entail ethical and safety risks for both the recipient and the donor, strict controls and effective oversight should be carried out by the health authorities to protect them.
translations.reference: Uchangiaji: Michango kutoka kwa watu walioaga dunia inapaswa kuendelezwa kwa uwezo wao wa juu zaidi wa kimatibabu ili kuepuka, inapowezekana, hatari za asili kwa wafadhili hai, kama ilivyoelezwa katika Kanuni ya 3 ya Mwongozo wa WHO. Kuna hitaji linalotambulika kwa jamii, na wataalamu wa afya, kuelimishwa vyema kuhusu uchangiaji na upandikizaji na huo ndio ufunguo wa mafanikio ya mipango ya uchangiaji wa marehemu. Hata hivyo, licha ya matumizi ya mara kwa mara ya vifaa vinavyotolewa na wafadhili waliofariki, michango kutoka kwa wafadhili wanaoishi ni muhimu kwa baadhi ya aina za upandikizaji au kufidia ugavi mdogo wa nyenzo zinazopatikana kutoka kwa wafadhili waliofariki ili kukidhi mahitaji ya mgonjwa. Utoaji hai unatekelezwa licha ya ukweli kwamba unahusisha hatari kwa wafadhili ambazo haziwezi kupuuzwa. Kwa vile ununuzi wa nyenzo za binadamu kwa ajili ya kupandikiza kutoka kwa wafadhili waliokufa au wanaoishi na upandikizaji wa alojeneki unaofuata unaweza kuhusisha hatari za kimaadili na kiusalama kwa mpokeaji na mfadhili, udhibiti mkali na uangalizi unaofaa unafaa kutekelezwa na mamlaka za afya ili kuwalinda.
translations.hypothesis: Michango: Michango kutoka kwa watu waliofariki inapaswa kutumika kikamilifu ili kuleta manufaa ya matibabu, na kuepuka, kadiri inawezekana, hatari zinazoweza kuhatarisha afya ya watu wanaotoa michango wakiwa hai, kama ilivyoelezwa katika Kanuni Nambari 3 ya Shirika la Afya Duniani (WHO). Kuna uhitaji mkubwa wa jamii na wataalamu wa afya kuwa na elimu bora kuhusu michango na utoaji wa viungo, na hiyo ndiyo ufunguo wa mafanikio ya programu za michango kutoka kwa watu waliofariki. Hata hivyo, licha ya matumizi ya mara kwa mara ya vifaa vilivyotolewa kutoka kwa watu waliofariki, michango kutoka kwa watu wanaoishi ni muhimu kwa aina fulani za utoaji wa viungo, au ili kukidhi upungufu wa vifaa vinavyopatikana kutoka kwa watu waliofariki, ili kukidhi mahitaji ya wagonjwa. Kwa hivyo, utoaji wa michango kutoka kwa watu wanaoishi unaendelea kufanywa, licha ya ukweli kwamba una hatari kwa mtu anayeitoa michango, hatari ambazo zinaweza kuwa kubwa. Kwa kuwa ukusanyaji wa vifaa vya binadamu kwa ajili ya utoaji wa viungo kutoka kwa watu waliofariki au wanaoishi, na utoaji wa viungo huo baadaye, unaweza kuleta hatari za kimaadili na za usalama kwa mpokeaji na mtu anayeitoa michango, ni muhimu kwamba mamlaka za afya zifuate taratibu kali na usimamizi bora ili kuwalinda.

translations.source: The Guiding Principles mandate optimal care for donors and recipients. The transparent oversight of the health authorities over donation and transplantation activities is also essential to increase the trust of the public in the system. In addition, the decision to be a donor is often based on the understanding that a contribution to the availability of transplant resources may someday benefit the health needs of the donor’s family. If we are prepared to receive a transplant should we need one, then we should be ready to give Ethics: WHO Guiding Principles on Human Cell, Tissue and Organ Transplantation
translations.reference: Kanuni Elekezi huamuru utunzaji bora kwa wafadhili na wapokeaji. Uangalizi wa uwazi wa mamlaka za afya juu ya shughuli za mchango na upandikizaji pia ni muhimu ili kuongeza imani ya umma katika mfumo. Kwa kuongezea, uamuzi wa kuwa mfadhili mara nyingi hutegemea ufahamu kwamba mchango katika upatikanaji wa rasilimali za upandikizaji unaweza kufaidisha mahitaji ya afya ya familia ya mfadhili siku moja. Ikiwa tumejiandaa kupokea upandikizaji tukihitaji, basi tunapaswa kuwa tayari kutoa Maadili: Kanuni Mwongozo wa WHO kuhusu Seli ya Binadamu, Tishu na Upandikizaji wa Kiungo
translations.hypothesis: Kanuni za msingi zinahitaji utunzaji bora kwa wafadhili na wapokeaji. Ufuatiliaji wa wazi wa mamlaka za afya kuhusu shughuli za utoaji na upandikizaji pia ni muhimu ili kuongeza imani ya umma katika mfumo huo. Zaidi ya hayo, uamuzi wa kuwa mfadhili mara nyingi hutokana na ufahamu kwamba mchango katika upatikanaji wa rasilimali za upandikizaji unaweza, siku moja, kunufaisha mahitaji ya afya ya familia ya mfadhili. Ikiwa sisi ni tayari kupokea upandikizaji ikiwa tunahitaji, basi tunapaswa kuwa tayari kutoa. Ethics: Kanuni za Msingi za WHO kuhusu Upandikizaji wa Seli, Tishu na Viungo vya Binadamu.

translations.source: In 1987 the fortieth World Health Assembly, concerned at the trade for profit in human organs, initiated the preparation of the first WHO Guiding Principles on Transplantation, endorsed by the Assembly in 1991 in resolution WHA44.25. These Guiding Principles have greatly influenced professional codes and practices as well as legislation around the world during almost two decades. After a consultation process that took several years, the Sixty-third World Health Assembly adopted resolution WHA63.22 on 21 May 2010, endorsing the updated WHO Guiding Principles and identifying areas of progress to optimize donation and transplantation practices. The Declaration of Istanbul Professionals of donation and transplantation from all regions, through many of their organizations and institutions, are endorsing the Declaration of Istanbul on organ trafficking and transplant tourism developed under the leadership of the Transplantation Society and the International Society of Nephrology in May 2008.
translations.reference: Mnamo 1987 Mkutano wa Arobaini wa Afya Ulimwenguni, unaohusika na biashara ya faida katika viungo vya binadamu, ulianzisha utayarishaji wa Kanuni za Kwanza za Mwongozo wa WHO juu ya Uhamishaji, iliyoidhinishwa na Bunge mnamo 1991 katika azimio la WHA44.25. Kanuni hizi Elekezi zimeathiri pakubwa kanuni na taratibu za kitaaluma pamoja na sheria kote ulimwenguni takriban miongo miwili. Baada ya mchakato wa mashauriano uliochukua miaka kadhaa, Bunge la Sitini na tatu la Afya Duniani lilipitisha azimio WHA63.22 tarehe 21 Mei 2010, likiidhinisha Kanuni za Mwongozo wa WHO zilizosasishwa na kubainisha maeneo ya maendeleo ili kuboresha uchangiaji na upandikizaji. Azimio la Istanbul Wataalamu wa mchango na upandikizaji kutoka mikoa yote, kupitia mashirika na taasisi zao nyingi, wanaidhinisha Azimio la Istanbul kuhusu usafirishaji haramu wa viungo na utalii wa kupandikiza ulioendelezwa chini ya uongozi wa Jumuiya ya Upandikizaji na Jumuiya ya Kimataifa ya Nephrology mnamo Mei 2008.
translations.hypothesis: Mnamo mwaka wa 1987, Mkutano wa 40 wa Shirika la Afya Duniani (WHO), ukiwa na wasiwasi kuhusu biashara ya viungo vya binadamu kwa faida, ulianza mchakato wa kuandaa Kanuni za Kiongozi za kwanza za WHO kuhusu upandikizaji, ambazo ziliidhinishwa na mkutano huo mwaka wa 1991 kupitia azimio la WHA44.25. Kanuni hizi zimekuwa na ushawishi mkubwa katika kanuni na mbinu za kitaalamu, pamoja na sheria duniani kote, kwa takriban miaka miwili. Baada ya mchakato wa mashauriano ambao ulichukua miaka kadhaa, Mkutano wa 63 wa Shirika la Afya Duniani ulipitisha azimio la WHA63.22 mnamo Mei 21, 2010, likiidhinisha Kanuni za Kiongozi zilizosasishwa za WHO na kutambua maeneo ya maendeleo ili kuboresha mbinu za utoaji na upandikizaji. Wataalamu wa utoaji na upandikizaji kutoka kanda zote, kupitia mashirika na taasisi zao nyingi, wanakiunga mkono Azimio la Istanbul kuhusu biashara haramu ya viungo na utalii wa upandikizaji, ambalo lilitengenezwa chini ya uongo wa Jumuiya ya Upandikizaji na Jumuiya ya Kimataifa ya Nephrology mnamo Mei 2008.

translations.source: Travel and health Overview: International travel can pose various risks to health, depending on the characteristics of both the traveller and the travel. Travellers may encounter sudden and significant changes in altitude, humidity, microbes, and temperature, which can result in ill-health. In addition, serious health risks may arise in areas where accommodation is of poor quality, hygiene and sanitation are inadequate, medical services are not well developed and clean water is unavailable. International travel is on the rise, as millions of people travel for professional, social, recreational and humanitarian purposes each year.
translations.reference: Usafiri na afya Muhtasari: Usafiri wa kimataifa unaweza kusababisha hatari mbalimbali kwa afya, kulingana na sifa za msafiri na wasafiri. Wasafiri wanaweza kukutana na mabadiliko ya ghafla na makubwa katika urefu, unyevu, vijidudu, na joto, ambayo inaweza kusababisha afya mbaya. Aidha, hatari kubwa za kiafya zinaweza kutokea katika maeneo ambayo malazi hayana ubora, usafi na usafi wa mazingira hautoshi, huduma za matibabu hazijaendelezwa vizuri na maji safi hayapatikani. Usafiri wa kimataifa unaongezeka, kwani mamilioni ya watu husafiri kwa madhumuni ya kitaaluma, kijamii, burudani na kibinadamu kila mwaka.
translations.hypothesis: Safari na afya: Usafiri wa kimataifa unaweza kuleta hatari mbalimbali za kiafya, kulingana na tabia za msafiri na pia eneo la safari. Wasafiri wanaweza kukumbana na mabadiliko ya ghafla na makubwa katika urefu, unyevu, vijidudu, na joto, ambayo yanaweza kusababisha magonjwa. Zaidi ya hayo, hatari kubwa za kiafya zinaweza kutokea katika maeneo ambapo malazi ni ya ubora duni, usafi na usafi ni hafifu, huduma za matibabu hazijakamilika, na maji safi hayapatikani. Usafiri wa kimataifa unaongezeka, kwani mamilioni ya watu husafiri kila mwaka kwa sababu za kitaaluma, kijamii, za burudani, na za kusaidia.

translations.source: All travellers must prepare for the variety of health risks they can be exposed to in unfamiliar environments before, during and after they travel. People planning to travel should seek advice on the potential hazards in their chosen destinations and understand how best to protect their health and minimize the risk of acquiring disease. Forward planning, appropriate preventive measures and careful precautions can protect the health of travellers, and minimize the risks of accident and of acquiring disease. Although the medical profession and the travel industry can provide extensive help and sound advice, it remains the traveller’s responsibility to seek information, to understand the risks involved and to take the necessary precautions to protect their health while travelling. Risk: When determining the health risks of international travel, travellers and health professionals should consider the following key factors:
translations.reference: Wasafiri wote lazima wajitayarishe kwa aina mbalimbali za hatari za kiafya wanazoweza kukabiliwa nazo katika mazingira yasiyofahamika kabla, wakati na baada ya kusafiri. Watu wanaopanga kusafiri wanapaswa kutafuta ushauri kuhusu hatari zinazoweza kutokea katika maeneo waliyochagua na kuelewa jinsi bora ya kulinda afya zao na kupunguza hatari ya kupata magonjwa. Kupanga mbele, hatua zinazofaa za kuzuia na tahadhari makini zinaweza kulinda afya ya wasafiri, na kupunguza hatari za ajali na kupata magonjwa. Ingawa taaluma ya matibabu na sekta ya usafiri inaweza kutoa usaidizi wa kina na ushauri mzuri, inasalia kuwa jukumu la msafiri kutafuta maelezo, kuelewa hatari zinazohusika na kuchukua tahadhari zinazohitajika ili kulinda afya zao wakati wa kusafiri. Hatari: Wakati wa kubainisha hatari za kiafya za usafiri wa kimataifa, wasafiri na wataalamu wa afya wanapaswa kuzingatia mambo muhimu yafuatayo:
translations.hypothesis: Watembea wote lazima wajiandae kwa hatari mbalimbali za kiafya ambazo wanaweza kukumbana nazo katika maeneo yasiyo ya kawaida, kabla, wakati na baada ya kusafiri. Watu wanaopanga kusafiri wanapaswa kutafuta ushauri kuhusu hatari zinazoweza kutokea katika maeneo wanayopanga kwenda, na kuelewa jinsi bora ya kulinda afya yao na kupunguza hatari ya kuugua magonjwa. Kupanga mapema, kuchukua hatua za kuzuia zinazofaa, na kuwa makini katika hatua zote kunaweza kulinda afya ya wasafiri, na kupunguza hatari za ajali na magonjwa. Ingawa wataalamu wa matibabu na sekta ya usafiri wanaweza kutoa msaada na ushauri muhimu, bado ni jukumu la mtembea kutafuta taarifa, kuelewa hatari zinazohusika, na kuchukua hatua muhimu za kulinda afya yao wakati wa kusafiri. Hatari: Wakati wa kutambua hatari za kiafya za usafiri wa kimataifa, wasafiri na wataalamu wa afya wanapaswa kuzingatia mambo muhimu yafuatayo:

translations.source: mode of transport destination(s) duration and season of travel purpose of travel standards of accommodation, food hygiene and sanitation
translations.reference: mfumo wa usafiri Mwisho(hatima) muda na msimu wa safari madhumuni ya kusafiri viwango vya malazi, usafi wa chakula na usafi wa mazingira
translations.hypothesis: namna ya usafiri, mahali (mahalali) pa kwenda, muda na msimu wa safari, sababu ya safari, viwango vya malazi, usafi wa chakula na usafi wa mazingira.

translations.source: behaviour of the traveller underlying health of the traveller Travellers may encounter the following health risks depending on their destination: animal and insect bites, including mosquitoes, ticks, fleas, and some flies, which can spread diseases such as malaria exposure to blood or other bodily fluids
translations.reference: tabia ya msafiri afya ya msingi ya msafiri Wasafiri wanaweza kukumbana na hatari zifuatazo za kiafya kulingana na hatima yao: kuumwa na wanyama na wadudu wakiwemo mbu, kupe, viroboto na baadhi ya nzi ambao wanaweza kueneza magonjwa kama malaria. yatokanayo na damu au maji maji mengine ya mwili
translations.hypothesis:
tabia ya msafiri, afya ya msafiri.

Msafiri anaweza kukumbana na hatari zifuatazo za kiafya, kulingana na mahali anakoenda:
*   Mishipa na uchungu kutoka kwa wanyama na wadudu, ikiwa ni pamoja na mbu, vidudu, vidonda, na aina fulani za nzi, ambazo zinaweza kusambaza magonjwa kama vile malaria.
*   Kuwasiliana na damu au vimiminika vingine vya mwili.

translations.source: environmental changes, such as altitude, humidity, temperature foodborne and waterborne diseases inadequate medical services injuries, including from road traffic accidents or recreational activities psychological health, including stress and culture shock
translations.reference: mabadiliko ya mazingira, kama vile urefu, unyevu, joto magonjwa yatokanayo na chakula na maji huduma duni za matibabu majeraha, ikiwa ni pamoja na ajali za barabarani au shughuli za burudani afya ya kisaikolojia, pamoja na mafadhaiko na mshtuko wa kitamaduni
translations.hypothesis: mabadiliko ya mazingira, kama vile urefu, unyevu, na joto; magonjwa yanayosababishwa na chakula na maji; huduma za matibabu zisizo za kutosha; majeraha, ikiwa ni pamoja na yale yanayosababishwa na ajali za barabarani au shughuli za burudani; afya ya akili, ikiwa ni pamoja na msongo wa mawazo na hali ya kutofaa na mazingira mapya; na utamaduni.

translations.source: Travellers may also be exposed to a number of infectious diseases, and many of these can be prevented by vaccination. However, there are some infectious diseases, including some of the most dangerous, for which no vaccines exist. Destinations where accommodation, hygiene and sanitation, medical care and water quality are of a high standard pose relatively few serious risks to the health of travellers. Where these services are not provided, travellers should take stringent precautions to avoid illness. Travel warnings from governmental sources should be taken seriously.
translations.reference: Wasafiri wanaweza pia kukabiliwa na magonjwa kadhaa ya kuambukiza, na mengi ya haya yanaweza kuzuiwa kwa chanjo. Hata hivyo, kuna baadhi ya magonjwa ya kuambukiza, ikiwa ni pamoja na baadhi ya hatari zaidi, ambayo hakuna chanjo zilizopo. Maeneo ambayo malazi, usafi na usafi wa mazingira, huduma ya matibabu na ubora wa maji ni wa kiwango cha juu husababisha hatari chache kwa afya ya wasafiri. Pale ambapo huduma hizi hazitolewi, wasafiri wanapaswa kuchukua tahadhari kali ili kuepuka maradhi. Maonyo ya usafiri kutoka kwa vyanzo vya serikali yanapaswa kuchukuliwa kwa uzito.
translations.hypothesis: Watalii wanaweza pia kukumbana na magonjwa mengiambukizi, na mengi ya magonjwa haya yanaweza kuzuiwa kupitia chanjo. Hata hivyo, kuna magonjwa mengiambukizi, yakiwemo baadhi ya hatari zaidi, ambayo hakuna chanjo inayopatikana. Maeneo ambapo huduma za malazi, usafi na usafi wa mazingira, huduma za matibabu, na ubora wa maji ni wa hali ya juu, huleta hatari ndogo sana kwa afya ya watalii. Pale ambapo huduma hizi hazipatikani, watalii wanapaswa kuchukua tahadhari kali ili kuepuka ugonjwa. Tahadhari za usafiri zinazotolewa na serikali zinapaswa kuchukuliwa kwa umakini.

translations.source: Prevention: Before travel: contact a travel medicine centre or a physician, preferably 4–8 weeks before departure to receive any required vaccinations; read up on local diseases related to your destination; request information on malaria risk and prevention of mosquito bites. This may include purchasing appropriate preventive medication and packing a bednet and insect repellent; get a medical and dental check-up;
translations.reference: Kinga: Kabla ya kusafiri: wasiliana na kituo cha dawa za usafiri au daktari, ikiwezekana wiki 4-8 kabla ya kuondoka ili kupokea chanjo zinazohitajika; soma kuhusu magonjwa ya eneo lako yanayohusiana na unakoenda; omba taarifa kuhusu hatari ya malaria na uzuiaji wa kuumwa na mbu. Hii inaweza kujumuisha kununua dawa zinazofaa za kuzuia na kufunga chandarua na dawa ya kufukuza wadudu; pata uchunguzi wa matibabu na meno;
translations.hypothesis: Kuzuia: Kabla ya kusafiri: wasiliana na kituo cha tiba ya usafiri au daktari, hasa wiki 4-8 kabla ya kuondoka, ili upate chanjo zozote zinazohitajika; soma kuhusu magonjwa ya eneo unakoelekea; omba taarifa kuhusu hatari za malaria na jinsi ya kujikinga kutokana na uchomaji wa mbu. Hii inaweza kujumuisha kununua dawa za kuzuia na kubeba mamba na dawa ya kuua mbu; fanya uchunguzi wa afya na meno;

translations.source: obtain prescriptions for medication according to length of stay and assemble a suitable medical kit; purchase medical insurance with appropriate cover abroad During travel: eat only thoroughly cooked food and drink only bottled or packaged cold drinks. Boil drinking-water if safety is doubtful.
translations.reference: kupata maagizo ya dawa kulingana na urefu wa kukaa na kukusanya kit cha matibabu kinachofaa; nunua bima ya matibabu na bima inayofaa nje ya nchi Wakati wa kusafiri: kula tu chakula kilichopikwa vizuri na kunywa tu vinywaji baridi vya chupa au vifungashio. Chemsha maji ya kunywa ikiwa usalama ni wa shaka.
translations.hypothesis: Pata maagizo ya dawa kulingana na muda wa kukaa na uweke vifaa muhimu vya matibabu. Nunua bima ya afya inayofaa kwa matibabu ya nje. Wakati wa kusafiri: kula tu vyakula vilivyopikwa vizuri na kunywa tu vinywaji baridi vilivyofungashwa au vilivyokuwa kwenye chupa. Chemsha maji ya kunywa ikiwa usalama wake hauhakikishwi.

translations.source: be aware of accidents or problems related to traffic, animals, allergies, sun and sport; carry a card showing your blood group; wear a medical alert bracelet, if you have allergies or a chronic disease Travellers should obtain a medical examination on their return if they: return with a fever from a country where malaria is or may be present
translations.reference: kuwa na ufahamu wa ajali au matatizo yanayohusiana na trafiki, wanyama, mizio, jua na michezo; kubeba kadi inayoonyesha kundi lako la damu; kuvaa bangili ya tahadhari ya matibabu, ikiwa una mzio au ugonjwa sugu. Wasafiri wanapaswa kufanyiwa uchunguzi wa kimatibabu wanaporudi ikiwa: kurudi na homa kutoka nchi ambako malaria iko au inaweza kuwepo
translations.hypothesis: Hakikisha unafahamu hatari zinazohusiana na ajali za barabarani, wanyama, mzio, jua na shughuli za michezo; beba kadi inayoeleza kundi lako la damu; valia mkanda wa mkono unaoonyesha hali yako ya kiafya, ikiwa una mzio au ugonjwa sugu. Wasafiri wanapaswa kupata uchunguzi wa kiafya wanaporudi nyumbani ikiwa: wanarudi na homa kutoka nchi ambapo malaria ipo au inaweza kuwepo.

translations.source: suffer from a chronic disease experience illness in the weeks following their return home received treatment for malaria while travelling may have been exposed to a serious infectious disease spent more than 3 months in a developing country
translations.reference: kuteseka na ugonjwa sugu hupata ugonjwa kwa wiki zinazofuata baada ya kurudi nyumbani alipata matibabu ya malaria akiwa safarini inaweza kuwa wazi kwa ugonjwa mbaya wa kuambukiza alitumia zaidi ya miezi 3 katika nchi inayoendelea
translations.hypothesis:
*   Anaugua ugonjwa sugu.
*   Alipata ugonjwa katika wiki chache baada ya kurudi nyumbani.
*   Alipokea matibabu ya malaria wakati alipokuwa anasafiri.
*   Huenda alifichuliwa kwa ugonjwa hatari wa kuambukiza.
*   Alitumia zaidi ya miezi mitatu katika nchi lenye uchumi hafifu.

translations.source: Tuberculosis Overview: TB is caused by bacteria (Mycobacterium tuberculosis) and it most often affects the lungs. TB is spread through the air when people with lung TB cough, sneeze or spit. A person needs to inhale only a few germs to become infected. Every year, 10 million people fall ill with tuberculosis (TB).
translations.reference: Kifua kikuu Muhtasari: Kifua kikuu husababishwa na bakteria (Mycobacterium tuberculosis) na mara nyingi huathiri mapafu. Kifua kikuu huenezwa kwa njia ya hewa wakati watu wenye TB ya mapafu wanapokohoa, kupiga chafya au kutema mate. Mtu anahitaji kuvuta vijidudu vichache tu ili kuambukizwa. Kila mwaka, watu milioni 10 wanaugua kifua kikuu (TB).
translations.hypothesis: **Maelezo ya Kifupi kuhusu Ugonjwa wa Kifua Kikuu:** Kifua kikuu husababishwa na bakteria (Mycobacterium tuberculosis) na mara nyingi huathiri mapafu. Ugonjwa huu huenezwa kupitia hewa wakati watu walio na kifua kikuu kwenye mapafu wanapokosa, kupiga mkono au kutaga. Mtu anahitaji tu kuvuta bakteria wachache ili kuambukizwa. Kila mwaka, watu milioni 10 husumbuliwa na ugonjwa wa kifua kikuu.

translations.source: Despite being a preventable and curable disease, 1.5 million people die from TB each year – making it the world’s top infectious killer. TB is the leading cause of death of people with HIV and also a major contributor to antimicrobial resistance Most of the people who fall ill with TB live in low- and middle-income countries, but TB is present all over the world. About half of all people with TB can be found in 8 countries: Bangladesh, China, India, Indonesia, Nigeria, Pakistan, Philippines and South Africa. About a quarter of the global population is estimated to have been infected with TB bacteria, but most people will not go on to develop TB disease and some will clear the infection.
translations.reference: Licha ya kuwa ni ugonjwa unaozuilika na unaotibika, watu milioni 1.5 hufa kutokana na TB kila mwaka - na kuifanya kuwa muuaji mkuu wa kuambukiza duniani. Kifua kikuu ndicho kisababishi kikuu cha vifo vya watu walio na VVU na pia mchangiaji mkubwa katika ukinzani wa viua viini Watu wengi wanaougua TB wanaishi katika nchi zenye kipato cha chini na cha kati, lakini TB ipo duniani kote. Takriban nusu ya watu wote walio na TB wanaweza kupatikana katika nchi 8: Bangladesh, China, India, Indonesia, Nigeria, Pakistan, Ufilipino na Afrika Kusini. Takriban robo ya idadi ya watu duniani inakadiriwa kuwa wameambukizwa bakteria wa TB, lakini watu wengi hawataendelea kupata ugonjwa wa TB na wengine wataondoa maambukizi.
translations.hypothesis: Ingawa ugonjwa wa kifua kikuu (TB) unaweza kuzuiwa na kutibika, watu milioni 1.5 huaga dunia kutokana na ugonjwa huo kila mwaka, na hivyo kuwa sababu kuu ya vifo vinavyosababishwa na magonjwa ya kuambukiza duniani. Kifua kikuu ndio chanzo kikuu cha vifo kwa watu walio na virusi vya Ukimwi (HIV), na pia ni mchango mkubwa katika suala la upinzani wa dawa za kuzuia maambukizi. Watu wengi wanaokumbwa na kifua kikuu wanaishi katika nchi zilizo na kipato cha chini na cha kati, lakini ugonjwa huo upo katika maeneo yote duniani. Takriban nusu ya watu wote walio na kifua kikuu wanaweza kupatikana katika nchi 8: Bangladesh, China, India, Indonesia, Nigeria, Pakistan, Philippines, na Afrika Kusini. Inakadiriwa kwamba robo moja ya idadi ya watu duniani imekuwa na maambukizi ya bakteria ya kifua kikuu, lakini watu wengi hawataendelea kuugua ugonjwa huo, na wengine watatoka na maambukizi hayo.

translations.source: Those who are infected but not (yet) ill with the disease cannot transmit it. People infected with TB bacteria have a 5–10% lifetime risk of falling ill with TB. Those with compromised immune systems, such as people living with HIV, malnutrition or diabetes, or people who use tobacco, have a higher risk of falling ill. Symptoms: Common symptoms of TB disease include: Prolonged cough, Chest pain, Weakness or fatigue, Weight loss, Fever, Night sweats Often, these symptoms will be mild for many months, thus leading to delays in seeking care and increasing the risk of spreading the infection to others.
translations.reference: Wale ambao wameambukizwa lakini sio (bado) wagonjwa na ugonjwa huo hawawezi kuusambaza. Watu walioambukizwa na bakteria wa TB wana hatari ya maisha ya 5-10% ya kuugua TB. Wale walio na kinga dhaifu, kama vile watu wanaoishi na VVU, utapiamlo au kisukari, au watu wanaotumia tumbaku, wana hatari kubwa ya kuugua. Dalili: Dalili za kawaida za ugonjwa wa TB ni pamoja na: Kikohozi cha muda mrefu, maumivu ya kifua, udhaifu au uchovu, kupungua uzito, homa, jasho la usiku. Mara nyingi, dalili hizi zitakuwa nyepesi kwa miezi mingi, hivyo kusababisha kuchelewa kutafuta huduma na kuongeza hatari ya kueneza maambukizi kwa wengine.
translations.hypothesis: Watu ambao wameambukizwa lakini hawajaugua ugonjwa huo hawana uwezo wa kuusambaza. Watu ambao wameambukizwa na bakteria ya kifua kikuu wana hatari ya asilimia 5-10 ya kuugua kifua kikuu katika maisha yao. Watu wenye mfumo wa kinga ulio dhaifu, kama vile wale wanaishi na HIV, lishe mbaya au kisukari, au wale wanaotumia tumbaku, wana hatari kubwa zaidi ya kuugua. Dalili: Dalili za kawaida za ugonjwa wa kifua kikuu ni pamoja na: Kukosa pumzi kwa muda mrefu, maumivu ya kifua, udhaifu au uchovu, kupoteza uzito, homa, jasho la usiku. Mara nyingi, dalili hizi huwa hafifu kwa miezi mingi, na hivyo kusababisha kuchelewa katika kutafuta huduma za matibabu na kuongeza hatari ya kusambaza maambukizi kwa wengine.

translations.source: If the healthcare provider suspects a patient to have TB disease, they will send the patient for testing. In the case of suspected lung TB disease, patients will be asked to give a sputum sample for testing for TB bacteria. For non-lung TB disease, samples of affected body fluids and tissue can be tested. WHO recommends rapid molecular diagnostic tests as initial tests for people showing signs and symptoms of TB. Other diagnostic tools can include sputum smear microscopy and chest X-rays.
translations.reference: Mtoa huduma wa afya akishuku mgonjwa kuwa na ugonjwa wa TB, atampeleka mgonjwa kupima. Katika kesi ya ugonjwa unaoshukiwa wa TB ya mapafu, wagonjwa wataombwa kutoa sampuli ya makohozi kwa ajili ya kupima bakteria wa TB. Kwa ugonjwa wa TB usio wa mapafu, sampuli za maji na tishu zilizoathirika za mwili zinaweza kupimwa. WHO inapendekeza vipimo vya haraka vya uchunguzi wa molekuli kama vipimo vya awali kwa watu wanaoonyesha dalili na dalili za TB. Zana nyingine za uchunguzi zinaweza kujumuisha hadubini ya smear ya sputum na X-rays ya kifua.
translations.hypothesis: Iwapo mtoa huduma ya afya anashuku kuwa mgonjwa ana ugonjwa wa kifua kikuu (TB), atampeleka mgonjwa ili apimwe. Katika kesi ya ugonjwa wa kifua kikuu unaohusiana na mapafu, wagonjwa wataombwa kutoa sampuli ya homa ili kupimwa na bakteria ya kifua kikuu. Kwa ugonjwa wa kifua kikuu usiohusiana na mapafu, sampuli za maji ya mwili na tishu zilizopata athari zinaweza kupimwa. Shirika la Afya Duniani (WHO) inapendekeza vipimo vya haraka vya kimolecular kama vipimo vya awali kwa watu wanaonyesha dalili na matokeo ya ugonjwa wa kifua kikuu. Vifaa vingine vya uchunguzi vinaweza kujumuisha uchunguzi wa homa chini ya mikroskopi na picha za X-ray za kifua.

translations.source: With TB infection, a person gets infected with TB bacteria that lie inactive in the body. This infection can develop into TB disease if their immune system weakens. People with TB infection do not show any signs or symptoms of TB. To identify TB infection, healthcare providers will screen at-risk patients to rule out active TB, and they may use a skin or blood test to check for TB infection. Treatment: TB disease is curable.
translations.reference: Kwa maambukizi ya TB, mtu huambukizwa na bakteria ya TB ambayo haifanyi kazi katika mwili. Maambukizi haya yanaweza kukua na kuwa ugonjwa wa TB ikiwa mfumo wao wa kinga utadhoofika. Watu wenye maambukizi ya TB hawaonyeshi dalili zozote za TB. Ili kutambua maambukizi ya TB, watoa huduma za afya watachunguza wagonjwa walio katika hatari ili kudhibiti TB hai, na wanaweza kutumia kipimo cha ngozi au damu ili kuangalia maambukizi ya TB. Matibabu: Ugonjwa wa TB unatibika.
translations.hypothesis: Katika maambukizi ya kifua kikuu, mtu huambukizwa na bakteria ya kifua kikuu ambayo husimama bila shughuli ndani ya mwili. Maambukizi haya yanaweza kubadilika kuwa ugonjwa wa kifua kikuu ikiwa mfumo wa kinga wa mtu huyo udhaifu. Watu walio na maambukizi ya kifua kikuu hawadhihirishi dalili au matokeo yoyote ya ugonjwa huo. Ili kubaini maambukizi ya kifua kikuu, watoa huduma za afya huangalia wagonjwa walio katika hatari ili kuondoa uwezekano wa kuwa na kifua kikuu kilichoendelea, na wanaweza kutumia mtihani wa ngozi au damu ili kuangalia kama kuna maambukizi ya kifua kikuu. Matibabu: Ugonjwa wa kifua kikuu unaweza kupona.

translations.source: It is treated by standard 6 month course of 4 antibiotics. Common drugs include rifampicin and isoniazid. In some cases the TB bacteria does not respond to the standard drugs. In this case, the patient has drug-resistant TB. for drug-resistant TB is longer and more complex.
translations.reference: Inatibiwa kwa kozi ya kawaida ya miezi 6 ya antibiotiki 4. Dawa za kawaida ni pamoja na rifampicin na isoniazid. Katika baadhi ya matukio, bakteria ya TB haijibu dawa za kawaida. Katika kesi hii, mgonjwa ana TB sugu ya dawa. kwa TB sugu ya dawa ni ya mda mrefu na ngumu zaidi.
translations.hypothesis: Matibabu yake hufanywa kwa kutumia dawa za kuzuia bakteria ambazo hutumiwa kwa muda wa miezi sita. Dawa za kawaida zinazotumika ni rifampisina na isoniazid. Katika baadhi ya kesi, bakteria ya kifua kikuu (TB) huendelea kuwa na ujanibari kwa dawa hizo za kawaida. Katika hali kama hiyo, mgonjwa huugua aina ya kifua kikuu ambayo haijibu dawa. Matibabu ya aina hii ya kifua kikuu huchukua muda mrefu zaidi na ni changamano zaidi.

translations.source: The course of TB drugs is provided to the patient with information, supervision and support by a health worker or trained volunteer. Without such support, treatment adherence can be difficult. If the treatment is not properly completed, the disease can become drug-resistant and can spread. In the case of TB infection (where the patient is infected with TB bacteria but not ill), TB preventive treatment can be given to stop the onset of disease. This treatment uses the same drugs for a shorter time.
translations.reference: Muda wa dawa za TB hutolewa kwa mgonjwa kwa taarifa, usimamizi na usaidizi na mfanyakazi wa afya au mtu wa kujitolea aliyefunzwa. Bila msaada kama huo, ufuasi wa matibabu unaweza kuwa mgumu. Ikiwa matibabu haijakamilika vizuri, ugonjwa unaweza kuwa sugu wa dawa na unaweza kuenea. Katika kesi ya maambukizi ya TB (ambapo mgonjwa ameambukizwa na bakteria ya TB lakini sio mgonjwa), matibabu ya kuzuia TB yanaweza kutolewa ili kukomesha kuanza kwa ugonjwa huo. Tiba hii hutumia dawa sawa kwa muda mfupi.
translations.hypothesis: Kozi ya dawa za kifua kikuu hupewa mgonjwa, pamoja na maelezo, usimamizi, na msaada kutoka kwa mfanyakazi wa afya au mjumbe aliye na mafunzo. Bila msaada huo, kuendeleza matibabu kunaweza kuwa changamoto. Ikiwa matibabu hayakamiliki vizuri, ugonjwa unaweza kuwa sugu kwa dawa na unaweza kusambaa. Katika kesi ya maambukizi ya kifua kikuu (ambapo mgonjwa amebeba bakteria ya kifua kikuu lakini hana ugonjwa), matibabu ya kuzuia kifua kikuu yanaweza kutolewa ili kuzuia ugonjwa huo kuanza. Matibabu haya hutumia dawa sawa, lakini kwa muda mfupi.

translations.source: Recent treatment options have shortened the duration to treatment to only 1 or 3 months, as compared to 6 months in the past.
translations.reference: Chaguo za matibabu za hivi majuzi zimefupisha muda wa matibabu hadi mwezi 1 au 3 tu, ikilinganishwa na miezi 6 iliyopita.
translations.hypothesis: Mbinu za matibabu za hivi karibuni zimefanya muda wa matibabu kupunguzwa hadi miezi 1 au 3 tu, ikilinganishwa na miezi 6 iliyokuwa kawaida hapo awali.

translations.source: Ultraviolet radiation Overview: Ultraviolet (UV) radiation covers the wavelength range of 100–400 nm, which is a higher frequency and lower wavelength than visible light. UV radiation comes naturally from the sun, but it can also be created by artificial sources used in industry, commerce and recreation. The UV region covers the wavelength range 100-400 nm and is divided into three bands: UVA (315-400 nm)UVB (280-315 nm)UVC (100-280 nm). As sunlight passes through the atmosphere, all UVC and approximately 90% of UVB radiation is absorbed by ozone, water vapour, oxygen and carbon dioxide.
translations.reference: Mionzi ya ultraviolet Muhtasari: Mionzi ya Ultraviolet (UV) hufunika masafa ya urefu wa 100-400 nm, ambayo ni masafa ya juu na urefu wa chini wa mawimbi kuliko mwanga unaoonekana. Mionzi ya UV hutoka kwa jua, lakini pia inaweza kuundwa na vyanzo vya bandia vinavyotumiwa katika sekta, biashara na burudani. Kanda ya UV inashughulikia safu ya urefu wa 100-400 nm na imegawanywa katika bendi tatu: UVA (315-400 nm) UVB (280-315 nm)UVC (100-280 nm). Mwangaza wa jua unapopita kwenye angahewa, UVC zote na takriban 90% ya mionzi ya UVB humezwa na ozoni, mvuke wa maji, oksijeni na dioksidi kaboni.
translations.hypothesis: Mionzi ya Ultravioleti: Mionzi ya ultravioleti (UV) inashughulikia aina ya urefu wa mawimbi ya kutoka 100 hadi 400 nanometeri (nm), ambayo ni masafa ya juu na urefu wa mawimbi wa chini kuliko mwanga unaoonekana. Mionzi ya UV hutoka asili kutoka kwenye jua, lakini pia inaweza kutengenezwa na vyanzo vya bandia ambavyo hutumika katika viwanda, biashara na shughuli za burudani. Eneo la UV linashughulikia urefu wa mawimbi wa 100-400 nm na limegawanywa katika bendi tatu: UVA (315-400 nm), UVB (280-315 nm), na UVC (100-280 nm). Wakati mwanga wa jua unapita kupitia anga, mionzi yote ya UVC na takriban 90% ya mionzi ya UVB hichwa na ozoni, mvuke wa maji, oksijeni na dioksidi ya kaboni.

translations.source: UVA radiation is less affected by the atmosphere. Therefore, the UV radiation reaching the Earth’s surface is largely composed of UVA with a small UVB component. The amount of UV radiation from the sun that hits the Earth’s surface depends on several factors, including the sun’s height in the sky, latitude, cloud cover, altitude, the thickness of the ozone layer and ground reflection. Reductions in the ozone layer due to human-created pollution increase the amount of UVA and UVB that reaches the surface. This can impact human health, animals, marine organisms and plant life.
translations.reference: Mionzi ya UVA huathiriwi kidogo na angahewa. Kwa hiyo, mionzi ya UV inayofikia uso wa Dunia kwa kiasi kikubwa inajumuisha UVA na sehemu ndogo ya UVB. Kiasi cha mionzi ya UV kutoka kwenye jua ambayo hupiga uso wa Dunia inategemea mambo kadhaa, ikiwa ni pamoja na urefu wa jua angani, latitudo, kifuniko cha mawingu, urefu, unene wa safu ya ozoni na kutafakari kwa ardhi. Kupungua kwa tabaka la ozoni kwa sababu ya uchafuzi unaotengenezwa na binadamu huongeza kiwango cha UVA na UVB kinachofika kwenye uso. Hii inaweza kuathiri afya ya binadamu, wanyama, viumbe vya baharini na maisha ya mimea.
translations.hypothesis: Mionzi ya UVA huathirika kidogo na anga. Kwa hivyo, mionzi ya UV inayofika kwenye uso wa dunia ina wingi wa mionzi ya UVA, na sehemu ndogo ya mionzi ya UVB. Kiasi cha mionzi ya UV kutoka jua inayofika kwenye uso wa dunia hutegemea mambo kadhaa, ikiwa ni pamoja na urefu wa jua angani, eneo (latitude), uwepo wa mawingu, urefu (altitude), unene wa safu ya ozone, na urefu wa nyuso za ardhi. Kupungua kwa safu ya ozone kutokana na uchafuzi unaosababishwa na binadamu huongeza kiasi cha mionzi ya UVA na UVB inayofika kwenye uso. Hii inaweza kuathiri afya ya binadamu, wanyama, viumbe vya baharini, na mimea.

translations.source: In humans, increased UV exposure can cause skin cancers, cataracts and immune system damage. Prevention: The rise in the number of cases of skin cancer over the past decades is strongly related to increased exposure to the sun during outdoor activities and to artificial sources of UV radiation such as sunlamps and tanning beds. Overexposure is also the underlying cause of harmful effects on the eyes and immune system. Adopting a few simple precautions can greatly reduce the risk of these health conditions: Limit time in the midday sun from 10 a.m. to 4 p.m., particularly on days when the UV index is high When UV rays are most intense and you must be outside, try to find shade and wear protective clothing, including a brimmed hat that provides sun protection for your head, face and neck.
translations.reference: Kwa wanadamu, kuongezeka kwa mfiduo wa UV kunaweza kusababisha saratani ya ngozi, mtoto wa jicho na uharibifu wa mfumo wa kinga. Kinga: Kuongezeka kwa idadi ya visa vya saratani ya ngozi katika miongo kadhaa iliyopita kunahusiana sana na kuongezeka kwa mwanga wa jua wakati wa shughuli za nje na vyanzo bandia vya mionzi ya UV kama vile taa za jua na vitanda vya ngozi. Mfiduo kupita kiasi pia ndio sababu kuu ya athari mbaya kwa macho na mfumo wa kinga. Kuchukua tahadhari chache rahisi kunaweza kupunguza sana hatari ya hali hizi za afya: Weka kikomo wakati wa jua la mchana kutoka 10 asubuhi hadi 4 p.m., hasa siku ambazo index ya UV iko juu. Wakati miale ya UV ni mikali zaidi na lazima uwe nje, jaribu kutafuta kivuli na kuvaa mavazi ya kujikinga, ikiwa ni pamoja na kofia yenye ukingo ambayo hutoa ulinzi wa jua kwa kichwa chako, uso na shingo.
translations.hypothesis: Kwa binadamu, ongezeko la mwangaza wa UV linaweza kusababisha saratani ya ngozi, mawe ya macho, na uharibifu wa mfumo wa kinga. Kuzuia: Kuongezeka kwa idadi ya kesi za saratani ya ngozi katika miongo iliyopita kuna uhusiano mkubwa na ongezeko la mfiduo wa jua wakati wa shughuli za nje, na pia kwa vyanzo vya bandia vya mionzi ya UV, kama vile taa za jua na vitanda vya kuchomwa na jua. Mfiduo mwingi pia ndio sababu kuu ya madhara ya kiafya kwenye macho na mfumo wa kinga. Kuchukua tahadhari chache rahisi kunaweza kupunguza sana hatari ya hali hizi za kiafya: Punguza muda wa kukaa jua wakati wa mchana, hasa kati ya saa 10 asubuhi na saa 4 mchana, hasa siku ambazo kiwango cha UV ni cha juu. Wakati mionzi ya UV inakuwa kali zaidi na lazima uwe nje, jaribu kutafuta kivuli na kuvaa nguo za kinga, ikiwa ni pamoja na kofia yenye kingo ambayo inalinda kichwa, uso na shingo yako dhidi ya jua.

translations.source: Use a broad-spectrum sunscreen of at least 15 SPF – ideally higher– and reapply every 2 hours. Avoid sunlamps and tanning parlours, as they are known to damage the skin and eyes. Children’s UV exposure should be carefully limited because they are in a dynamic state of growth and therefore more susceptible to environmental threats than adults. Many vital functions such as the immune system are not fully developed at birth, and unsafe environments may interfere with their normal development. WHO Response: WHO works with Member States and partners to increase public understanding of the effects of overexposure to ultraviolet radiation.
translations.reference: Tumia kinga ya jua yenye wigo mpana wa angalau SPF 15 - bora zaidi - na upake tena kila saa 2. Epuka taa za jua na vyumba vya ngozi, kwani vinajulikana kuharibu ngozi na macho. Mionzi ya UV ya watoto inapaswa kupunguzwa kwa uangalifu kwa sababu wako katika hali ya ukuaji na kwa hivyo huathirika zaidi na matishio ya mazingira kuliko watu wazima. Kazi nyingi muhimu kama vile mfumo wa kinga haujatengenezwa kikamilifu wakati wa kuzaliwa, na mazingira yasiyo salama yanaweza kuingilia ukuaji wao wa kawaida. Majibu ya WHO: WHO inafanya kazi na Nchi Wanachama na washirika ili kuongeza uelewa wa umma juu ya athari za mionzi ya urujuanimno kupita kiasi.
translations.hypothesis: Tumia bidhaa ya kinga jua inayofaa kwa aina mbalimbali ya ngozi, yenye kiwango cha kinga cha angalau 15 SPF – ikiwezekana kiwango cha juu zaidi – na uitumie tena kila baada ya saa 2. Epuka matumizi ya taa za jua bandia na saluni za kuchomwa na jua, kwani zimeonekana kusababisha uharibifu kwenye ngozi na macho. Ufunuo wa watoto kwa mionzi ya UV unapaswa kudhibitiwa kwa uangalifu, kwa sababu wako katika hatua ya ukuaji na, kwa hivyo, wao ni rahisi zaidi kuathirika na hatari za mazingira kuliko watu wazima. Kazi muhimu nyingi, kama vile mfumo wa kinga, hazijaendelezwa kikamilifu kwa kuzaliwa, na mazingira hatari yanaweza kuathiri maendeleo yao ya kawaida. Jibu la WHO: Shirika la Afya Duniani (WHO) hufanya kazi na nchi wanachama na washirika ili kuongeza ufahamu wa umma kuhusu madhara ya kufichwa kwa mionzi ya ultraviolet.

translations.source: The INTERSUN Programme is a collaboration between WHO, the United Nations Environment Programme, the World Meteorological Organization, the International Agency on Cancer Research and the International Commission on Non-Ionizing Radiation Protection. It promotes and evaluates research on the health effects of UV radiation, and develops an appropriate response through guidelines, recommendations and information dissemination. The goals of the programme including providing practical and sound advice on the health and environmental impacts of UV exposure, to encourage countries to take action to reduce UV-induced health risks and to provide guidance to national authorities about sun awareness programmes. To this end, the programme collaborates with experts and specialist agencies to implement key research activities, identifies and quantifies health risks from UV radiation, develops reliable predictions of health and environmental consequences of changes in UV exposure with stratospheric ozone depletion, and develops practical ways of monitoring change in UV-induced health effects over time. The programme provides tools and guidelines related to the UV index, sun protection for children, artificial sunbeds, tourism and occupational health.
translations.reference: Mpango wa INTERSUN ni ushirikiano kati ya WHO, Mpango wa Mazingira wa Umoja wa Mataifa, Shirika la Hali ya Hewa Duniani, Shirika la Kimataifa la Utafiti wa Saratani na Tume ya Kimataifa ya Ulinzi wa Mionzi isiyo ya Ion. Inakuza na kutathmini utafiti juu ya athari za kiafya za mionzi ya UV, na kukuza jibu linalofaa kupitia miongozo, mapendekezo na usambazaji wa habari. Malengo ya mpango huo ikiwa ni pamoja na kutoa ushauri wa kiutendaji na mzuri kuhusu athari za kiafya na kimazingira zitokanazo na mionzi ya jua, kuhimiza nchi kuchukua hatua ili kupunguza hatari za kiafya zitokanazo na UV na kutoa mwongozo kwa mamlaka za kitaifa kuhusu mipango ya uhamasishaji wa jua. Ili kufikia mwisho huu, mpango huo unashirikiana na wataalam na mashirika maalum kutekeleza shughuli muhimu za utafiti, kutambua na kuhesabu hatari za afya kutokana na mionzi ya UV, kuendeleza utabiri wa kuaminika wa matokeo ya afya na mazingira ya mabadiliko ya mfiduo wa UV na kupungua kwa ozoni ya stratospheric, na kuendeleza njia za vitendo za ufuatiliaji mabadiliko katika athari za kiafya zinazotokana na UV kwa wakati. Mpango huo hutoa zana na miongozo inayohusiana na fahirisi ya UV, ulinzi wa jua kwa watoto, vitanda vya jua bandia, utalii na afya ya kazini.
translations.hypothesis: Programu ya INTERSUN ni ushirikiano kati ya Shirika la Afya la Ulimwengu (WHO), Mpango wa Mazingira wa Umoja wa Mataifa, Shirika la Kimataifa la Utabiri wa Hali ya Anga, Shirika la Kimataifa la Utafiti wa Saratani, na Tume ya Kimataifa ya Ulinzi dhidi ya Mionzi isiyo ya Ionizing. Programu hii inakuza na kutathmini utafiti kuhusu athari za mionzi ya UV kwenye afya, na huunda majibu sahihi kupitia miongozo, mapendekezo, na usambazaji wa taarifa. Lengo la programu hii ni kutoa ushauri wa vitendo na wa kuaminika kuhusu athari za mionzi ya UV kwenye afya na mazingira, kuhimiza nchi kuchukua hatua za kupunguza hatari za kiafya zinazosababishwa na mionzi ya UV, na kutoa mwongozo kwa mamlaka za kitaifa kuhusu programu za uhamasishaji kuhusu jua. Kwa madhumuni haya, programu hii inashirikiana na wataalamu na mashirika maalum ili kutekeleza shughuli muhimu za utafiti, kutambua na kupima hatari za kiafya zinazosababishwa na mionzi ya UV, kuunda uwezekano wa kuaminika wa athari za kiafya na za mazingira kutokana na mabadiliko katika mionzi ya UV, pamoja na kupungua kwa safu ya ozone katika anga, na kuunda njia za vitendo za kufuatilia mabadiliko katika athari za kiafya zinazosababishwa na mionzi ya UV kwa muda. Programu hii hutoa zana na miongozo inayohusiana na kiwango cha mionzi ya UV, ulinzi dhidi ya jua kwa watoto, matumizi ya mashine za bandia za kunawia, utalii, na afya ya wafanyakazi.

translations.source: Universal health coverage Overview: Universal health coverage (UHC) means that all people have access to the full range of quality health services they need, when and where they need them, without financial hardship. It covers the full continuum of essential health services, from health promotion to prevention, treatment, rehabilitation and palliative care. To deliver on this promise, countries need to have strong, efficient and equitable health systems that are rooted in the communities they serve. Primary health care (PHC) is the most effective and cost-efficient way to get there.
translations.reference: Chanjo ya afya kwa wote Muhtasari: Huduma ya afya kwa wote (UHC) ina maana kwamba watu wote wanapata huduma kamili za ubora wa afya wanazohitaji, wakati na mahali wanapozihitaji, bila matatizo ya kifedha. Inashughulikia mwendelezo kamili wa huduma muhimu za afya, kutoka kwa uhamasishaji wa afya hadi kinga, matibabu, urekebishaji na utunzaji wa uponyaji. Ili kutimiza ahadi hii, nchi zinahitaji kuwa na mifumo thabiti ya afya, yenye ufanisi na yenye usawa ambayo imejikita katika jumuiya zinazohudumia. Huduma ya afya ya msingi (PHC) ndiyo njia bora na ya gharama nafuu ya kufika hapo.
translations.hypothesis: Huduma ya afya kwa kila mtu: Huduma ya afya kwa kila mtu (UHC) inamaanisha kwamba watu wote wanaweza kupata huduma kamili za afya za ubora wanazohitaji, wakati na mahali wanapohitaji, bila kuwa na shida za kifedha. Inajumuisha kila hatua muhimu katika huduma za afya, kuanzia utangazaji wa afya, kuzuia magonjwa, matibabu, tiba ya uponyaji, na huduma za kupunguza maumivu. Ili kutimiza ahadi hii, nchi zinahitaji kuwa na mifumo ya afya yenye nguvu, inayofanya kazi vizuri, na inayoweka umakini sawa kwa wote, na ambayo imejikita katika jamii wanazohudumia. Huduma ya afya ya msingi (PHC) ndiyo njia bora na yenye gharama nafuu ya kufikia lengo hili.

translations.source: Every country has a different path to achieving UHC and to decide what to cover based on the needs of their populations and the resources at hand. Investing in PHC ensures that all those needs are identified, prioritized and addressed in an integrated way; that there is a robust and equipped health and care workforce; and that all sectors of society contribute to confronting the environmental and socio-economic factors that affect health and well-being, including preparing for, responding to and recovering from emergencies. WHO response: As a foundation for UHC, WHO recommends reorienting health systems using a primary health care (PHC) approach. Achieving UHC is a WHO strategic priority, with the goal of 1 billion more people benefitting from universal health coverage by 2025. In countries with fragile health systems, we focus on technical assistance to build national institutions and service delivery to fill critical gaps in emergencies.
translations.reference: Kila nchi ina njia tofauti ya kufikia UHC na kuamua nini cha kushughulikia kulingana na mahitaji ya idadi ya watu na rasilimali zilizopo. Kuwekeza katika PHC huhakikisha kwamba mahitaji hayo yote yanatambuliwa, yanapewa kipaumbele na kushughulikiwa kwa njia jumuishi; kwamba kuna nguvu kazi ya afya na utunzaji imara na yenye vifaa; na kwamba sekta zote za jamii zinachangia katika kukabiliana na mambo ya kimazingira na kijamii na kiuchumi yanayoathiri afya na ustawi, ikiwa ni pamoja na kujiandaa kwa ajili ya, kukabiliana na hali ya dharura. Jibu la WHO: Kama msingi wa UHC, WHO inapendekeza kuelekeza upya mifumo ya afya kwa kutumia mbinu ya huduma ya afya ya msingi (PHC). Kufikia UHC ni kipaumbele cha kimkakati cha WHO, kukiwa na lengo la watu bilioni 1 zaidi kunufaika na huduma ya afya kwa wote ifikapo 2025. tunazingatia usaidizi wa kiufundi ili kujenga taasisi za kitaifa na utoaji wa huduma ili kujaza nafasi muhimu wakati wa dharura.
translations.hypothesis: Kila nchi ina njia tofauti ya kufikia huduma za afya za kila mtu (UHC), na inaamua ni huduma gani zinazohitajika kulingana na mahitaji ya watu wake na rasilimali zinazopatikana. Kuwekeza katika huduma za afya za msingi (PHC) huhakikisha kwamba mahitaji yote yanatambuliwa, yamepangwa kwa umuhimu, na yanakabiliwa kwa njia iliyounganishwa; kwamba kuna wafanyakazi wa afya na huduma ambao wana uwezo na vifaa vya kutosha; na kwamba sekta zote za jamii zinashiriki katika kukabiliana na mambo ya mazingira na kijamii ambayo yanaathiri afya na ustawi, ikiwa ni pamoja na maandalizi, majibu, na ahueni kutoka kwa dharura. Jibu la WHO: Kama msingi wa UHC, WHO inapendekeza kurejea mifumo ya afya kwa kutumia mbinu ya huduma za afya za msingi (PHC). Kufikia UHC ni kipaumbele muhimu cha WHO, kwa lengo la kwamba watu bilioni 1 zaidi wanabakiwa na huduma za afya za kila mtu ifikapo mwaka wa 2025. Katika nchi ambazo zina mifumo ya afya dhaifu, tunazingatia kutoa usaidizi wa kiufundi ili kuimarisha taasisi za kitaifa na utoaji wa huduma ili kujaza pengo muhimu wakati wa dharura.

translations.source: In more robust health system settings, we drive public health impact towards health coverage for all through policy dialogue for the systems of the future and strategic support to improve performance. This work is supported by normative guidance and agreements; data, research and innovation; and leadership in the realms of diplomacy, advocacy, gender equality, health equity and human rights, multisectoral action, and finance. 2030 Agenda for SDGs WHO’s work is aligned with the Sustainable Development Goals (SDG) target 3.8, which focuses on achieving universal health coverage, including financial risk protection, access to quality essential health-care services and access to safe, effective, quality and affordable essential medicines and vaccines for all. Globally, progress towards universal health coverage is a challenge. Progress in service coverage has stalled while the proportion of the population facing catastrophic out-of-pocket health spending increases continuously.
translations.reference: Katika mipangilio thabiti zaidi ya mfumo wa afya, tunasukuma athari za afya ya umma kuelekea huduma ya afya kwa wote kupitia mazungumzo ya sera ya mifumo ya siku zijazo na usaidizi wa kimkakati ili kuboresha utendaji kazi. Kazi hii inaungwa mkono na mwongozo na makubaliano ya kawaida; na uongozi katika nyanja za diplomasia, utetezi, usawa wa kijinsia, usawa wa afya na haki za binadamu, hatua za sekta mbalimbali, na fedha. Ajenda ya 2030 ya SDGs Kazi ya WHO inawiana na Malengo ya Maendeleo Endelevu (SDG) lengo la 3.8, ambalo linalenga kufikia bima ya afya kwa wote, ikiwa ni pamoja na ulinzi wa hatari za kifedha, upatikanaji wa huduma bora za afya na gharama nafuu za dawa muhimu na chanjo za bei nafuu kwa wote. Ulimwenguni, maendeleo kuelekea huduma ya afya kwa wote ni changamoto. Maendeleo katika utoaji wa huduma yamekwama huku idadi ya watu wanaokabiliwa na matumizi mabaya ya afya nje ya mfukoni ikiongezeka kila mara.
translations.hypothesis: Katika mifumo ya afya yenye uthabiti zaidi, tunalenga kuongeza athari za afya ya umma ili kuhakikisha kila mtu anapata huduma za afya, kupitia majadiliano ya sera kuhusu mifumo ya afya ya siku zijazo na usaidizi wa kimkakati ili kuboresha utendaji. Kazi hii inasaidiwa na mwongozo na makubaliano; data, utafiti na ubunifu; na uongozi katika nyanja za diplomasia, utetezi, usawa wa kijinsia, usawa katika huduma za afya na haki za binadamu, utekelezaji wa hatua katika sekta mbalimbali, na fedha. Ajenda ya 2030 ya Lengo la Maendeleo Endelevu (SDGs) Kazi ya WHO inalingana na Lengo la 3.8 la SDGs, ambalo linazingatia kufikia huduma za afya kwa kila mtu, ikiwa ni pamoja na ulinzi dhidi ya hatari za kifedha, upataji wa huduma bora na muhimu za afya, na upataji wa dawa na chanjo muhimu, salama, bora na za bei nafuu kwa kila mtu. Kimataifa, maendeleo kuelekea huduma za afya kwa kila mtu ni changamoto. Maendeleo katika upataji wa huduma yamekwama, huku idadi ya watu wanaokumbana na gharama kubwa za afya ikiendelea kuongezeka.

translations.source: WHO monitors progress and publishes global reports to reveal the latest evidence every two years. See the reports here and visit the UHC fact sheet for more information.
translations.reference: WHO hufuatilia maendeleo na kuchapisha ripoti za kimataifa ili kufichua ushahidi wa hivi punde kila baada ya miaka miwili. Tazama ripoti hapa na utembelee karatasi ya ukweli ya UHC kwa habari zaidi.
translations.hypothesis: Shirika la Afya Duniani (WHO) linafuatilia maendeleo na hutoa ripoti za kimataifa ili kuchapisha matokeo ya hivi punde kila baada ya miaka miwili. Angalia ripoti hizi hapa na tembelea ukurasa wa habari kuhusu huduma bora za afya (UHC) ili kupata maelezo zaidi.

translations.source: Urban health Overview: Urbanization is one of the leading global trends of the 21st century that has a significant impact on health. Over 55% of the world’s population live in urban areas – a proportion that is expected to increase to 68% by 2050. As most future urban growth will take place in developing countries, the world today has a unique opportunity to guide urbanization and other major urban development trends in a way that protects and promotes health. This is important, not least because the health and well-being of citizens is perhaps a city’s most important asset.
translations.reference: Afya ya mjini Muhtasari: Ukuaji wa miji ni mojawapo ya mitindo inayoongoza duniani ya karne ya 21 ambayo ina athari kubwa kwa afya. Zaidi ya 55% ya watu duniani wanaishi mijini - idadi ambayo inatarajiwa kuongezeka hadi 68% ifikapo 2050. Kwa vile ukuaji mkubwa wa miji katika siku zijazo utafanyika katika nchi zinazoendelea, dunia leo ina fursa ya kipekee ya kuongoza ukuaji wa miji na mwelekeo mwingine mkuu wa maendeleo ya miji kwa njia ambayo inalinda na kukuza afya. Hii ni muhimu, sio kwa sababu afya na ustawi wa raia labda ndio mali muhimu zaidi ya jiji.
translations.hypothesis: Afya katika miji: Mabadiliko ya mijini ni mojawapo ya mienendo muhimu zaidi duniani katika karne ya 21, ambayo ina athari kubwa kwa afya. Zaidi ya asilimia 55 ya watu duniani wanaishi katika maeneo ya mijini – na idadi hii inatarajiwa kuongezeka hadi asilimia 68 ifikapo mwaka wa 2050. Kwa kuwa ukuaji wa mijini unaotarajia kutokea katika siku zijazo utafanyika hasa katika nchi zinazoendelea, dunia ina fursa ya kipekee ya kuongoza mabadiliko ya mijini na mienendo mingine muhimu ya maendeleo ya mijini, kwa njia ambayo inalinda na kukuza afya. Hii ni muhimu, hasa kwa sababu afya na ustawi wa wananchi huenda ni jambo muhimu zaidi kwa mji wowote.

translations.source: However, most of the 4.2 billion people living in cities suffer inadequate housing and transport, poor sanitation and waste management, and air quality that fails WHO guidelines. Other forms of pollution, such as noise, water and soil contamination, so-called urban heat islands, and a lack of space for walking, cycling and active living further combine to make cities epicentres of a noncommunicable disease epidemic and drivers of climate change. WHO Repository on Urban Health Impact: Around 40% of urban growth is in slums that lack safe water and sanitation, and 91% of people in urban areas breathe polluted air. When it comes to healthy diets, urbanization increases the distance from farm to fork, driving demand for unhealthy, processed foods.
translations.reference: Hata hivyo, wengi kati ya watu bilioni 4.2 wanaoishi mijini wanakabiliana na ukosefu wa makazi na usafiri, usafi wa mazingira na usimamizi duni wa taka, na ubora wa hewa ambao unakiuka miongozo ya WHO. Aina zingine za uchafuzi wa mazingira, kama vile kelele, uchafuzi wa maji na udongo, kinachojulikana kama visiwa vya joto mijini, na ukosefu wa nafasi ya kutembea, kuendesha baiskeli na kuishi hai huchanganyika zaidi na kufanya miji kuwa kitovu cha janga la magonjwa yasiyoambukiza na vichochezi vya mabadiliko ya hali ya hewa. Hazina ya WHO kwa Afya ya Mjini Athari: Takriban 40% ya ukuaji wa miji uko katika makazi duni ambayo hayana maji salama na vyoo, na 91% ya watu katika maeneo ya mijini wanapumua hewa chafu. Linapokuja suala la lishe bora, ukuaji wa miji huongeza umbali kutoka shamba hadi uma, na kusababisha mahitaji ya vyakula visivyo na afya, vilivyochakatwa.
translations.hypothesis: Hata hivyo, watu bilioni 4.2 wanaoishi katika miji wengi wanakabiliwa na nyumba zisizo na ubora, usafiri duni, usafi mbaya na usimamizi wa taka, na ubora wa hewa ambao haukidhi viwango vya Shirika la Afya Duniani (WHO). Aina nyingine za uchafuzi, kama vile kelele, uchafuzi wa maji na udongo, kile kinachojulikana kama "vijiwe vya joto vya mijini," na ukosefu wa nafasi za kutembea, kutumia baiskeli, na maisha shughuli, huongeza matatizo na kuifanya miji kuwa kitovu cha magonjwa ambayo hayasambazwi kupitia kuambukiza na kusababisha mabadiliko ya tabianchi. Hifadhi ya WHO kuhusu athari za afya katika miji: Karibu asilimia 40 ya ukuaji wa mijini unafanyika katika maeneo ya maskini ambako hakuna maji safi na usafi, na asilimia 91 ya watu wanaoongezeka katika maeneo ya mijini hupumua hewa iliyochafuzwa. Linapokuja suala la lishe bora, miji huongeza umbali kati ya shamba na meza, na hivyo kuongeza mahitaji ya vyakula visivyo na afya na yaliyopigwa.

translations.source: Urban dwellers are also highly vulnerable to the effects of climate change because of their dependence on fossil fuels for transport, cooking and heating. Cities account for over two thirds of the world’s energy and emit 60% of greenhouse gases, and those inland may experience temperatures 3–5º C higher than surrounding rural areas because of their large expanses of concrete and limited open green spaces. All of this puts urban dwellers’ health at risk. Most the top 10 causes of death are closely related to rapid and unplanned urbanization, and poor urban design and planning. Cities face the triple health burden of infectious diseases like HIV/AIDS, tuberculosis, pneumonia, dengue and diarrhoea;
translations.reference: Wakazi wa mijini pia wako katika hatari kubwa ya kuathiriwa na mabadiliko ya hali ya hewa kwa sababu ya utegemezi wao wa nishati ya mafuta kwa usafiri, kupikia na kupasha joto. Miji huchukua zaidi ya theluthi mbili ya nishati ya ulimwengu na hutoa 60% ya gesi chafuzi, na zile za ndani zinaweza kukumbwa na halijoto ya 3–5º C juu kuliko maeneo ya vijijini yanayozunguka kwa sababu ya upana wake mkubwa wa saruji na nafasi finyu za kijani kibichi. Haya yote yanahatarisha afya ya wakazi wa mijini. Sababu 10 kuu za vifo zinahusiana kwa karibu na ukuaji wa miji wa haraka na usio na mpango, na muundo mbaya wa mijini na mipango. Miji inakabiliwa na mzigo wa kiafya mara tatu wa magonjwa ya kuambukiza kama vile VVU/UKIMWI, kifua kikuu, nimonia, dengi na kuhara;
translations.hypothesis: Watu wanaokaa mijini pia wako katika hatari kubwa ya athari za mabadiliko ya tabianchi kwa sababu ya utegemezi wao kwa mafuta ya fosili kwa ajili ya usafiri, kupika na inapokota. Miji inachangia zaidi ya theluthi mbili ya nishati duniani na hutokoa asilimia 60 ya gesi za chafu, na maeneo ya mijini yaliyoko mbali na pwani yanaweza kuwa na joto la digrii 3-5 Celcius zaidi kuliko maeneo ya vijijini yaliyo karibu, kutokana na ukubwa wa maeneo yaliyojengwa kwa saruji na upungufu wa maeneo ya kijani. Yote haya yanaweka afya ya watu wanaokaa mijini hatarini. Sababu kuu za vifo kumi bora zina uhusiano wa karibu na ukuaji wa haraka na usio wa mpangilio wa mijini, pamoja na ubuni na mipango duni ya mijini. Miji yanakabiliwa na changamoto tatu za kiafya, ikiwa ni pamoja na magonjwa ya kuambukiza kama vile UKIMWI, kifua kikuu, pneumonia, dengue na utapiamu.

translations.source: noncommunicable diseases like heart disease, stroke, asthma, cancer, diabetes and depression; and violence and injuries, including road traffic injuries. WHO response: Despite their challenges, cities can create opportunities for better health, cleaner environments and climate action. Strong urban policies must prioritize health, as it is essential for fostering good urban livelihoods, building a productive workforce, creating resilient and vibrant communities, enabling mobility, promoting social interaction and protecting vulnerable populations. WHO’s approach to urban health focuses on better air quality, water and sanitation, and other environmental determinants; healthy urban planning; healthier and smoke-free environments; safe and healthy mobility; preventing violence and injuries; promoting healthy food systems and diets; environmental management of vector-borne diseases; and urban preparedness for emergencies.
translations.reference: magonjwa yasiyo ya kuambukiza kama vile ugonjwa wa moyo, kiharusi, pumu, saratani, kisukari na unyogovu; na vurugu na majeraha, ikiwa ni pamoja na majeraha ya barabarani. Jibu la WHO: Licha ya changamoto zao, miji inaweza kuunda fursa kwa afya bora, mazingira safi na hatua za hali ya hewa. Sera madhubuti za mijini lazima zitangulize afya, kwani ni muhimu kwa ajili ya kuendeleza maisha bora ya mijini, kujenga nguvu kazi yenye tija, kuunda jamii zenye uthabiti na uchangamfu, kuwezesha uhamaji, kukuza mwingiliano wa kijamii na kulinda idadi ya watu walio hatarini. Mtazamo wa WHO kuhusu afya ya mijini unazingatia ubora bora wa hewa, maji na usafi wa mazingira, na viashirio vingine vya mazingira; mipango ya miji yenye afya; mazingira bora na yasiyo na moshi; uhamaji salama na kiafya; kuzuia vurugu na majeraha; kukuza mifumo ya chakula na lishe bora; usimamizi wa mazingira ya magonjwa yanayoenezwa na vekta.na kujiandaa kwa miji kwa dharura.
translations.hypothesis: Magonjwa ambayo hayasambamki, kama vile magonjwa ya moyo, uvimbe, pumu, saratani, kisukari na unyogovu; na pia vurugu na majeraha, ikiwa ni pamoja na majeraha yanayosababishwa na ajali za barabarani. Jibu la WHO: Licha ya changamoto zake, miji inaweza kuleta fursa za afya bora, mazingira safi na juhudi za kukabiliana na mabadiliko ya tabianchi. Sera kali za mijini lazima zipendelee afya, kwani ni muhimu kwa kukuza maisha bora katika miji, kuunda wafanyakazi wazalendo, kujenga jamii imara na zenye shughuli, kuwezesha usafiri, kukuza mwingiliano wa kijamii na kulinda makundi yenye hatari. Mbinu ya WHO kuhusu afya ya mijini inalenga katika ubora bora wa hewa, maji na usafi, na mambo mengine ya mazingira; mipango bora ya mijini; mazingira yenye afya na yasiyo na moshi; usafiri salama na wenye afya; kuzuia vurugu na majeraha; kukuza mifumo na lishe bora ya chakula; usimamizi wa mazingira wa magonjwa yanayosababishwa na vimelea; na utayarifu wa mijini kwa dharura.

translations.source: Working across sectors and ensuring the coherence of policies across different areas is key to creating supportive and enabling environments for health, which ensures that health and equity considerations are integrated throughout the planning process, investments and policy decisions at the local level. To help Member States address the above priorities, WHO works to strengthen the evidence base to help policy-makers make informed decisions when addressing health risks. It provides tools and guidance on what works, and supports monitoring of key health-related indicators. WHO leads and engages in fostering city-to-city exchanges and helps develop institutional and policy frameworks for good urban governance for health and well-being in cities.
translations.reference: Kufanya kazi katika sekta zote na kuhakikisha uwiano wa sera katika maeneo mbalimbali ni muhimu katika kuunda mazingira ya kusaidia na kuwezesha afya, ambayo yanahakikisha kwamba masuala ya afya na usawa yanaunganishwa katika mchakato wa kupanga, uwekezaji na maamuzi ya sera katika ngazi ya ndani. Ili kusaidia Nchi Wanachama kushughulikia vipaumbele vilivyo hapo juu, WHO inafanya kazi ili kuimarisha msingi wa ushahidi ili kusaidia watunga sera kufanya maamuzi sahihi wanaposhughulikia hatari za kiafya. Inatoa zana na mwongozo juu ya kile kinachofanya kazi, na inasaidia ufuatiliaji wa viashiria muhimu vinavyohusiana na afya. WHO inaongoza na kushiriki katika kuhimiza mabadilishano ya jiji hadi jiji na kusaidia kuandaa mifumo ya kitaasisi na sera ya utawala bora wa miji kwa afya na ustawi katika miji.
