translations.source: Integrating COVID-19 services into essential health services In another important step towards system recovery and transition, most countries have made progress in integrating COVID-19 services into routine health service delivery. About 80-90% of countries have fully integrated COVID-19 vaccination, diagnostic and case management services as well as services for post COVID-19 condition into routine service delivery. Still, most countries (80% of 83 responding countries) reported at least one bottleneck to scaling up access to essential COVID-19 tools (e.g. COVID-19 diagnostics, therapeutics, vaccines and personal protective equipment - PPE), with health workforce issues and lack of funding representing the most common barriers. Further support needed for recovery, resilience and preparedness Most countries have started to apply what they have learnt during the COVID-19 pandemic, including through the institutionalization of a number of innovative service disruption mitigation strategies into routine health service delivery.
translations.reference: Kuunganisha huduma za UVIKO-19 katika huduma muhimu za afya katika hatua nyingine muhimu kuelekea ufufuaji na mabadiliko ya mfumo, nchi nyingi zimepiga hatua katika kuunganisha huduma za UVIkO-19 katika utoaji wa huduma za afya za kawaida. Takribani 80-90% ya nchi zimeunganisha kikamilifu huduma za chanjo ya UVIKO-19, uchunguzi na udhibiti wa kesi vilevile huduma za hali baada ya UVIKO-19 katika utoaji wa huduma za kawaida. Bado, nchi nyingi (80% yanchi 83 zinazojibu) ziliripoti angalau kizuizi kimoja cha kuongeza ufikiaji wa zana muhimu za UVIKO-19 (m.f uchunguzi wa UVIKO-19, matibabu, chanjo na vifaa vya kinga ya kibinafsi- PPE), na masuala ya wahudumu wa afya na ukosefu wa fedha unaowakilisha vikwazo vya kawaida. Msaada zaidi unahitajika kwa ajili ya kurejeshwa, uthabiti na kujitayarisha Nchi nyingi zimeanza kutumia yale ambayo wamejifunza kipindi cha janga la UVIKO-19, ikijumuisha kupitia kuweka mikakati kadhaa bunifu ya kupunguza usumbufu wa huduma katika utoaji wa huduma za afya.
translations.hypothesis: Kuunganisha huduma za COVID-19 na huduma muhimu za afya. Katika hatua nyingine muhimu kuelekea urekebishaji na mabadiliko ya mfumo, nchi nyingi zimefanya maendeleo katika kuunganisha huduma za COVID-19 na utoaji wa kawaida wa huduma za afya. Takriban asilimia 80-90 ya nchi zimeunganisha kikamilifu huduma za chanjo ya COVID-19, uchunguzi, utunzaji wa wagonjwa, na huduma za wagonjwa waliohitimu kutoka COVID-19 katika utoaji wa kawaida wa huduma. Hata hivyo, nchi nyingi (asilimia 80 ya nchi 83 zilizojibu) zimeripoti angalau kikwazo kimoja katika kuongeza upatikanaji wa zana muhimu za COVID-19 (k.m., uchunguzi wa COVID-19, dawa, chanjo, na vifaa vya kinga - PPE), huku masuala ya wafanyakazi wa afya na ukosefu wa fedha yakirejelewa kama vikwazo vya kawaida. Msaada zaidi unahitajika kwa ajili ya urekebishaji, ustahimilivu, na uandaaji. Nchi nyingi zimeanza kutumia yale ambayo wamejifunza wakati wa janga la COVID-19, ikiwa ni pamoja na kupitia utekelezaji wa mikakati mbalimbali ya kupunguza athari za kukatizwa kwa huduma katika utoaji wa kawaida wa huduma za afya.

translations.source: These include deployment of telemedicine approaches, promotion of home-based care or self-care interventions, approaches for strengthening health workers availability, capacities and support mechanisms, innovations in procuring and delivering medicines and supplies, more routine community communications, and partnerships with private sector providers. Three quarters of countries reported additional funding allocation towards longer term system recovery, resilience and preparedness. Countries expressed need for WHO support to address remaining challenges in the COVID-19 context and beyond, most frequently related to health workforce strengthening, building the monitoring capacities of health services, designing primary health care -- oriented models of care, governance, policy and planning and financial planning and funding. Note to editors: In the fourth round of WHO’s global pulse survey, 222 countries, territories and areas were invited to respond to a standardized web-based survey between November 2022 and January 2023. The fourth survey followed up on WHO’s previous 2020 and 2021 pulse surveys: Round 1 (May-September 2020), Round 2 (January-March 2021), and Round 3 (November-December 2021) which showed the extent to which the pandemic was affecting the continuity of essential health services and how countries are taking action.
translations.reference: Hizi ni pamoja na kupelekea mbinu za matibabu ya simu, uhamasishaji wa huduma za nyumbani au uingiliaji wa kujitegemea, mbinu za kuimarisha upatikanaji wa wahudumu wa afya, uwezo na mbinu za usaidizi, ubunifu katika ununuzi na usambazaji wa dawa na vifaa, mawasiliano zaidi ya kawaida ya jamii,n na ushirikiano na watoa huduma na sekta binafsi. Robo tatu ya nchi ziliripoti mgao wa ziada wa ufadhili kuelekea ufufuaji wa mfumo wa muda mrefu, ustahimilivu na utayari. Nchi zilieleza haja ya msaada wa WHO ili kushughulikia changamoto zilizosalia katika muktadha wa UVIKO-19 na zaidi, mara nyingi zinazohusiana na uimarishaji wa wahudumu wa afya, kujenga uwezo wa ufuatiliaji wa huduma za afya, kubuni huduma za afya ya msingi --mfano inayolengwa ya utunzaji, utawala, sera na mipango na mipango ya fedha na ufadhili. Maelezo kwa mhariri: Katika duru ya nne ya uchunguzi wa kimataifa wa Pulse wa WHO, nchi 222, wilaya na maeneo walialikwa kujibu uchunguzi sanifu wa msingi wa mtandao kati ya Novemba 2022 na Januari 2023. Uchunguzi wa nne ulifuatia tafiti za awali za WHO za 2020 na 2021: Duru ya 1(Mei - Septemba 2020), Duru ya 2(Januari - Machi 2021) na Duru ya 3 (Novemva - Desemba 2021) ambao ulionyesha kiwango ambacho janga hilo lilikuwa likiathiri muelekeo wa huduma za afya na jinsi nchi zinavyochukua hatua.
translations.hypothesis: Hapa kuna mambo ambayo yamejumuishwa, kama vile utumiaji wa teknolojia ya mbali katika utoaji wa huduma za afya, kukuza huduma za afya zinazotolewa nyumbani au kujitunza, njia za kuimarisha upatikanaji, uwezo na mifumo ya usaidizi kwa wafanyakazi wa afya, ubunifu katika ununuzi na utoaji wa dawa na vifaa, mawasiliano ya kawaida na jamii, na ushirikiano na watoa huduma wa sekta binafsi. Nchi takriban tatu kati ya nne zimeripoti kuwa zimeongeza fedha kwa ajili ya juhudi za muda mrefu za kupona, kuimarika na kuwa tayari kwa dharura. Nchi zimeeleza hitaji la usaidizi kutoka Shirika la Afya Duniani (WHO) ili kushughulikia changamoto zilizosalia katika muktadha wa COVID-19 na baada yake, hasa kuhusiana na kuimarisha wafanyakazi wa afya, kuimarisha uwezo wa kufuatilia huduma za afya, kuunda mifumo ya huduma za afya inayozingatia huduma za msingi, utawala, sera na mipango, na mipango ya kifedha na utoaji wa fedha. Kumbukumbu kwa wahariri: Katika awamu ya nne ya utafiti wa kimataifa wa WHO, nchi 222, wilaya na maeneo zaliwaalika kushiriki katika utafiti uliofanyika mtandaoni kati ya Novemba 2022 na Januari 2023. Utafiti wa nne ulikuwa unafuata tafiti za awali za WHO za mwaka 2020 na 2021: Awamu ya 1 (Mei-Septemba 2020), Awamu ya 2 (Januari-Machi 2021), na Awamu ya 3 (Novemba-Desemba 2021), ambazo zilionyesha jinsi janga hilo lilivyoathiri utoaji wa huduma muhimu za afya na jinsi nchi zinavyochukua hatua.

translations.source: While pulse surveys have limitations such as reporting bias and representativeness, the strength of this effort is that it is comprehensive and delivers information rapidly. The term “country” should be understood to include all countries, territories and areas. Trend analyses was completed based on responses from 84 countries, territories, or areas that responded to at least one survey section in all four pulse survey rounds.
translations.reference: Ilhali tafiti za Pulse zina vikwazo kama vile kuripoti upendeleo na uwakilishi, nguvu ya juhudi hii ni ya kina na inatoa taarifa kwa haraka. Neno "nchi" linapaswa kueleweka kujumuisha nchi, wilaya na maeneo yote. Uchunguzi wa mienendo ulikamilishwa kulingana na majibu kutoka nchi, wilaya au maeneo 84 ambayo yalijibu angalau sehemu moja ya utafiti katika duru zote nne za uchunguzi wa Pulse.
translations.hypothesis: Ingawa tafiti za haraka zina mapungufu kama vile ubaguzi katika taarifa na uwakilishi, nguvu ya juhudi hii ni kwamba ni kamili na hutoa taarifa kwa haraka. Neno "nchi" linapaswa kueleweka kuwa linajumuisha nchi zote, maeneo na eneo lolote. Uchambuzi wa mwelekeo ulifanywa kwa msingi wa majibu kutoka nchi 84, maeneo au eneo lolote ambazo zilijibu angalau sehemu moja ya tafiti katika raundi zote nne za tafiti za haraka.

translations.source: Statement of celebration for HRP’s 50th anniversary: Sweden and the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland To capture the spirit of Policy and Coordination Committee (PCC) members celebrating HRP’s 50th anniversary and its numerous achievements, Sweden and the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland read the following note of appreciation and congratulations at the 36th meeting of the HRP PCC. Our generation has seen more progress in science and technology than all preceding generations together and witnessed several intellectual, technological, medical and social transformation spearheaded by the contraceptive revolution and followed by advances in reproductive health, health innovations as well as rights and gender equality. However, considerable challenges in the field of SRHR continue, and, arguably, some advances are in question again today. We applaud HRP-staff, partners and supporters on the numerous achievements to date and recognise the enormous strides accomplished during half a century in producing the evidence that supports full and equal access to sexual and reproductive health and rights.
translations.reference: Taarifa ya kusherehekea maadhimisho ya miaka 50 ya HRP: Swideni na Uingereza na Ireland kaskazini Ili kukamata ari ya wajumbe wa Kamati ya Sera na Uratibu (PCC) wanaosherehekea miaka 50 ya HRP na mafanikio yake mengi, Sweden na Uingereza na Ireland Kaskazini walisoma maelezo yafuatayo ya shukurani na pongezi katika mkutano wa 36 wa HRP PCC. Kizazi chetu kimeshuhudia maendeleo zaidi katika sayansi na teknolojia kuliko vizazi vyote vilivyotangulia kwa pamoja na kushuhudia mabadiliko kadhaa ya kiakili, kiteknolojia, kiafya na kijamii yakiongozwa na mapinduzi ya uzazi wa mpango na kufuatiwa na maendeleo ya afya ya uzazi,ubunifu wa afya vilevile haki na usawa wa kijinsia. Hata hivyo, changamoto kubwa katika uga wa SRHR zinaendelea, na, bilashaka, baadhi ya maendeleo leo yanahojiwa tena. Tunawapongeza wafanyakazi wa HRP, washirika na waungaji mkono kwa mafanikio mengi hadi sasa na kutambua hatua kubwa zilizofikiwa katika nusu karne katika kutoa ushahidi unaounga mkono upatikanaji kamili wa usawa wa afya na haki za ngono na uzazi.
translations.hypothesis:
Tamko la sherehe kwa kumbukumbu ya miaka 50 ya HRP: Uswidi na Ufalme wa Muungano wa Uingereza na Ireland ya Kaskazini.

Ili kuonesha heshima na furaha ya wanachama wa Kamati ya Sera na Uratibu (PCC) ya HRP wakisherehekea miaka 50 ya shirika hilo na mafanikio yake mengi, Uswidi na Ufalme wa Muungano wa Uingereza na Ireland walisoma taarifa ifuatayo ya shukrani na pongezi katika mkutano wa 36 wa PCC ya HRP.

Kizazi chetu kimeshuhudia maendeleo makubwa katika sayansi na teknolojia kuliko vizazi vyote vilivyopita, na tumeshuhudia mabadiliko kadhaa muhimu katika sayansi, teknolojia, tiba na jamii, ambayo yameongozwa na mapinduzi ya udhibiti wa uzazi na yamefuatwa na maendeleo katika afya ya uzazi, ubunifu wa afya, pamoja na haki na usawa wa kijinsia. Hata hivyo, changamoto nyingi bado zipo katika uwanja wa afya ya uzazi na kijinsia, na, inaonekana, baadhi ya maendeleo yaliyopatikana yanaendelea kukabiliwa na maswali leo.

Tunawapongeza wafanyakazi, washirika na wafuasi wa HRP kwa mafanikio mengi ambayo yamepatikana hadi sasa, na tunatambua hatua kubwa ambazo zimefanywa katika kipindi cha miaka hamsini katika kutoa ushahidi unaounga mkono upataji kamili na sawa wa huduma za afya na haki za uzazi.

translations.source: We would like to outline three major categories of achievements: Firstly, the high-quality research by HRP has had tangible impact on public health policy, standards, and practice worldwide addressing the evolving needs of diverse populations. Secondly, HRP, notably through the HRP Alliance, has strengthened research institutions locally and globally, and, enabled numerous researchers to acquire the research skills that ensure that high-quality SRHR research can continue, and indeed increase, into the future. Thirdly, in a world beset by crisis, conflict and increasing resistance to gender equality, HRP has facilitated the upholding of the basic rights of women and girls and vulnerable populations. HRP remains the main instrument within the United Nations system for research in human reproduction and continues to be an exceptional leader in global sexual and reproductive health.
translations.reference: Tungependa kutaja makundi makuu matatu ya mafanikio: Kwanza, utafiti wa ubora wa juu wa HRP umekuwa na athari inayoonekana kwenye sera ya afya ya umma, viwango, na mazoezi Dunianikote kushughulikia mahitaji yanayoendelea ya watu mbalimbali. Pili,HRP, hasa kupitia muungano wa HRP, imeimarisha taasisi za utafii ndani na nje ya nchi, na imewezesha watafiti wengi kupata ujuzi wa utafiti ambao unahakikisha kwamba utafiti wa ubora wa juu wa SRHR unaweza kuendelea, na kwa hakika kuongezeka, katika siku zijazo. Tatu, katika Dunia iliyokumbwa na matatizo, migogoro na kuongezeka upinzani dhidi ya usawa wa kijinsia, HRP imewezesha uzingatiaji wa haki za kimataifa za wanawake na wasichana na watu walio katika mazingira magumu. HRP inasalia kuwa chombo kikuu ndani ya mfumo wa umoja wa mataifa kwa ajili utafiti katika uzazi wa Binadamu na inaendelea kuwa kiongozi wa kipekee katika afya ya ngono na uzazi Duniani.
translations.hypothesis: Tungependa kuainisha makundi matatu makuu ya mafanikio: Kwanza, utafiti wa hali ya juu unaofanywa na HRP umekuwa na athari kubwa katika sera, viwango, na utendaji wa afya ya umma ulimwenguni, ukihudumia mahitaji yanayobadilika ya makundi mbalimbali ya watu. Pili, HRP, hasa kupitia Shirika la HRP, imeuongeza nguvu kwa taasisi za utafiti katika eneo la ndani na la kimataifa, na imewezesha watafiti wengi kupata ujuzi wa utafiti ambao huhakikisha kwamba utafiti wa ubora wa juu katika afya ya uzazi na kingono unaendelea, na hata kuongezeka, katika siku zijazo. Tatu, katika ulimwengu unaokumbwa na migogoro, vita, na upinzani unaoongezeka dhidi ya usawa wa kijinsia, HRP imesaidia kuhifadhi haki za msingi za wanawake na wasichana, na makundi yenye hatari. HRP bado ndio chombo muhimu zaidi ndani ya mfumo wa Umoja wa Mataifa kwa ajili ya utafiti katika uzazi wa binadamu, na inaendelea kuwa kiongozi bora katika afya ya uzazi na kingono duniani.

translations.source: HRP and its partners have brought us closer to the promise of the 1994 International Conference on Population and Development and its call to put the rights of individuals, especially women and girls, at the heart of sustainable development. We want to express our appreciation and gratitude to the HRP secretariat, its partners, co-sponsors and the global network of collaborating centres and experts helping to ensure its efforts contribute to making a difference to the women, men, children, adolescents and various communities HRP is committed to serve. On behalf of PCC members, allow us to reaffirm our partnership with you to support the achievements of HRP, upon which the success of the 2030 Agenda depends.
translations.reference: HRP na washirika wake wametuleta karibu na ahadi ya mkutano wa kimataifa wa idadi ya watu na maendeleo wa 1994 na wito wake wa kuweka hali za watu binafsi, hususani wanawake na wasichana, katika mhimili wa maendeleo endelevu. Tunataka kutoa pongezi na shukurani zetu kwa sekretarieti ya HRP, washirika wake, wafadhili wenza na mtandao wa kimataifa wa vituo shirikishi na wataalamu kusaidia kuhakikisha juhudi zake zinachangia kuleta mabadiliko kwa wanawake, wanaume, watoto, vijana na jamii mbalimabali HRP imejitolea kuhudumia. Kwa niaba ya wanachama wa PCC, turuhusu kuthibitisha ushirikiano wetu nanyi ili kuunga mkono mafanikio ya HRP, ambayo mafanikio ya ajenda ya 2030 inategemea.
translations.hypothesis: Shirika la HRP na washirika wake wametufikisha karibu na ahadi ya Mkutano wa Kimataifa wa Mwaka wa 1994 kuhusu Idadi ya Watu na Maendeleo, na wito wake wa kuweka haki za watu binafsi, hasa wanawake na wasichana, katika msingi wa maendeleo endelevu. Tunataka kuonyesha shukrani zetu kwa sekretarieti ya HRP, washirika wake, wadhamini, na mtandao wa kimataifa wa vituo vya ushirikiano na wataalamu ambao husaidia kuhakikisha kwamba juhudi zake zinaathiri maisha ya wanawake, wanaume, watoto, vijana, na jamii mbalimbali ambazo HRP imejitolea kuwahudumia. Kwa niaba ya wanachama wa PCC, tunataka kurudia tena ushirikiano wetu na nyinyi ili kusaidia mafanikio ya HRP, ambayo yanafanya kazi muhimu katika kufanikisha Ajenda ya 2030.

translations.source: WHO launches new initiative to improve pandemic preparedness To help countries better prepare for future pandemics, WHO launched a new initiative today that provides guidance on integrated planning for responding to any respiratory pathogen such as influenza or coronaviruses. The new Preparedness and Resilience for Emerging Threats Initiative, or PRET, incorporates the latest tools and approaches for shared learning and collective action established during the COVID-19 pandemic and other recent public health emergencies. Through the initiative, WHO will use a mode of transmission approach to guide countries in pandemic planning, given that many capacities and capabilities are common among groups of pathogens. PRET answers the call for technical guidance and support for promoting and strengthening integrated preparedness and response, as outlined in World Health Assembly resolutions.
translations.reference: WHO inazindua mpango mpya wa kuboresha utayari wa janga Ili kusaidia nchi kujiandaa vyema kwa magonjwa ya mlipuko ya siku sijazo, WHO imezindua mpango mpya leo ambao unatoa muongozo kuhusu upangaji jumuishi wa kukabiliana na vimelea vyovyote vya upumuaji kama vile mafua au virusi vya Korona. Mpango mpya wa kujitayarisha na ustahimilivu kwa mpango wa vitisho vinavyoibuka, au PRET, inajmuisha zana na mbinu za hivi punde zaidi za kujifunza pamoja na kuchukua hatua za pamoja zilizoainishwa wakati wa janga la UVIKO-19 na dharura nyingine za hivi karibuni za afya ya Umma. Kupitia mpango huo, WHO itatumia mbinu ya maambukizi kuziongoza nchi katika upangaji wa janga hilo, ikizingatiwa kwamba nafasi na uwezo mkubwa ni wa wakaida miongoni mwa makundi ya vimelea vya magonjwa. PRET inajibu mwito wa muongozo za kiufundi na usaidizi kwa ajili ya kukuza na kuimarisha utayari jumuishi na muitikio, kama ilivyoainishwa katika maazimio ya baraza la afya ulimwenguni.
translations.hypothesis: Shirika la Afya Duniani (WHO) limezindua mpango mpya ili kuimarisha uwezekano wa nchi kukabiliana na magonjwa ya kuambukiza. Mpango huu, ambao ulizinduliwa leo, unatoa mwongozo kuhusu mipango iliyounganishwa ya kukabiliana na magonjwa yoyote ya mfumo wa upumuaji, kama vile homa ya kawaida au virusi vya corona. Mpango mpya wa "Uwezekano na Uimara Dhidi ya Tishio la Magonjwa Mapya," au PRET, unajumuisha zana na mbinu za kisasa za kujifunza pamoja na kuchukua hatua za pamoja, ambazo zimeanzishwa wakati wa janga la COVID-19 na dharura zingine za hivi karibuni za afya ya umma. Kupitia mpango huu, WHO itatumia mbinu ya njia za kuambukiza ili kuongoza nchi katika mipango ya kukabiliana na magonjwa ya kuambukiza, kwani uwezo na rasilimali nyingi ni sawa kati ya makundi tofauti ya magonjwa. Mpango wa PRET unaletea suluhisho kwa mahitaji ya mwongozo na usaidizi wa kiufundi wa kukuza na kuimarisha uwezekano na utayarifu wa kukabiliana na magonjwa, kama ilivyoelezwa katika azimio za Mkutano Mkuu wa Afya Duniani.

translations.source: The COVID-19 pandemic and other health emergencies have shown that countries need to be operationally ready to respond to infectious disease threats, with tailored preparedness plans in hand and better coordination and collaboration with other sectors such as agriculture. “Preparedness, prevention, and response activities must not be the province of the health sector alone,” said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO Director-General. “Just as health emergencies have impacts across many sectors, so must our preparedness and response efforts span sectors, disciplines and pathogens. It is critical, too, that community engagement and equity are the centre of our efforts, especially for those populations that are marginalized and most at risk.” PRET is an evolution in WHO’s approach to pandemic preparedness through the application of a mode of transmission lens, rather than a focus on specific diseases.
translations.reference: Janga la UVIKO-19 na dharura nyingine za kiafya zimeonyesha kuwa nchi zinahitaji kuwa tayari kukabiliana na matishio ya magonjwa ya kuambukiza, na mipango mahususi ya kujitayarisha mikononi na uratibu bora na ushirikiano na sekta nyingine kama vile kilimo. "Shughuli za kujitayarisha, kuzuia, na kukabiliana hazipaswi kuwa eneo la sekta ya afya pekee", alisema Dk Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, mkurugenzi mkuu wa WHO. "Kama vile hali za dharura za kiafya zinavyoathiri katika sekta nyingi, ndivyo pia juhudi zetu za kujitayarisha na kukabiliana nazo zienee katika sekta, taaluma na viini vya magonjwa. Pia ni muhimu kwamba ushirikishwaji wa jamii na usawa ni kitovu cha jamii zetu, hususani kwa wale watu waliotengwa na walio hatarini zaidi". PRET ni mageuzi katika mkabala wa WHO wa kujitayarisha kwa janga kupitia utumiaji wa lenzi ya maambukizi, badala ya kuzingatia magonjwa mahususi.
translations.hypothesis: Janga la COVID-19 na dharura zingine za kiafya zimeonyesha kwamba nchi zinahitaji kuwa tayari kikamilifu ili kujibu hatari za magonjwa ya kuambukiza, na kuwa na mipango maalum ya uandaaji, pamoja na ushirikiano bora na sekta nyingine kama vile kilimo. "Shughuli za uandaaji, kuzuia, na kukabiliana na magonjwa lazima zisikabidhiwe sekta ya afya pekee," alisema Dkt. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Mkurugenzi Mkuu wa WHO. "Kama vile dharura za kiafya zinaathiri sekta nyingi, hivyo pia juhudi zetu za uandaaji na kukabiliana na magonjwa zinapaswa kujumuisha sekta, taaluma, na vimelea tofauti. Pia, ni muhimu sana kwamba ushirikishwaji wa jamii na usawa ndio msingi wa juhudi zetu, hasa kwa makundi yaliyopotea na yaliyo katika hatari kubwa." Mpango wa PRET ni mabadiliko katika mbinu ya WHO ya uandaaji wa majanga, kwa kutumia mbinu ya kuangalia njia za kuenea kwa magonjwa, badala ya kuzingatia magonjwa mahususi.

translations.source: WHO will continue to develop and disseminate guidance on specific diseases as needed. The launch of the initiative is taking place on the last day of meetings with over 120 individuals representing Member States, partners, and the WHO secretariat, to define the path forward for PRET, including the development of a global implementation roadmap for respiratory pathogen pandemic preparedness. The PRET Initiative’s first module focuses on respiratory pathogens, including influenza, coronaviruses, and respiratory syncytial virus. Given the ongoing COVID-19 pandemic and the possible threat of avian influenza, this module will enable countries to critically review, test, and update their respiratory pandemic planning efforts to ensure they have the functional capacities and capabilities in place. A process is underway to identify the next group of pathogens, such as arboviruses, to be addressed under this initiative.
translations.reference: WHO itaendelea kuandaa na kusambaza muongozo kuhusu magonjwa maalumu inapohitajika. Uzinduzi wa mpango huo unafanyika katika siku ya mwisho ya mikutano na zaidi ya watu 120 wanaowakilisha nchi wanachama, washirika na sekretarieti ya WHO, kufafanua mustakabali ujao wa PRET, ikijumuisha maendeleo ya mpango wa utekelezaji wa kimataifa kwa ajili ya maandalizi ya janga la vimelea. Sehemu ya kwanza ya mpango wa PRET inaangazia vimelea vya magonjwa ya kupumua, ikiwa ni pamojana mafua, virusi vya Korona, virusi vya kupumua vya syncytial. Kwa kuzingatia janga la UVIKO-19 linaloendelea na tishio linalowezekana la mafua ya ndege, sehemu hii itawezesha nchi kukagua, kupima na kusasisha juhudi zao za kupanga janga la kupumua ili kuhakikisha kuwa zina nafasi na uwezo wa kufanya kazi. Mchakato unaendelea ili kutambua kundi la linalofuata la vimelea vya magonjwa, kama vile Arboviruses, kushughulikiwa chini ya mpango huu.
translations.hypothesis: Shirika la Afya Duniani (WHO) litaendelea kuendeleza na kusambaza maelekezo kuhusu magonjwa mahususi, kadri itakavyohitajika. Uzinduzi wa mpango huu unafanyika siku ya mwisho ya mikutano ambayo imehudhuriwa na zaidi ya watu 120, wawakilishi wa nchi wanachama, washirika, na makao makuu ya WHO, ili kuamua mwelekeo wa mpango wa PRET, ikiwa ni pamoja na kuandaa mpango wa kimataifa wa utekelezaji wa ulinzi dhidi ya magonjwa ya mapafu yanayoweza kusababisha janga. Moduli ya kwanza ya mpango wa PRET inalenga magonjwa ya mapafu, ikiwa ni pamoja na homa ya kawaida, virusi vya corona, na virusi vya mapafu vya syncytial. Kutokana na janga la COVID-19 linaloendelea na hatari inayowezekana ya homa ya ndege, moduli hii itawawezesha nchi kuchunguza, kujaribu, na kusasisha mipango yao ya ulinzi dhidi ya magonjwa ya mapafu ili kuhakikisha kwamba zina uwezo na mitambo inayohitajika. Mchakato unaendelea wa kutambua kundi lingine la magonjwa, kama vile virusi vya arbovirus, ambayo yatazingatiwa chini ya mpango huu.

translations.source: This will follow priorities identified through the ten proposals to strengthen the global architecture for health emergency preparedness, response, and resilience (HEPR). The PRET Initiative ushers in a new era for pandemic preparedness and represents an evolution of WHO’s core activities to support all Member States in strengthening health emergency preparedness, prevention, and response capacities and capabilities. The initiative can also serve to operationalize the objectives and provisions of the Pandemic Accord, which is currently being negotiated by WHO Member States. Following inputs and discussions among meeting participants, WHO invites stakeholders to this global call to action to advance respiratory pathogen pandemic preparedness. Call to Action
translations.reference: Hii itafuata vipaumbele vilivyoainishwa kupitia mapendekezo kumi ya kuimarisha usanifu wa kimataifa kwa ajili ya maandalizi ya dharura ya afya, majibu, na ustahimilivu (HEPR). Mpango wa PRET unaoleta enzi mpya ya kujitayarisha kwa janga na inawakilisha mageuzi ya shughuli za msingi za WHO ili kusaidia nchi wanachama wote katika kuimarisha utayari wa dharura wa afya, kuzuia, na kukabiliana na nafasi na uwezo. Pia mpango huo unaweza kutumika kutekeleza malengo na masharti ya makubaliano ya janga la ugonjwa ambao kwa sasa unajadiliwa na nchi wanachama wa WHO. Kufuatia mijadala na mazungumzo miongoni mwa washiriki wa mkutano, WHO inawaalika washikadau kwenye wito huu wa kimataifa kuchukua hatua ili kuendeleza utyarishaji wa vimelea vya magonjwa ya kupumua. Wito kwa hatua.
translations.hypothesis: Hii itafuata vipaumbele ambavyo vimetambuliwa kupitia mapendekezo kumi ya kuimarisha mfumo wa kimataifa wa utayarifu, utendaji, na uimara katika kukabiliana na dharura za afya (HEPR). Mpango wa PRET unaanza enzi mpya ya utayarifu wa janga la kimataifa na unawakilisha maendeleo ya shughuli muhimu za Shirika la Afya Duniani (WHO) katika kuunga mkono mataifa yote yanayokuwa wanachama ili kuimarisha uwezo na uwezo wa utayarifu, kuzuia, na kukabiliana na dharura za afya. Mpango huu pia unaweza kutumika kutekeleza malengo na masharti ya Mkataba wa Janga la Kimataifa, ambao kwa sasa unajadiliwa na mataifa yanayokuwa wanachama wa WHO. Baada ya maoni na majadiliano kati ya washiriki wa mkutano, WHO inawaalika wadau kushiriki katika mwaliko huu wa kimataifa wa kuchukua hatua ili kuendeleza utayarifu wa kukabiliana na magonjwa ya njia ya hewa. Mwaliko wa Kuchukua Hatua.

translations.source: WHO, Member States, and partners are working together to identify and implement priority actions to strengthen national, regional, and global preparedness for pandemics and emerging infectious disease threats. We recognize the role of communities and all sectors, and the need for harmonized and sustained momentum to end the cycle of panic and neglect that is all too common in pandemic preparedness and response. We recall lessons from previous epidemics and pandemics, especially those caused by respiratory pathogens. These pathogens have and will continue to pose a significant global threat with the potential to cause tremendous morbidity and mortality, overwhelm health systems, de-stabilize the global economy, and exacerbate inequities, which exist in the access to both the tools to prevent pandemics and health care for all people. This is a Call to Action to accelerate preparedness for pandemics and emerging threats globally.
translations.reference: WHO, nchi wanachama, na washirika wanafanya kazi pamoja kutambua na kutekeleza hatua za kipaumbele ili kuimarisha utayari wa kitaifa, kikanda na kimataifa kwa magonjwa ya milipuko na matishio yanayoibuka ya magonjwa ya kuambukiza. Tunatambua jukumu la jamii na sekta zote, na haja ya kuimarishwa na kuendelea kwa kasi ili kumaliza mzunguko wa hofu na kutekelezwa ambao ni wa kawaida sana katika kujiandaa na kukabiliana na janga. Tunakumbuka mafunzo kutoka kwa majanga na magonjwa ya milipuko ya hapo awali, hususani yale yanayosababishwa na magonjwa ya kupumua. Vimelea hivi vina na vitaendelea kuwa tishio kubwa la kimataifa lenye uwezekano wa kusababisha magonjwa na vifo vingi, kuelemea mifumo ya afya, na kudhoofisha uchumi wa Dunia, na kuzidisha ukosefu wa usawa, ambao upo katika upatikanaji wa zana zote mbili za kuzuia magonjwa ya mlipuko na huduma za afya kwa watu wote. Huu ni wito wa kuchukua hatua ili kuharakisha kujitayarisha kwa magonjwa ya mlipuko na vitisho vinavyoibuka Duniani.
translations.hypothesis: Shirika la Afya Duniani (WHO), nchi wanachama, na washirika wanafanya kazi pamoja ili kubaini na kutekeleza hatua muhimu za kuimarisha utayari wa kitaifa, kikanda, na wa kimataifa kwa ajili ya magonjwa ya kuenea haraka na hatari za magonjwa ya kuambukiza ambazo zinaibuka. Tunatambua umuhimu wa jamii na sekta zote, na pia tunatambua haja ya kuunganisha juhudi na kuendeleza maendeleo endelevu ili kukomesha mzunguko wa hofu na uliopotea ambao mara nyingi hutokea katika utayaraji na kukabiliana na magonjwa ya kuenea haraka. Tunakumbuka masomo kutoka kwa epidemiki na magonjwa ya kuenea haraka yaliyotokea hapo awali, hasa yale yaliyosababishwa na vimelea vinavyosababisha magonjwa ya mfumo wa kupumua. Vimelea hivi vimekuwa na bado yanaendelea kuwa na hatari kubwa kwa ulimwengu, na yana uwezo wa kusababisha magonjwa mengi na vifo, kuzidisha mfumo wa afya, kusababisha utata katika uchumi wa ulimwengu, na kuongeza tofauti ambazo zipo katika upatikanaji wa njia za kuzuia magonjwa ya kuenea haraka na huduma za afya kwa watu wote. Hii ni wito wa kuchukua hatua ili kuongeza kasi ya utayaraji kwa magonjwa ya kuenea haraka na hatari nyingine ambazo zinaibuka duniani kote.

translations.source: Effective preparedness relies on robust planning and coordinated action. A collective commitment on the following actions will see progress achieved by December 2025 as will be described in the (Preparedness and Resilience for Emerging Threats (PRET) monitoring framework: Update preparedness plans that affirm priority actions and that have considered learnings from past events. Recognizing the risk posed by respiratory pathogens, planning for a respiratory pathogen pandemic based on the themes identified in the PRET Module #1: Planning for Respiratory Pathogen Pandemics is a priority
translations.reference: Kujiandaa kwa ufanisi kunategemea mipango thabiti na hatua zilizoratibiwa. Ahadi ya pamoja ya hatua zifuatazo itaona maendeleo yaliyofikiwa ifikapo Desemba 2025 kama yatakavyo fafanuliwa katika mfumo wa ufuatiliaji wa (kujiandaa na ustahimilivu kwa vitisho vinavyojitokeza (PRET): Kusasisha mipango ya kujitayarisha ambayo inathibitisha vitendo vya kipaumbele na ambavyo imezingatia mafunzo kutoka kwa matukio ya zamani. Kutambua hatari inayoletwa na vimelea vya magonjwa ya kupumua, kupanga janga la vimelea vya upumuaji kwa kuzingatia mada zilizoainishwa katika Moduli ya PRET #1. Kupanga kipaumbele kwa magonjwa ya vimelea vya upumuaji
translations.hypothesis:
Maandalizi madhubuti yanategemea mipango thabiti na utekelezaji wa hatua zilizoratibiwa. Ahadi ya pamoja ya kuchukua hatua zifuatazo itasababisha maendeleo kufikiwa ifikapo Desemba 2025, kama ilivyoelezwa katika mfumo wa ufuatiliaji wa "Maandalizi na Uimara Dhidi ya Tishio Linalokua (PRET)". Hii inajumuisha:

*   Kusahihisha mipango ya maandalizi ili kuangazia hatua muhimu na kuzingatia masomo yaliyopatikana kutoka kwa matukio ya zamani.
*   Kutambua hatari inayotokana na magonjwa ya njia ya hewa, na hivyo kuandaa mipango ya kukabiliana na janga la magonjwa ya njia ya hewa, kulingana na mada zilizotambulika katika Moduli ya #1 ya PRET: "Maandalizi ya Janga la Magonjwa ya Njia ya Hawa." Hii ni jambo la muhimu.

translations.source: Increase connectivity among stakeholders in pandemic preparedness planning through systematic coordination and cooperation. This includes building equitable systems; conducting joint exercises; and sharing information on good practices, challenges, and opportunities. Dedicate sustained investments, financing and monitoring of pandemic preparedness with a particular focus on addressing the gaps identified during past pandemics and epidemics. The COVID-19 pandemic response has demonstrated what can be achieved with political commitment, community engagement and funding.
translations.reference: Kuongeza muunganisho kati ya washikadau katika upangaji maandalizi ya janga kupitia uratibu na ushirikiano wa kimfumo. Hii inajumuisha kujenga mifumo yenye usawa; kufanya mazoezi ya viungo; Na kushiriki taarifa kuhusu mazoea mazuri, changamoto na fursa. Kujitolea kwa uwekezaji endelevu, ufadhili na ufuatiliaji wa utayari wa janga kwa kuzingatia mapengo yaliyo ainishwa wakati w magonjwa ya mlipuko na milipuko iliyopita. Muitikio wa janga la UVIKO-19 umeonyesha kile kinachoweza kufikiwa kwa kujitolea kisiasa, ushirikishwaji wa jamii na ufadhili.
translations.hypothesis: Kuongeza uhusiano kati ya wadau katika upangaji wa maandalizi ya janga kwa njia ya ushirikiano na utaratibu wa pamoja. Hii inajumuisha kujenga mifumo yenye usawa; kufanya mazoezi ya pamoja; na kushiriana habari kuhusu mbinu bora, changamoto, na fursa. Toa uwekezaji endelevu, ufadhili, na ufuatiliaji wa maandalizi ya janga, huku ukijikita hasa katika kuondoa pengo ambazo zimetambuliwa wakati wa majanga na magonjwa ya zamani. Jibu la janga la COVID-19 limeonyesha kile ambacho kinaweza kufanikishwa kwa kujitolea kisiasa, ushirikishwaji wa jamii, na ufadhili.

translations.source: At the heart of this work is to ensure equity to be ready for the next pandemic together. Whole-of-society action is needed to make the progress outlined in this Call to Action. Implementation should therefore strengthen the resilience of communities; maintain, sustain, and build on routine systems; and leverage broader capacities for emergency preparedness and response. This Call to Action was released at the Preparedness and Resilience for Emerging Threats (PRET): Global Meeting for Future Respiratory Pathogen Pandemics held on 24-26 April 2023 in Geneva, Switzerland.
translations.reference: Kiini cha kazi hii ni kuhakikisha usawa kuwa tayari kwa janga lijalo pamoja. Hatua ya jamuii nzima inahitajika ili kufanya maendeleo yaliyoainishwa katika wito huu wa hatua. Utekelezaji unapaswa kuimarisha uimara wa jamii, kudumisha , kuendeleza, na kujenga juu ya mifumo ya kawaida; na kuongeza uwezo mpana zaidi wa kujiandaa na kukabiliana na Dharura. Wito huu wa kuchukua hatua ulitolewa katika maandalizi na ustahimilivu kwa vitisho vinavyoibuka (PRET): mkutano wa kimataifa wa magonjwa ya vimelea vya upumuaji ya baadae uliofanyika tarehe 24-26 Aprili 2023 huko Jeniva, Uswisi.
translations.hypothesis: Lengo kuu la kazi hii ni kuhakikisha uwiano na utayari wa pamoja kwa ajili ya janga linalokuja. Hatua za kushirikisha jamii zote zinahitajika ili kufanikisha maendeleo yaliyoelezwa katika wito huu. Utendaji kazi unapaswa, kwa hivyo, kuimarisha uimara wa jamii, kudumisha na kuendeleza mifumo ya kawaida, na kutumia uwezo mpana wa utayaraji na majibu ya dharura. Wito huu ulitangazwa katika mkutano wa kimataifa wa "Utabiraji na Uimara kwa Ajili ya Tishio Linalokua" (PRET), uliofanyika kuanzia Aprili 24 hadi 26, 2023, mjini Geneva, Uswizi.

translations.source: WHO urges increased implementation of recommended tools to combat malaria Marking World Malaria Day, the World Health Organization (WHO) is issuing a call for increased implementation of new and existing interventions to save lives from malaria. Nearly 1.5 million children at high risk of illness and death from malaria in Ghana, Kenya and Malawi have now received their first dose of the first malaria vaccine, RTS,S/AS01 (RTS,S), thanks to an ongoing pilot programme coordinated by WHO. The malaria vaccine pilots, launched in 2019, are increasing equity in access to malaria prevention for the most vulnerable and are saving lives. If implemented broadly, WHO estimates that malaria vaccines could save the lives of tens of thousands of children each year.
translations.reference: WHO inapendekeza kuongezeka kwa utekelezaji wa zana zinazopendekezwa za kukabiliana na Malaria Kuadhimisha siku ya Malaria Duniani, shirika la afya Duniani (WHO) linatoa wito wa kuongezwa kwa utekelezaji wa afua mpya na zilizopo ili kuokoa misha kutokana na Malaria. Karibu watoto milioni 1.5 walio katika hatari kubwa ya ugonjwa na kifo kutokana na Malaria nchini Ghana, Kenya na Malawi sasa wamepokea dozi yao ya kwanza ya chanjo ya kwanza ya Malaria, RTS,S/AS 01 (RTS,S), shukrani kwa mpango unaoendelea wa majaribio unaoratibiwa na WHO. Majaribio ya chanjo ya Malaria, ilyozinduliwa mwaka 2019, inaongeza usawa katika ufikiaji wa kuzuia Malaria kwa walio hatarini zaidi na wanaookoa maisha. Kama itatekelezwa kwa mapana, WHO inakadiria kuwa chanjo ya Malaria inaweza kuokoa maisha ya makumi kwa maelfu ya watoto kila mwaka.
translations.hypothesis: Shirika la Afya Duniani (WHO) linaomba kuongezwa utekelezaji wa zana zilizopendekezwa ili kupambana na ugonjwa wa malaria. Katika maadhimisho ya Siku ya Kimataifa ya Malaria, Shirika la Afya Duniani (WHO) linatoa wito wa kuongezwa utekelezaji wa hatua mpya na za zamani ili kuokoa maisha kutoka kwa ugonjwa wa malaria. Watoto takriban 1.5 milioni ambao wako katika hatari kubwa ya kuugua na kufa kutokana na malaria nchini Ghana, Kenya na Malawi, sasa wamepokea dozi yao ya kwanza ya chanjo ya kwanza ya malaria, RTS,S/AS01 (RTS,S), shukrani kwa programu ya majaribio inayoongezwa na WHO. Majaribio ya chanjo ya malaria, ambayo yalianzishwa mwaka wa 2019, yanaongeza usawa katika upatikanaji wa tiba za kuzuia malaria kwa watu walio katika hatari kubwa, na yanaokoa maisha. Ikiwa itatekelezwa kwa ukubwa, WHO inakadiria kwamba chanjo za malaria zinaweza kuokoa maisha ya maelfu ya watoto kila mwaka.

translations.source: “We have the tools to drive down malaria, a package of interventions that includes vector control, preventive medicines, testing, and treatment,” said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Director-General of WHO. “These are joined by a safe and effective malaria vaccine, which could save the lives of tens of thousands children every year. With sustained investment and scaled-up efforts to reach those most at risk, malaria elimination in many countries is in reach. "World Malaria Day 2023 is being marked under the theme “Time to deliver zero malaria: invest, innovate, implement”. Within this theme, WHO is urging more effective implementation of available tools and strategies to prevent, diagnose and treat malaria, particularly among marginalized populations.
translations.reference: "Tunazo zana za kukabiliana na ugonjwa wa Malaria, kifurushi cha afua ambacho kinajumuisha udhibiti wa Vekta, Dawa za kuzuia, upimaji na matibabu", alisema Dk Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, mkurugenzi mkuu wa WHO. "Hawa wameunganishwa na chanjo salama na yenye ufanisi ya Malaria, ambayo, inaweza kuokoa maisha ya makumi ya maelfu ya watoto kila mwaka. Kwa uwekezaji endelevu na juhudi zilizoongezwa kufikia walio hatarini zaidi, kutokomeza Malaria katika nchi nyingi kunawezekana. "Siku ya Malaria Duniani 2023 inaadhimisha chini ya kauli mbiu "wakati wa kutokomeza Malaria: Wekeza, Vumbua, tekeleza". Ndani ya Mada hii, WHO inahimiza utekelezaji bora zaidi wa zana na mikakati iliyopo ya kuzuia, kuchunguza na kutibu Malaria, hasa miongoni mwa watu waliotengwa.
translations.hypothesis:
“Tunayo vifaa ambavyo tunaweza kutumia kupunguza magonjwa ya malaria, ambayo ni pamoja na hatua mbalimbali kama vile udhibiti wa mbu, dawa za kuzuia, uchunguzi, na matibabu,” alisema Dkt. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Mkurugenzi Mkuu wa Shirika la Afya Duniani (WHO). “Hizi zinaunganishwa na chanjo salama na yenye ufanisi dhidi ya malaria, ambayo inaweza kuokoa maisha ya watoto maelfu kila mwaka. Kwa uwekezaji endelevu na juhudi kubwa za kufikia wale walio katika hatari kubwa, kuondoa malaria katika nchi nyingi ni jambo linalowezekana.”

Siku ya Dunia ya Malaria ya mwaka 2023 inaadhimishwa kwa kaulimbiu "Ni wakati wa kutekeleza ahadi ya kuondoa malaria: wekeza, ubunifu, na utekelezaji." Katika kaulimbiu hii, WHO inawahimiza watu kutekeleza kwa ufanisi zaidi vifaa na mikakati iliyopo ili kuzuia, kutambua, na kutibu malaria, hasa miongoni mwa makundi yaliyotengwa.

translations.source: According to the latest World malaria report, published in December 2022, there were an estimated 247 million new cases of malaria in 2021. The WHO African Region continues to shoulder the heaviest burden of the disease – accounting for an estimated 95% of all malaria cases (234 million) and 96% of all deaths (593 000) in 2021. Nearly 80% of malaria deaths in the African Region were among children under the age of five. New strategies and tools Countries have made some progress in expanding access to malaria services for most-at-risk populations.
translations.reference: Kulingana na ripoti ya hivi karibuni ya ugonjwa wa Malaria Duniani, iliyochapishwa mnamo Desemba 2022, kulikuwa na visa vipya milioni 247 vya Malaria mwaka 2021. WHO kanda ya Afrika inaendelea kubeba mzigo mzito zaidi wa ugonjwa huo ikichukua wastani wa 95% ya visa vyote vya Malaria (milioni 234) na 96% ya vifo vote (593,000) mnamo 2021. Karibu 80% ya vifo vya Malaria katika kanda ya Afrika vilikuwa miongoni mwa watoto chini ya umri wa miaka mitano. Mikakati na zana mpya Nchi zimepiga hatua katika kupanua upatikanaji wa huduma za Malaria kwa watu walio katika hatari zaidi.
translations.hypothesis: Kulingana na ripoti ya hivi karibuni ya Kimataifa kuhusu ugonjwa wa malaria, iliyochapishwa mwezi Desemba wa mwaka wa 2022, takriban visa milioni 247 vipya vya ugonjwa wa malaria vilitokea mwaka wa 2021. Eneo la Afrika la Shirika la Afya Duniani (WHO) bado linabeba mzigo mkubwa zaidi wa ugonjwa huo – likiwa na takriban asilimia 95 ya visa vyote vya malaria (milioni 234) na asilimia 96 ya vifo vyote (593,000) mwaka wa 2021. Takriban asilimia 80 ya vifo vya malaria katika eneo la Afrika vilikuwa kati ya watoto walio chini ya umri wa miaka mitano. Nchi zimefanya maendeleo fulani katika kupanua upatikanaji wa huduma za ugonjwa wa malaria kwa makundi yenye hatari kubwa.

translations.source: Despite some progress, many people at high risk of malaria still lack access to services that can prevent, detect and treat the disease. Challenges in expanding access to malaria services have been compounded, particularly in sub-Saharan Africa, by the COVID-19 pandemic, converging humanitarian crises, restricted funding, weak surveillance systems, and declines in the effectiveness of core malaria-fighting tools. To address these threats and support countries in building more resilient malaria programmes, WHO recently published new strategies and frameworks, including: a new strategy to contain antimalarial drug resistance in Africa; a new initiative to stop the spread of Anopheles stephensi in urban environments;
translations.reference: Licha ya mafanikio fulani, watu wengi walio katika hatari kubwa ya Malaria bado wanakosa huduma zinazoweza kuzuia, kugundua na kutibu ugonjwa huo. Changamoto katika kupanua upatikanaji wa huduma za Malaria zimeongezeka, hususani huko Afrika kusini mwa jangwa la Sahara, janga la UVIKO-19, kuunganisha migogoro ya kibinadamu, vikwazo vya fedha, mifumo dhaifu ya ufuatiliaji, na kupungua kwa ufanisi wa zana kuu za kupambana na malaria. Kushughulikia matishio haya na kusaidia nchi katika kujenga programu zinazostahimili ugonjwa wa Malaria, WHO hivi karibuni ilichapisha mikakati na mifumo mipya, ikijumuisha: Mkakati mpya wa kudhibiti ukinzani wa dawa za Malaria barani Afrika; Mpango mpya wa kukomesha kuenea kwa Anopheles stephensi katika mazingira ya mijini;
translations.hypothesis: Licha ya maendeleo fulani, watu wengi ambao wako katika hatari kubwa ya ugonjwa wa malaria bado hawapati huduma ambazo zinaweza kuzuia, kutambua na kutibu ugonjwa huo. Changamoto za kupanua upatikanaji wa huduma za malaria zimeongezeka, hasa katika Afrika ya Mashariki na Kusini, kutokana na janga la COVID-19, hali ya dharura ya kibinadamu ambayo imekuwa ikiongezeka, upungufu wa fedha, mifumo dhaifu ya ufuatiliaji, na kupungua kwa ufanisi wa zana muhimu za kupambana na malaria. Ili kukabiliana na hatari hizi na kusaidia nchi katika kuimarisha programu zao za kupambana na malaria, Shirika la Afya Duniani (WHO) hivi karibuni limechapisha mikakati na mfumo mpya, ikiwa ni pamoja na: mkakati mpya wa kudhibiti upinzani wa dawa za kuzuia malaria katika Afrika; na mpango mpya wa kusimamisha kuenea kwa mbu aina ya *Anopheles stephensi* katika mazingira ya mijini.

translations.source: a new framework, developed jointly by WHO and UN-Habitat, to guide city leaders in urban malaria control. a new toolkit to help countries assess their malaria surveillance systems and identify areas for investment WHO has also increased the transparency, flexibility and access to its malaria recommendations. The consolidated WHO Guidelines for malaria are now available through two digital platforms: MAGICapp and the “Malaria Toolkit” app. WHO encourages countries to tailor the recommendations to local disease settings for maximum impact.
translations.reference: Mfumo mpya, ulioandaliwa kwa pamoja na WHO na UN-Habitat, ili kuwaongoza viongozi wa majiji katika udhibiti wa Malaria mijini. Zana mpya za kusaisdia nchi kutathmini mifumo yao ya uchunguzi wa Malaria na kutambua maeneo ya uwekezaji Pia WHO imeongeza uwazi, kubadilika na kufikia mapendekezo yake ya Malaria. Miongozo iliyounganishwa na WHO kuhusu Malaria sasa inapatikana kupitia majukwaa mawili ya kidijitaali: MAGICapp na programu ya "Malaria Toolkit". WHO inazihimiza nchi kurekebisha mapendekezo kulingana na mipangilio ya magonjwa ya eneo hilo kwa athari kubwa zaidi.
translations.hypothesis: Mfumo mpya, ambao umeundwa kwa kushirikiana na Shirika la Afya Duniani (WHO) na UN-Habitat, umeanzishwa ili kuongoza viongozi wa miji katika juhudi za kudhibiti ugonjwa wa malaria katika maeneo ya mijini. Pia, zana mpya imetengenezwa ili kusaidia nchi kutathmini mifumo yao ya ufuatiliaji wa malaria na kutambua maeneo ambapo uwekezaji unaweza kufanyika. WHO pia imeongeza uwazi, uhuru, na upatikanaji wa mapendekezo yake kuhusu malaria. Miongozo iliyounganishwa ya WHO kuhusu malaria sasa inapatikana kupitia majukwaa mawili ya kidijitali: programu ya MAGICapp na programu ya "Malaria Toolkit." WHO inawahimiza nchi kuboresha mapendekezo hayo ili kuendana na hali ya ugonjwa katika kila eneo, ili kupata matokeo bora.

translations.source: Prospects for new interventions Continued investment in the development and deployment of new malaria vaccines and next-generation tools will be key to achieving the 2030 global malaria targets. A second malaria vaccine, the R21/Matrix-M (R21) vaccine, if approved, could help close the sizable gap between supply and demand and further reduce child illness and death from malaria. It is a priority for WHO to continue the thorough and efficient expert review of the R21 malaria vaccine once additional key safety and efficacy data from the ongoing R21 phase 3 trial are available and provided to WHO. Phased introductions of the RTS,S malaria vaccine in additional countries in Africa are expected to begin in early 2024.
translations.reference: Matarajio kwa afua mpya Kuendelea kwa uwekezaji katika maendeleo ya usambazaji wa chanjo mpya za Malaria na zana za kizazi kijacho itakuwa muhimu katika kufikia malengo ya kimataifa ya 2030 ya Malaria. Chanjo ya pili ya Malaria, chanjo ya R21/matrix-M (R21), kama itaidhinishwa, inaweza kusaidia kuziba pengo kubwa kati ya usambazaji na mahitaji na kupunguza zaidi ugonjwa wa watoto na kifo kutokana na Malaria. Ni kipaumbele kwa WHO kuendeleza na mapitio ya kina na madhubuti ya kitaalamu ya chanjo ya Malaria ya R21 mara Data muhimu za ziada za usalama na ufanisi kutoka kwa majaribio yanayoendelea ya awamu ya 3 ya R21 zitakazopatikana na kutolewa kwa WHO. Utangulizi wa hatua kwa hatua wa chanjo ya Malaria ya RTS,S katika nchi za ziada barani Afrika unatarajiwa kuanza mapema 2024.
translations.hypothesis:
Matarajio ya hatua mpya za kukabiliana na ugonjwa wa malaria:

Kuendeleza na kutumia chanjo mpya za malaria na teknolojia za kisasa itakuwa muhimu katika kufanikisha malengo ya kimataifa ya kupunguza ugonjwa wa malaria ifikapo mwaka wa 2030. Chanjo nyingine ya malaria, inayojulikana kama R21/Matrix-M (R21), ikiwa itapitishwa, inaweza kusaidia kufunga pengo kubwa kati ya upatikanaji na mahitaji, na pia kupunguza zaidi magonjwa na vifo vya watoto kutokana na malaria. Ni jambo la muhimu kwa Shirika la Afya Duniani (WHO) kuendelea na ukaguzi wa kina na wa ufanisi wa chanjo ya malaria ya R21, baada ya data muhimu zaidi kuhusu usalama na ufanisi kutoka kwa jaribio la awamu ya tatu la R21 kupatikana na kuwasilishwa kwa WHO. Kuanzishwa kwa chanjo ya RTS,S ya malaria katika nchi za ziada barani Afrika inatarajiwa kuanza mapema mwaka wa 2024.

translations.source: In the vector control space, there are 28 new products in the R&D pipeline. Tools under evaluation include, for example, new types of insecticide-treated nets, targeted baits that attract mosquitoes, spatial repellents, lethal house lures (eaves tubes) and genetic engineering of mosquitoes. Researchers are prioritizing the development of non-ACT treatments in the field of antimalarial medicines due to the emergence and spread of partial artemisinin resistance. The development of next-generation medicines, such as "triple ACTs," which use a combination of artemisinin and two partner drugs, is underway to reduce the risk of drug resistance. Notes to the editor:For more information on the WHO World Malaria Day campaign, visit: https://www.who.int/campaigns/world-malaria-day/2023
translations.reference: Katika nafasi ya kudhibiti Vekta, kuna bidhaa 28 mpya katika bomba la R&D. Zana zinazofanyiwa tathmini hujumuisha, kwa mfano, aina mpya za vyandarua vilivyotiwa dawa, chambo lengwa ambacho huvutia mbu, dawa za kuua mbu, viambata vya kuu vya nyumbani (mirija ya michirizi) na uhandisi jeni wa mbu. Watafiti wanatanguliza uendelezaji wa matibabu yasiyo ya ACT katika uwanja wa Dawa za Malaria kutokana na kuibuka na kuenea kwa ukinzani kwa sehemu ya artemisinin. Uundaji wa Dawa za kizazi kijacho, kama vile "ACT tatu", ambazo hutumia mchanganyiko wa Artemisinin na dawa mbili za washirika, unaendelea ili kupunguza hatari ya ukinzani wa Dawa. Maelezo kwa mhariri: Kwa taarifa zaidi kuhusu kampeni ya siku ya Malaria Duniani ya WHO, tembelea: https://www.who.int/campaigns/world-malaria-day-/2023.
translations.hypothesis:
Katika eneo la udhibiti wa wadudu, kuna bidhaa 28 mpya ambazo ziko katika hatua za utafiti na maendeleo. Vifaa ambavyo vinafanyiwa tathmini, kwa mfano, ni aina mpya za vitambaa vilivyotibiwa na dawa za kuua wadudu, vitendea vinavyolenga kuvutia mbu, dawa za kuenea katika nafasi ambazo huwafukuza wadudu, vifaa vya kuvutia mbu ndani ya nyumba (vifaa vinavyowekwa kwenye sehemu za juu za nyumba) na uhandisi wa jenetiki wa mbu. Watafiti wanakipa kipaumbele maendeleo ya matibabu ambayo hayajumuishi dawa za ACT (Artemisinin-based Combination Therapies) katika uwanja wa dawa za kuzuia malaria, kutokana na kuongezeka na kuenea kwa ulinzi wa sehemu dhidi ya artemisinin. Maendeleo ya dawa za kizazi cha pili, kama vile "ACTs tatu," ambazo hutumia mchanganyiko wa artemisinin na dawa mbili nyingine, yanaendelea ili kupunguza hatari ya ulinzi dhidi ya dawa.

Kwa wahariri: Kwa maelezo zaidi kuhusu kampeni ya Siku ya Kimataifa ya Malaria ya WHO, tembelea: https://www.who.int/campaigns/world-malaria-day/2023

translations.source: mRNA Technology Transfer Programme moves to the next phase of its development Inauguration of the mRNA technology Hub facility at Afrigen, South Africa. Partners gather in Cape Town to discuss the next phase of the mRNA vaccine development and knowledge sharing. Over 200 international participants working with the mRNA Technology Transfer Programme met in Cape Town this week for their first face-to-face meeting. Joined by World Health Organization (WHO) Director General, Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus and Dr. Joe Phaahla, Minister of Health and Minister of Trade and Industry, Mr. Ebrahim Patel of South Africa together with high-level officials from funding countries, this unique meeting reviewed the progress since WHO and Medicines Patent Pool (MPP) launched it in June 2021.
translations.reference: Mpango wa uhawilishaji wa teknolojia ya mRNA unasonga hadi hatua ijayo maendeleo yake Uzinduzi wa kituo cha teknolojia ya mRNA Hub huko Afrige, Afrika kusini. Washirika wanakusanyika Cape Town kujadili awamu inayofuata ya ukuzaji wa chanjo ya mRNA na kubadilishana maarifa. Zaidi ya washirika 200 wa kimaifa wanaofanya kazi na mpango wa uhawilishaji wa mRNA walikutana jijini Cape Town wiki hii kwa mkutano wao wa kwanza wa ana kwa ana. Akijumuika na mkurugenzi mkuu wa shirika la afya Duniani (WHO), Dk.Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus na Dk. Joel Phaahla, waziri wa afya na waziri wa biashara na viwanda, Bw. Ebrahim Patel wa Afrika Kusini pamoja na maafisa wa ngazi za juu kutoka nchi zinazofadhili mkutano wa kipekee ulikagua maendeleo tangu WHO na Medicines Patent Pool (MPP) ilipozinduliwa mwezi Juni 2021.
translations.hypothesis: Programu ya Uhamishaji Teknolojia ya mRNA inaingia katika awamu yake inayofuata ya maendeleo. Ufunguzi wa kituo cha teknolojia ya mRNA katika kampuni ya Afrigen, nchini Afrika Kusini. Washirika walikusanyika mjini Cape Town ili kujadili awamu inayofuata ya utengenezaji wa chanjo za mRNA na ushirikishwaji wa maarifa. Zaidi ya washiriki 200 kutoka duniani kote, wanaofanya kazi na Programu ya Uhamishaji Teknolojia ya mRNA, walikutana mjini Cape Town wiki hii kwa mkutano wao wa kwanza wa uso kwa uso. Pamoja na Mkurugenzi Mkuu wa Shirika la Afya Duniani (WHO), Dkt. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, na Dkt. Joe Phaahla, Waziri wa Afya, na Waziri wa Biashara na Viwanda, Bw. Ebrahim Patel wa Afrika Kusini, pamoja na maafisa wakuu kutoka nchi zinazotoa ufadhili, mkutano huu wa kipekee uliangazia maendeleo yaliyopatikana tangu WHO na Shirika la Leseni za Madawa (MPP) lilianzisha programu hiyo mwezi Juni mwaka wa 2021.

translations.source: “I am delighted to be here in Cape Town with our partners to support a sustainable model for mRNA technology transfer to give low- and middle-income countries equitable access to vaccines and other lifesaving health products,” said WHO Director-General Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus. “I am immensely proud of the achievement of all those involved in this project; in less than two years we have shown that when we work collaboratively, we succeed collectively.” The meeting participants include biomanufacturing partners from 15 countries in the Programme, leading experts, industry, civil society representatives, and funders. During the five-day meeting, participants will share progress and discuss critical enablers for the sustainability of the Programme such as intellectual property issues and regulatory aspects, as well as the science of mRNA technologies and key applications relevant to LMICs in other disease areas such as HIV and tuberculosis. The COVID-19 pandemic has underscored that gross inequity exists in access to health products, especially vaccines.
translations.reference: Nina furaha kuwa hapa Cape Town na washirika wetu kusaidia mtindo endelevu wa uhamishaji wa Teknolojia ya mRNA ili kuzipa nchi za kipato cha chini na cha kati fursa sawa ya kupata chanjo na bidhaaa nyingine za kuokoa maisha", alisema mkurugenzi mkuu wa WHO, Dk Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus. "Ninajivunia sana mafanikio ya wale wote waliohusika katika mradi huu; kwa chini ya miaka miwili tumeonyesha kuwa tunapofanya kazi kwa ushirikiano, tunafanikiwa kwa pamoja" Washiriki wa mkutano ni pamoja na washirika wa utengenezaji wa viumbe hai kutoka nchi 15 katika mpango, wataalamu wakuu, viwanda, wawakilishi wa mashirika ya kiraia, na wafadhili. Wakati wa mkutano wa siku tano, washiriki watashiriki maendeleo na kujadili viwezeshaji muhimu vya uendelevu wa mpango kama vile masuala ya mali miliki na vipengele vya udhibiti, vilevile sayansi ya teknolojia ya mRNA na matumizi muhimu yanayohusiana na LMICs katika maeneo mengine ya ugonjwa kama vile VVU na kifua kikuu. Janga la UVIKo-19 limesisitiza kuwa ukosefu wa usawa upo katika upatikanaji wa bidha za afya, hususani chanjo.
translations.hypothesis: "Ninafurahia kuwa hapa Cape Town pamoja na washirika wetu ili kusaidia mfumo endelevu wa uhamishaji wa teknolojia ya mRNA, ili kuwezesha nchi zilizo na kipato cha chini na cha kati kupata chanjo na bidhaa zingine muhimu za afya kwa usawa," alisema Mkurugenzi Mkuu wa WHO, Dkt. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus. "Ninajivunia sana mafanikio ya watu wote waliohusika katika mradi huu; katika kipindi cha chini ya miaka miwili, tumethibitisha kwamba tunapofanya kazi kwa pamoja, tunafanikiwa pamoja." Washiriki wa mkutano huo ni pamoja na washirika wa utengenezaji wa bidhaa za kibiolojia kutoka nchi 15 zinazoshiriki katika programu hiyo, wataalamu wakuu, sekta ya viwanda, wawakilishi wa jamii, na wafadhili. Katika mkutano huo ambao utadumu kwa siku tano, washiriki watashiriki maendeleo na kujadili mambo muhimu ya kuendeleza programu hiyo, kama vile masuala ya hakimiliki na masuala ya udhibiti, pamoja na sayansi ya teknolojia ya mRNA na matumizi muhimu yanayohusiana na nchi zilizo na kipato cha chini na cha kati katika maeneo mengine ya magonjwa, kama vile UKIMWI na kifua kikuu. Janga la COVID-19 limeonyesha kuwa kuna ukosefu mkubwa wa usawa katika upatikanaji wa bidhaa za afya, hasa chanjo.

translations.source: As of March 2023, more than three years after WHO declared COVID-19 a Public Health Emergency of International Concern (PHEIC), 69.7% of the global population had received at least one dose of a COVID-19 vaccine. Notably, this proportion still remains below 30% in low-income countries (LICs) . This Programme aims to contribute to equitable access to mRNA vaccines by increasing the distribution of sustainable manufacturing capacity across LMICs, enhancing regional and inter-regional collaborations, and developing and empowering a local workforce through tailored and inclusive training and expert support. What is unique in the mRNA Technology Transfer model is the multilateral process that allows sharing of technologies to multiple recipients so that through local and regional production those in need can be reached rapidly. Dr Phaahla, Minister of Health South Africa, said: “What we see here today, is a moment in history, a Programme that is aimed at empowering LMICs through a global collaborative network.
translations.reference: Kufikia Machi 2023, zaidi ya miaka mitatu baada ya WHO kutangaza UVIKO-19 kwa dharura ya afya ya umma ya wasiwasi wa kimataifa (PHEIC), 69.7% ya watu Duniani walikuwa wamepokea angalau Dozi moja ya chanjo ya UVIKo-19. Hasa, sehemu hii bado inasalia chini ya 30% katika nchi zenye kipato cha chini (LICs). Mpango huu unalenga kuchangia upatikanaji sawa wa chanjo za mRNA kwa kuongeza usambazaji wa uwezo endelevu wa utengenezaji katika LMICs, kuimarisha ushirikiano wa kikanda na baina ya kanda, na kuendeleza na kuwezesha wafanyakazi wa ndani kupitia mafunzo yaliyowekwa na kuja kujumuisha na usaidizi wa kitaalamu. Kipi cha kipekee katika kielelezo cha uhamisho wa Teknolojia ya mRNA ni mchakato wa kimataifa unaoruhusu ugavi wa Teknolojia kwa wapokeaji wengi ili kupitia uzalishaji wa ndani na kikanda wale wanaohiotaji waweze kufikiwa kwa haraka. Dk Phaahla, waziri wa afya wa Afrika Kusini, alisema : "Tunachokiona hapa leo, ni wakati katika historia, mpango ambao unalenga kuwezesha LMICs kupitia mtandao wa ushirikiano wa kimataifa.
translations.hypothesis: Mnamo Machi 2023, zaidi ya miaka mitatu baada ya Shirika la Afya Duniani (WHO) kutangaza COVID-19 kama janga la afya ya umma la kimataifa, asilimia 69.7 ya watu duniani walikuwa wamepokea angalau dozi moja ya chanjo ya COVID-19. Ni muhimu kuzingatia kwamba, idadi hii bado ni chini ya asilimia 30 katika nchi zilizo na kipato cha chini (LICs). Programu hii inalenga kuchangia upatikanaji sawa wa chanjo za mRNA kwa kuongeza usambazaji wa uwezo wa utengenezaji endelevu katika nchi zilizo na kipato cha chini na kati (LMICs), kuimarisha ushirikiano wa kikanda na wa kimkoa, na kuendeleza na kuwajumuisha wafanyakazi wa eneo hilo kupitia mafunzo maalum na usaidizi wa wataalamu. Kitu ambacho kinatofautisha mfumo wa uhamishaji wa teknolojia ya mRNA ni mchakato wa ushirikiano ambao huruhusu kushiriki teknolojia kwa wapokeaji wengi, ili kupitia utengenezaji wa ndani na wa kikanda, watu wanaohitaji waweze kufikiwa haraka. Dkt. Phaahla, Waziri wa Afya wa Afrika Kusini, alisema: "Kile tunachoona hapa leo ni wakati muhimu katika historia, programu ambayo inalenga kuwajumuisha nchi zilizo na kipato cha chini na kati kupitia mtandao wa ushirikiano wa kimataifa."

translations.source: I am thrilled to see the progress made in such a relatively short time and welcome the support from so many different countries - countries like South Africa that have a strong vibrant biomanufacturing capacity and that are willing to work together, learn from and share with each other.” Minister Blade Nzimande of Higher Education and Science, said: “Ours is the vision of the mRNA Technology Transfer Hub beyond just COVID-19. The capabilities we are building are looking to empower us to deal with other future pandemics whose vaccines could use the same mRNA technology platform. It is a vision that has set its eyes on diseases that are prevalent in our environments such as Malaria, Tuberculosis and HIV and AIDS.” This occasion also marks an important milestone for the Programme with the inauguration of the mRNA technology Hub facility at Afrigen in Cape Town in the presence of Dr. Tedros and Dr. Matshidiso Moeti WHO Regional Director for Africa, Charles Gore Executive Director of MPP and the Honorable Ministers who took part in a ribbon cutting ceremony at Afrigen.
translations.reference: Nimefurahi kuona maendeleo yaliyofikiwa kwa muda mfupi na nakaribisha msaada kutoka kwa nchi nyingi tofauti- nchi kama Afrika Kusini ambazo zina uwezo mkubwa wa kutengeneza viumbe na ambazo ziko tayari kufanya kazi pamoja, kujifunza kutoka na kushiriki na kila mmoja". Waziri Blade Nzimande wa Elimu ya juu na Sayansi, alisema: "Letu ni dira ya mRNA Technology Transfer Hub zaidi ya UVIKo-19. Uwezo tunaojenga unatazamia kutupa ili kukabiliana na magonjwa mengine baadae ambayo chanjo zake zinaweza kutumia jukwaa la Teknolojia la mRNA. Ni Dira ambayo imeweka macho yake kuhusu magonjwa ambayo yameenea katika mazingira yetu kama vile Malaria, kifua kikuu na VVU na UKIMWI" Hafla hii pia inaashiria hatua muhimu kwa mpango kwa kuzinduliwa kwa kituo cha teknolojia ya mRNA Hub huko Afrigen huko Cap Town mbele ya Dk. Tedros na Dk. Matshidiso Moeti Mkurugenzi wa WHO kanda ya Afrika, Charles Gore Mkurugenzi Mtendaji wa MPP na Waheshimiwa Mawaziri walishiriki katika hafla ya kukata utepe huko Afrigen.
translations.hypothesis: Ninafurahia sana kuona maendeleo yaliyopatikana katika kipindi kifupi tu, na ninathamini usaidizi kutoka kwa nchi nyingi tofauti - nchi kama Afrika Kusini ambazo zina uwezo mkubwa na unaendelea kukua katika utengenezaji wa bidhaa za kibayolojia, na ambazo ziko tayari kushirikiana, kujifunza kutoka kwa kila mmoja, na kushirikiana.” Waziri Blade Nzimande wa Elimu ya Juu na Sayansi alisema: “Lengo letu ni kuendeleza Kituo cha Uhamishaji Teknolojia ya mRNA, sio tu kwa ajili ya COVID-19. Uwezo tunaojenga unalenga kutuwezesha kukabiliana na magonjwa mengine ya baadaye ambayo yanaweza kutumia teknolojia hiyo hiyo ya mRNA. Lengo hili linazingatia magonjwa ambayo yanapatikana sana katika mazingira yetu, kama vile malaria, kifua kikuu, na UKIMWI.” Tukio hili pia linashuhudia hatua muhimu kwa ajili ya Programu, ikiwa ni pamoja na ufunguzi wa kituo cha teknolojia ya mRNA katika Afrigen, mjini Cape Town, katika uwepo wa Dkt. Tedros na Dkt. Matshidiso Moeti, Mkurugenzi Mkuu wa WHO kwa Afrika, Charles Gore, Mkurugenzi Mtendaji wa MPP, na Mawaziri husika ambao walishiriki katika sherehe ya ufunguzi katika Afrigen.

translations.source: Professor Petro Terblanche, Executive Director of Afrigen said: “The entire Afrigen team are thrilled to reach this important milestone with the completion of the mRNA technology platform. This platform is housed within the end-to-end mRNA vaccine development and production facility where the mRNA Hub Covid 19 vaccine candidate AfriVac 2121 is currently in scale-up phase. Over the last 18 months, Afrigen has undergone an incredible transformation with the support of a network of partners and mentors enabled by this Programme. We have grown our capability and capacity to meet the highest quality standards of mRNA vaccine development, serving the objective to build sustainable capacity in LMICs to produce mRNA vaccines”. Afrigen has successfully established a COVID-19 vaccine manufacturing process at a laboratory scale and is currently scaling up that process to a level suitable for manufacturing vaccine batches to be used in Phase I/ II clinical trials to GMP standards.
translations.reference: Profesa Petro Terblanche, mkurugenzi mtendaji wa Afrigen alisema: "Timu nzima ya Afrigen ina furaha kufikia hatua hii muhimu kwa kukamilika kwa jukwaa la Teknolojia ya mRNA. Jukwaa hili liko ndani ya kituo cha kutengeneza chanjo ya mwisho hadi mwisho ya mRNA ambapo mtahiniwa wa chanjo ya mRNA Hub Covid 19 AfriVac 2121 kwa sasa yuko katika hatua ya kuongeza. Katika kipindi cha miezi 18 iliyopita, Afrigen imepitia mabadiliko ya ajabu kwa usaidizi wa mtandao wa washirika na washauri wanaowezeshwa na mpango huu. Tumekuza uwezo wetu na uwezo wa kufikia viwango vya ubora wa juu zaidi vya ukuzaji wa chanjo ya mRNA, ikitumikia lengo la kujenga uwezo endelevu katika LMICs kuzalisha chanjo za mRNA". Afrigen imefanikiwa kuimarisha mchakato wa kutengeneza chanjo ya UVIKO-19 kwa kiwango cha maabara na kwa sasa inaongeza mchakato huo hadi kufikia kiwango kinachofaa kwa utengenezaji makundi ya chanjo zitakazo tumika katika majaribio ya matibabu ya awamu ya I/II.
translations.hypothesis: Profesa Petro Terblanche, Mkurugenzi Mtendaji wa Afrigen, alisema: "Timu nzima ya Afrigen imefurahia sana kufikia hatua hii muhimu kwa kukamilisha teknolojia ya mRNA. Jukwaa hili limeanzishwa ndani ya kituo cha uzalishaji na utengenezaji wa chanjo za mRNA, ambapo chanjo ya wahusika ya AfriVac 2121, inayolenga virusi vya COVID-19, kwa sasa iko katika awamu ya kuongeza uzalishaji. Katika kipindi cha miezi 18 iliyopita, Afrigen imepitia mabadiliko makubwa sana kwa msaada wa mtandao wa washirika na washauri, ambapo mradi huu umesaidia. Tumesongeza uwezo na ujanibari wetu ili kukidhi viwango vya juu zaidi vya ubora katika utengenezaji wa chanjo za mRNA, na hivyo kuwezesha lengo la kuunda uwezo endelevu katika nchi za kipato cha chini na kati (LMICs) za kuzalisha chanjo za mRNA." Afrigen imefanikiwa kuanzisha mchakato wa utengenezaji wa chanjo ya COVID-19 katika kiwango cha maabara, na kwa sasa inapanua mchakato huo ili kufikia kiwango kinachofaa kwa utengenezaji wa idadi kubwa ya chanjo ambazo zitatumika katika majaribio ya kliniki ya Awamu ya I/II, kulingana na viwango vya GMP.

translations.source: In a parallel process, Afrigen will continue to carry out training and technology transfer to the network partners. The funders play a crucial role in supporting the Programme with total funding to date at US$ 117 million and France being the first to fund the mRNA technology transfer work. Present at the meeting were representatives from the European Commission, Belgium, Germany, as well as Norway, Canada, African Union, South Africa, and the ELMA Foundation. Caroline Delany, GlobalAffairs Canada’s Director General for Southern and Eastern Africa said: “Canada reaffirms its continued support of the mRNA Technology Transfer Programme and we are delighted to announce a further contribution of 15 million Canadian dollars at the meeting today. This brings Canada’s total contribution to the Programme up to 45 million Canadian dollars.
translations.reference: Katika mchakato sambamba, Afrigen itaendelea kutekeleza mafunzo na uhamisho wa Teknolojia kwa washirika wa mtandao. Wafadhili wanajua jukumu muhimu katika kusaidia mpango wa ufadhili wa jumla hadi sasa wa Dola za kimarekani milioni 117 na Ufaransa ikiwa ya kwanza kufadhili kazi ya uhamishaji wa Teknolojia ya mRNA. Waliohudhuria mkutano huo ni wawakilishi kutoka Tume ya Ulaya, Ubelgiji, Ujerumani, vilevile Norway, Kanada, Umoja wa Afrika, Afrika Kusini, na wakfu wa ELMA. Caroline Delany, mkurugenzi mkuu wa Global Affairs Kanada kusini na mashariki mwa Afrika alisema: "Kanada inathibitisha kuendelea kuunga mkono mpango wa uhawilishaji Teknolojia wa mRNA na tuna furaha kutangaza mchango zaidi wa Dola milioni 15 za Kanada katika mkutano wa leo. Hii inaleta jumla ya mchango wa Kanada kwenye mpango hadi Dola milioni 45 za kikanada.
translations.hypothesis: Katika mchakato sambamba, Afrigen itaendelea kuendesha mafunzo na uhamisho wa teknolojia kwa washirika wa mtandao. Wadhamini wana jukumu muhimu katika kusaidia programu hii, na jumla ya fedha zilizotolewa hadi sasa ni dola za Kimarekani milioni 117, huku Ufaransa ikiwa nchi ya kwanza kutoa ufadhili kwa kazi ya uhamisho wa teknolojia ya mRNA. Mkutano huo ulihudhuriwa na wawakilishi kutoka Tume ya Ulaya, Ubelgiji, Ujerumani, pamoja na Norway, Kanada, Umoja wa Afrika, Afrika Kusini, na Shirika la ELMA. Caroline Delany, Mkurugenzi Mkuu wa Global Affairs Canada kwa Afrika Kusini na Mashariki, alisema: "Kanada inaangazia tena msaada wake endelevu kwa Programu ya Uhamisho wa Teknolojia ya mRNA, na tunafurahi kutangaza mchango wa ziada wa dola za Kanada milioni 15 katika mkutano huu wa leo. Hii huleta jumla ya mchango wa Kanada kwa programu hiyo hadi dola za Kanada milioni 45."

translations.source: These funds support the running of the Hub in South Africa as well as network partners around the world. We firmly believe in the importance of building capacity at local and regional level”. Mr Martin Seychell, Deputy Executive Director European Commission, said. “The EC has already contributed €40 million to the establishment of the mRNA technology transfer Hub and has recently signed another grant with the EIB for €15,5 million to facilitate the expansion of vaccines manufacturing capacity. This is part of the overall investment under the EU Global Gateway strategy, where more than €1 Billion have been mobilised under the Team Europe Initiative on Manufacturing health products. It is now particularly important to also address possible regulatory and demand bottlenecks, to ensure not only production of vaccines of the highest standard in all regions of the world, but also rapid and equitable access to those vaccines.”
translations.reference: Fedha hizi zinasaidia uendeshaji wa Hub nchini Afrika Kusini vilevile washirika wa mtandao Duniani kote. Tunaamini kwa dhati umuhimu wa kujenga uwezo katika ngazi ya nchi na kikanda", alisema Bw Martin Seychell, Naibu mkurugenzi mtendaji tume ya ulaya. "EC tayari imechangia yuro milioni 40 katika uanzishwaji wa kituo cha uhawilishaji wa Teknolojia ya mRNA na hivi karibuni imetia saini ruzuku nyingine na EIB kwa yuro milioni 15.5 kuwezesha upanuzi wa uwezo wa kutengeneza chanjo. Hii ni sehemu ya uwekezaji wa jumla ya mkakati wa EU Global Gatway, ambapo zaidi ya yuro Bilioni 1 zimekusanyw chini ya mpango wa timu ya Ulaya juu ya utengenezaji wa bidhaa za afya. Kwa sasa ni muhimu hasa kushughulikia vikwazo vinavyowezekana vya udhibiti na mahitaji, ili kuhakikisha sio tu uzalishaji wa chanjo za kiwango cha juu katika maeneo yote Duniani, lakini pia upatikanaji wa haraka na sawa wa chanjo hizo"
translations.hypothesis: Mifuko hiyo inasaidia kuendesha shughuli za kituo hicho nchini Afrika Kusini, pamoja na washirika wa mtandao ulimwenguni. Tunaamini sana umuhimu wa kuimarisha uwezo katika ngazi za ndani na za kikanda,” alisema Bwana Martin Seychell, Naibu Mkurugenzi Mtendaji wa Tume ya Ulaya. “Tume ya Ulaya tayari imechangia euro milioni 40 kwa ajili ya kuanzishwa kwa kituo cha uhamisho wa teknolojia ya mRNA, na hivi karibuni imesaini mkataba mwingine wa ufadhili na Benki ya Uropa ya Uwekezaji (EIB) wa euro milioni 15.5 ili kusaidia kuongeza uwezo wa utengenezaji wa chanjo. Hii ni sehemu ya uwekezaji wa jumla chini ya mkakati wa EU Global Gateway, ambapo zaidi ya euro bilioni 1 zimekusanywa kupitia Mpango wa Team Europe wa utengenezaji wa bidhaa za afya. Sasa ni muhimu sana pia kushughulikia vizuizi vinavyowezekana katika masuala ya udhibiti na mahitaji, ili kuhakikisha si tu utengenezaji wa chanjo za ubora wa hali ya juu katika maeneo yote ya ulimwengu, bali pia upataji wa haraka na sawa wa chanjo hizo.”

translations.source: Key leadership appointments made to drive WHO strategic direction and initiatives The World Health Organization (WHO) has appointed five new senior figures to its headquarters leadership team in Geneva. The new appointments follow the reappointment of Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus to his second five-year term as Director-General. The overall leadership team has been consolidated to align with the Organization’s priorities for the next five years and will work closely with the Director-General to drive forward these priorities and WHO’s ambitious transformation agenda. To accelerate progress on implementation of WHO’s 13th General Programme of Work (GPW13), and achievement of its “triple billion” targets and the health-related Sustainable Development Goals, WHO’s work for the coming five years is focused on five priorities:
translations.reference: Uteuzi muhimu wa uongozi ulifanywa ili kusimamia muelekeo na mipango ya kimkakati ya WHO Shirika la afya Duniani (WHO) limeteua vigogo watano wapya kwa tume yake ya uongozi ya makao makuu mjini Jeniva. Uteuzi huo mpya unafuatia kuteuliwa tena kwa Dk Tedros Adhanom Ghereyesus katika muhula wake wa pili wa miaka mitano kama mkurugenzi mkuu. Timu ya jumla ya uongozi imeunganishwa ili kuendana na vipaumbele vya shirika kwa miaka mitano ijayo na itafanya kazi kwa karibu na mkurugenzi mkuu ili kuendeleza vipaumbele hivi na ajenda ya mabadiliko ya WHO. Ili kuharakisha maendeleo katika utekelezaji wa mpango mkuu wa 13 wa kazi za WHO (GPW13), kufikia malengo yake ya "Bilioni tatu" na malengo ya maendeleo endelevu yanayousiana na afya, kazi ya WHO kwa miaka mitano ijayo inazingatia vipaumbele vitano:
translations.hypothesis: Shirika la Afya Duniani (WHO) limefanya uteuzi muhimu wa viongozi ili kuendeleza mwelekeo na mipango yake. WHO imemteua viongozi wanne wapya katika timu yake ya uongozi iliyopo makao makuu mjini Geneva. Miteuzi hii imefanyika baada ya Dkt. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus kuendelea na muhula wake wa pili wa miaka mitano kama Mkurugenzi Mkuu. Timu ya uongozi kwa ujumla imeboreshwa ili kuendana na malengo ya Shirika kwa miaka mitano ijayo, na itafanya kazi kwa karibu na Mkurugenzi Mkuu ili kuendeleza malengo hayo na mpango mkubwa wa mageuzi wa WHO. Ili kuongeza kasi ya utekelezaji wa Mpango Mkuu wa Tatu wa WHO (GPW13), na kufanikisha malengo yake ya "trilioni tatu" na Malengo ya Maendeleo Endelevu yanayohusiana na afya, kazi ya WHO kwa miaka mitano ijayo itazingatia malengo matano:

translations.source: making a paradigm shift towards promoting health and preventing disease by addressing its root causes; radically reorienting health systems towards primary health care; strengthening the systems for health emergency preparedness and response; harnessing the power of science, innovation, data and technologies to advance health; and strengthening WHO as the leading authority on global health and to achieve impact in countries.
translations.reference: Kufanya mabadiliko ya mtazamo kuelekea kukuza afya na kuzuia magonjwa kwa kushughulikia vyanzo vyake; Kuelekeza upya mifumo ya afya kuelekea huduma ya afya ya msingi Kuimarisha mifumo ya maandalizi na majibu ya dharura ya afya; Kutumia nguvu ya sayansi, uvumbuzi , Data na teknolojia ili kuendeleza afya; na Kuimarisha WHO kama mamlaka inayoongoza kwa afya Duniani na kufikia athari katika nchi.
translations.hypothesis: kufanya mabadiliko makubwa katika mtazamo, kwa lengo la kukuza afya na kuzuia magonjwa kwa kushughulikia sababu zake za msingi; kubadilisha kabisa mifumo ya afya ili kuziweka katika mstari wa huduma ya afya ya msingi; kuimarisha mifumo ya utayaraji na utendaji katika hali za dharura za kiafya; kutumia nguvu ya sayansi, ubunifu, data na teknolojia ili kuendeleza afya; na kuimarisha Shirika la Afya Duniani (WHO) kama taasisi inayoongoza katika masuala ya afya duniani, ili kufanikisha matokeo chanya katika nchi mbalimbali.

translations.source: The headquarters leadership team, comprising equal numbers of women and men, will work jointly with WHO regional and country offices. New members of the headquarters leadership team: Dr Jeremy Farrar will become WHO’s Chief Scientist as of 8 May 2023. The appointment of Dr Farrar was previously announced in December 2022. In this role Dr Farrar will oversee the Science Division, bringing together the best experts and networks in science and innovation from around the world to guide, develop and deliver high quality health policies and services to the people who need them most. Prior to joining WHO Dr Farrar was Director of the Wellcome Trust.
translations.reference: Timu ya uongozi ya makao makuu, inayojumuisha idadi sawa ya wanawake na wanaume, itafanya kazi kwa pamoja na ofisi za WHO za kikanda na nchi. Wanachama wapya wa timu ya uongozi ya makao makuu: Dk Jeremy Farrar atakuwa mwanasayansi mkuu wa WHO kuanzia tarehe 8 Mei 2023. Uteuzi wa Dk Farrar ulitangazwa hapo awali mwezi Desemba 2022. Katika jukumu hili Dk Farrar atasimamia kitengo cha sayansi, akiwaleta pamoja watalaamu wobobezi na mitandao katika sayansi na uvumbuzi kutoka Duniani kote ili kuongoza, kuendeleza na kutoa sera na huduma za afya za ubora wa juu kwa watu wanaohitaji zaidi. Kabla ya kujiunga na WHO Dk Farrar alikuwa mkurugenzi wa Wellcome Trust.
